Data Policy Home>Documentation>10 Best Practices To Consider When Designing QR Codes for Digital Out-of-Home Creative. Delivr 10 Best Practices To Consider When Designing QR Codes for Digital Out-of-Home Creative. 10 Best Practices To Consider When Designing QR Codes for Digital Out-of-Home Creative. 1. QR code size. QR codes at bus shelter and sidewalk / roadside displays are engaged with at different distances. Sizes always refer to an exact square shape because otherwise, your QR code will not function properly. Minimum size for QR codes at a distance of 3 meters 22 -30 cm excluding the margin Ratio of scanning distance to QR code size 10:1 Formula for calculating safe QR code size based on scanning distance QR code minimum size Scanning distance (in cm or inches) ÷ 10 2. Good contrast. Good contrast between the background color of the QR code and the dot pattern color of the QR code is very important when a standard Black & White code is not used. In cases of colorized codes, the QR code should have a dark color with a light background color. Also note that some QR code readers cannot read reversed colors e.g., light color with a dark background color, white with black background color. Good contrast also ensures your QR code is able to be seen by people with visual impairments. 3. Quality and format. Avoid pixelated or blurry QR codes and make sure to include them in your digital ad sharp and clear. Designers prefer vector files like SVG. The advantage of the SVG format is that they can be scaled easily to any size — without compromising on quality. 4. Number of QR codes. Printing multiple QR codes close to each other or even placing QR Codes next to barcodes can lead to accidentally scanning the wrong one. When possible, use only one QR code per digital ad. This creates focus on the action you want consumers to take. It also prevents phone cameras from jumping focus from code to code. 5. Margins. Regardless of application, the margins around a QR code should be equivalent to at least four data modules (the Delivr default). QR code modules are the dots that make up QR code. Keep in mind that the number of modules can differ among QR codes that have the same overall dimensions. 6. Quiet zone. The quiet zone is the space surrounding the QR code that separates the QR code from other designs. QR codes need a quiet zone so that scanners can detect what images are QR code pixels and which aren’t. Without the quiet zone, it’s likely that the QR code will not work. 7. Call-to-action (CTA). A QR code on its own is not a call-to-action. A simple directive such as "Open Camera & Scan Code" near the code can incentivize consumers to reach for their phones. It is recommended that you clearly inform the consumer what content they will receive if they scan your code. 8. Placement. Keep QR codes within reach. Placing your QR code too high up, too low on the ground, or too far away means your customers will have a hard time reaching to scan the code. Place QR codes within appropriate reach for maximum accessibility. Would you crouch down or use a ladder just to scan a QR code? Placing QR codes at eye-level is recommended. 9. Timing. Show the QR code for as long as possible. Consider the time it takes for a person to walk on the sidewalk or drive by in a car, see the content on digital signage, notice the QR code, decide they want to scan it, and successfully scan it. Many out of home ads appear for 10 seconds or less, so even if the creative is dynamic, designers should present the QR code for the full length of the slot to maximize the opportunity for successful conversion. 10. Check your content. Imagine your customers’ dismay when they take the time to scan your QR code just to find a broken link or outdated information. This is a mishap we’ve frequently seen that can be easily avoided. To keep from being stuck with a QR code that you can’t ever update, make sure to create a Dynamic QR Code. With this type of code, you can change the destination URL. Verify your QR code will scan at the size and distance it will be displayed before using it. 1.title: A Practical Guide to Spotting Fake QR Codes and Staying Safe.
1.content: A Practical Guide to Spotting Fake QR Codes and Staying Safe. URL: https://delivr.com/faq/1618/a-practical-guide-to-spotting-fake-qr-codes-and-staying-safe Title: A Practical Guide to Spotting Fake QR Codes and Staying Safe. URL Source: http://delivr.com/faq/1618/a-practical-guide-to-spotting-fake-qr-codes-and-staying-safe Markdown Content: What makes a QR code “Fake”? ---------------------------- A fake QR code works perfectly — but not in your favor. It mimics a legitimate code while secretly directing you to phishing sites, fake login pages, or fraudulent payment portals. Sometimes the scam is digital (such as a malicious link in an email), but it can also be physical — a sticker covering a genuine QR code. The goal is the same: trick you into giving up personal data, credentials, or money. Since QR codes don’t show their destination until scanned, scammers rely on your trust and speed. Common QR code Scams. --------------------- 1. Quishing (QR Phishing) ------------------------- Attackers embed malicious QR codes in emails or public places. Scanning leads to a fake site that appears real but is designed to steal sensitive information, such as banking details. 2. QRLjacking (Credential Theft) -------------------------------- QR-based login is convenient — but it can be dangerous if abused. Scammers send fake QR login prompts that capture your session or credentials once scanned. 3. Fake Payment Requests ------------------------ Scammers replace legitimate payment codes in public places. Instead of paying a business, you’re unknowingly transferring money to a fraudster. 4. Malware Distribution ----------------------- Some QR codes initiate downloads disguised as apps, coupons, or documents. In reality, they install malware that spies, logs keystrokes, or hijacks your device. 5. Risky Auto-Connections to Rogue Wi-Fi ---------------------------------------- Specific QR codes can automatically connect your device to a compromised Wi-Fi network, allowing attackers to intercept data or deliver malware. 6. QR Code Scams Are Getting Smarter ------------------------------------ While many scams still use basic tricks like fake payment or login pages, some are becoming more advanced. A new method called Scanception shows how clever these scams can be. In one case, attackers sent emails with PDF files that looked normal. But inside was a QR code leading to a fake Microsoft 365 login page. If someone scanned it and logged in, their information was stolen — even if they had Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) turned on. How they did it. ---------------- Used a technique called Adversary-in-the-Middle (AiTM) to steal login tokens and bypass MFA. Sent stolen data over encrypted channels, making it harder to spot. Hid dangerous links behind trusted services like Google or Bing to avoid suspicion. Why it matters. --------------- Even tech-savvy users who follow best practices can still be tricked. It’s not just about checking the link — it’s about trusting the source. If a QR code comes from an unexpected email or file, don’t scan it without thinking twice. How to Spot Malicious QR code. ------------------------------ 1. Preview the Link ------------------- After scanning, check the URL: Does it use “https://”? DIs the domain familiar? DAny odd spellings or characters? If the URL looks suspicious or shortened, don’t click. 2. Avoid Scanning Without Context --------------------------------- Legitimate QR codes typically include a label (e.g., “Scan to view menu”). If there’s no explanation, it could be fake. 3. Be Wary in Public Spaces --------------------------- Scammers may place stickers over real codes. Watch for signs of tampering, such as bubbling, misalignment, or mismatched materials. 4. Watch for Urgency or Emotional Triggers ------------------------------------------ Messages like “Scan now to avoid charges” are red flags. Scammers want you to act fast — pause and think before you scan. 5. Trust Your Intuition. ------------------------ If something feels off — don’t scan. When in doubt, visit the website 10 tips to stay safe when scanning QR codes. -------------------------------------------- 1. Stick to Trusted Sources --------------------------- Avoid scanning QR codes that appear suspicious, out of place, or damaged. 2. Inspect First ---------------- If a code appears to be tampered with, skip it. Seek an alternative approach to complete your task. 3. Use Your Phone’s Built-in Camera ----------------------------------- Avoid third-party scanner apps unless they come from reputable developers. 4. Choose Apps with Security Features ------------------------------------- Use apps that show link previews and scan URLs for safety. 5. Always Preview the URL ------------------------- Check for “https://” and verify the domain before clicking. 6. Keep Your Device Updated --------------------------- Install the latest OS and browser updates to guard against known vulnerabilities. 7. Enable Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) ----------------------------------------- Add an extra layer of protection to essential accounts — even if your password gets stolen. 8. Don’t Click Suspicious QR Links ---------------------------------- Be cautious with QR codes sent via email or text, especially from unknown sources. 9. Avoid Unfamiliar Wi-Fi Networks ---------------------------------- Turn off auto-connect. Use a VPN for added security when on public networks. 10. Stay Informed and Share What You Know ----------------------------------------- Educate others about the risks associated with QR codes to help prevent scams. QR codes make life easier, let’s keep it that way. -------------------------------------------------- QR codes aren’t going anywhere — and that’s a good thing. Yes, scams exist, but with a little caution, you can use them safely and confidently. Take a moment before you scan, trust your instincts, and watch for anything that feels off. For businesses, it’s just as important to keep QR codes clear, secure, and easy to trust. When everyone plays their part, QR codes can keep doing what they’re meant to do: making everyday moments quicker, easier, and more connected.

Data Policy Home>Legal>Accessibility Statement Accessibility Statement Delivr believes that martech software and services companies have a powerful role to play in ensuring technology helps bridge the divide for people with disabilities. To support this belief, we strive to make delivr.com as accessible as possible to people of all levels of ability. ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- General ------- Delivr strives to ensure that its services are accessible to people with disabilities. Delivr has invested a significant amount of resources to help ensure that its website is made easier to use and more accessible for people with disabilities, with the strong belief that every person has the right to live with dignity, equality, comfort and independence. Accessibility on Delivr ----------------------- As our guide across the site we use the World Wide Web Consortium’s Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1. Our goal is for delivr.com to meet Level AA standards, WCAG’s recommended level of conformance for entire sites. Disclaimer ---------- Delivr continues its efforts to constantly improve the accessibility of its site and services in the belief that it is our collective moral obligation to allow seamless, accessible and unhindered use also for those of us with disabilities. In an ongoing effort to continually improve and remediate accessibility issues, we regularly measure our site using a variety of methods, such as browser plug-ins, external vendor assessments, and individual page testing by our own team. This continuous approach helps ensure that accessibility will be maintained as guidelines change and technology evolves. We view our overall site and platform as meeting Level AA conformance, while recognizing that some pages may have occasional accessibility errors. Further, in spite of our efforts to make all pages and content on Delivr fully accessible, some content may not have yet been fully adapted to the strictest accessibility standards. This may be a result of not having found or identified the most appropriate technological solution. Here For You ------------ If you are experiencing difficulty with any content on Delivr's website, any of its services or require assistance with any part of our site or software, please contact us during normal business hours as detailed below and we will be happy to assist. Contacting Us ------------- If you wish to report a problem, have any questions or need assistance, or have suggestions on how we can further improve the accessibility of our site, please click here to submit a support request. Data Policy ACUITY℠ INTELLIGENT LINK MANAGEMENT The smart link layer that thinks. Ruie-driven routing. Smarter engagement. Better experiences. The smart link layer that thinks. Ruie-driven routing. Smarter engagement. Better experiences. ------------------------------------------------------------ Home>Engagement Suite>Intelligent Link Management Acuity. The next generation of intelligent link management. Acuity℠ isn’t just a link shortener — it’s a performance-driven link intelligence platform built for marketers who care about precision, privacy, and meaningful results. Every managed Short link is powered by advanced routing, real-time behavioral insights, and a zero-knowledge privacy architecture — all delivered through a beautifully simple, marketer-friendly interface. Acuity℠ helps you create smarter, more trustworthy connections at every touchpoint. Strengthen credibility and brand recognition with fully branded short links and QR codes, using custom domains, visual styles, and on-brand QR code designs so every scan and click feels consistent, authentic, and unmistakably yours. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Intelligent Link Routing ------------------------ Deliver the right experience to every user automatically. Links can route based on device location, language or operating system — sending customers to the most relevant page, app store, or localized content with no extra steps on the part of your audience. Image 4: Intelligent Link Routing Diagram Dynamic Link Updates -------------------- Update link destinations at any time — even after your campaign is live. Keep existing QR codes and short links in circulation while seamlessly redirecting users to new pages, promotions, or experiences without reprinting or replacing assets. Deep, Private Analytics ----------------------- Gain powerful insights into performance — such as scans, engagement patterns, and campaign outcomes — while protecting user privacy. Our analytics are designed to be privacy-first, providing meaningful trend data without identifying individuals or collecting personal information. Branded Links ------------- Strengthen trust and recognition with fully branded short links and QR codes. Use custom domains, visual styles, and brand elements so every scan and click feels consistent, authentic, and unmistakably yours. URL Slugs / Custom Back-Halfs ----------------------------- A URL slug — also called a custom back-half — is the part of a shortened or branded link that appears after the final slash. It helps make links more meaningful, memorable, and easier to recognize. For example: qr.yourdomain.com/productname . In this link, “productname” is the URL slug. Rather than using a random string of characters, a custom slug lets you define this portion of the link so it reflects the content or destination — such as a product name, campaign, location, or promotion. Well-designed slugs can: Make links easier to read and share Improve user trust and click-through rates Help with organization and reporting Support SEO for branded short domains (where applicable) To get the best results, choose slugs that are short, descriptive, and consistent in style (for example: lowercase, hyphen-separated words). Campaign Controls ----------------- Put your links on your terms. Acuity℠ lets you manage what happens before, during, and after a campaign — all without coding or complex workflows. Expire links on a set date, replace outdated destinations with updated content, display custom messages, or automatically redirect users to fallback pages — ensuring no one ever lands on a broken or irrelevant experience. With Campaign Controls, every link stays intentional, on-brand, and aligned with your strategy — because your links deserve the same care and craftsmanship as your brand. Built for Marketers ------------------- Delivr gives marketing teams full control without needing IT. Add Google® Analytics UTMs automatically. UTMs integrate with Adobe Analytics®, Hubspot®, Marketo®, Salesforce®, Mixpanel®, Amplitude®, and Segment®. Edit or expire links. Maintain 100% GDPR and CCPA compliance. Enjoy real-time reporting that’s fast, clear, and ethical. Learning Center --------------- A curated collection of solutions, resources, and FAQs to help you evaluate with confidence. How to add Geotargeting URLs to your campaign. How to add Language URLs to your campaign. How to add Application URLs to your Campaign. How to build and use campaign parameters (UTMs). 4.title: Sell at your storefront, whether you're open or closed.
4.content: Sell at your storefront, whether you're open or closed. URL: https://delivr.com/page/753/always-open-by-delivr Title: Always Open by Delivr. URL Source: http://delivr.com/page/753/always-open-by-delivr Markdown Content: Always Open® is a service that helps businesses sell their products and services to consumers with signage that bridges the divide between storefront windows and online shopping. ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Smartphones changed how we moved through stores. Global health crises are changing how we move through the world. Big box retailers became showrooms for online commerce. Pop-up stores appeared wherever customers gathered. Amazon began “eating the retail world,” accelerating the decline of traditional brick-and-mortar. Digital and physical experiences blurred, and consumers stopped distinguishing between online and offline shopping. Suddenly, we’re living in a different world. -------------------------------------------- Widespread pandemics and the threat of infectious pathogens have made digital technology even more integral to daily life. Shoppers increasingly choose mobile ordering, home delivery, curbside, and contact-minimal pickup to protect their health and reduce exposure. These shifts have left a lasting impact on society, pushing brands and retailers to prioritize safer, smarter, and more resilient shopping experiences. Top 5 ways retail has changed. ➀ Shoppers know as much as salespeople. ------------------------------------- People used to come into stores with little to no knowledge and relied on a salesperson to advise them on what to buy. Today’s shoppers are doing their online research to find and learn more about the products they are considering and to feel secure about their purchases. Facilitate their education with smart packaging and smart POS displays with QR codes. ➁ Relevant suggestions are increasingly coming from a real-time context-aware mobile interaction. ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Retailing began with shopkeepers who would welcome in people from the neighborhood and then come to learn about their customers’ needs and preferences. In our constantly connected world, a device is just a proxy for what matters — getting to know your customers. Devices provide context, helping us learn what matters to consumers in a particular location and time. Layering AR and VR into the online shopping experience enables independent, self-serve shopping that approaches the results of bespoke sales and product sampling. ➂ Opinions carry more weight than ever. ------------------------------------- The force of the positive or negative word of mouth has always impacted retail. Social is more important than ever, with online reviews now among the first places a person checks to feel confident about an intended purchase. Real-time feedback and reviews give retailers a defining factor as social media recommendations influence online shoppers. Do you facilitate reviews and integrate these reviews into your shopping experience? ➃ Mobile devices drive foot traffic to stores. -------------------------------------------- Finding the right store — and the product you needed — used to depend on familiarity or serendipity. As the lines have blurred between online and offline, retailers are increasingly integrating mobile into their brick-and-mortar store experience. But what if people can't enter your store or you're closed?; We suggest having your display window and POS as a gateway to your online store. ➄ Retail's role in public health safety has changed rapidly during the Coronavirus emergency. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- While mobile devices drive people to stores, disease risk can drive them away. Restaurants are taking the lead in “sanitizing” their establishments. To help slow down the spread of coronavirus on surfaces, restaurants are replacing their paper menus with a QR code on their entrance doors. Some establishments even useQR codes to replace the ordering and payment system. Further, QR codes reduce the need to interact with sales associates or wait in checkout lines. Respond with the perfect mix of impulse, information, and convenience. QR codes can turn anything, anywhere, into a storefront. This applies to your storefront window, regardless of whether you're open or closed. In a competitive or challenging retail environment, you first need to give your customers a way to act on their interests and learn more immediately. And if they are interested enough to buy it, they should also be able to do that. QR codes can help you with both. Place a QR code in a newspaper or magazine ad allowing people to buy the product you advertised directly. Place one or more QR codes in posters, billboards, or storefront windows, creating a virtual store. The virtual store concept has been a resounding success for companies like Tesco, a British multinational grocery and general merchandise retailer. It would work like this: 1. Smartphone customers scan QR codes on posters next to pictures of products they want to purchase. The signs in the virtual stores can be designed to resemble a regular store's actual aisles and shelves, making the experience very user-friendly. 2. Once the products are scanned, the customer can add them to their shopping cart and pay online once their order is complete. 3. Customers schedule a time for home delivery. Drive people from your storefront window and outdoor signage to a mobile landing page or online store regardless if you're open or closed. How does Always Open work? Always Open is a service that helps businesses promote their products and services to consumers with signage that bridges the divide between posters, billboards, storefront windows, and online shopping. Always Open provides solutions ranging from "Do it yourself" (DIY) to a complete turnkey service. The Always Open Program includes a mix of services. Consult to discuss your business and evaluate your needs. While independent retailers, franchises, dealerships, and retail chains share common challenges, requirements, and budget impact scope. Twenty minutes is all we'll need. Dynamic QR code(s) redirecting to an existing link of yours, a mobile landing page we host, or an online store we develop with you. Do you need outdoor signage? If you're a DIY type, you can easily design and print them yourself. However, if you need some assistance, we're happy to work with you in any way we can to help you design and print your desired signage. Our printing partners can supply stickers, labels, and even packaging can be facilitated with our printing partners. Dashboard with campaign management & analytics. Ongoing maintenance and management is available self-service or as a managed service. Custom website development with Shopify integration is available. Ready to get started? Complete the form on this page to learn more. No risk. No obligation.

5.title: Anti-Bribery and Anti-Corruption Policy
5.content: Anti-Bribery and Anti-Corruption Policy URL: https://delivr.com/page/779/anti-bribery-and-anti-corruption-policy Title: Anti-Bribery and Anti-Corruption Policy URL Source: http://delivr.com/page/779/anti-bribery-and-anti-corruption-policy Markdown Content: Delivr prohibits any form of bribery or corruption, whether involving foreign governmen officials, public officials, employees of state-owned enterprises, private sector company or individuals, and whether direct or indirect through a third-party representative. ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 1. Policy --------- At Delivr, we are committed to the highest standards of ethics and integrity in all our activities. This Anti-Bribery and Anti-Corruption Policy (this “Policy”) sets forth Delivr’s commitment to ensure that Delivr, its affiliates, and others acting on Delivr’s behalf abide by all international and local Anti-Bribery and Anti-Corruption Laws (as defined below) in countries in which Delivr conducts business. Delivr prohibits any form of bribery or corruption, whether involving a Public Official (as defined below) or a private sector company or individual, and whether direct or indirect through a Third-Party Representative (as defined below). This means that Delivr prohibits giving, offering, promising or receiving Anything of Value (as defined below), directly or indirectly, with the intent to obtain an improper business advantage for Delivr. Delivr does not distinguish between Public Officials and employees of private sector organizations. However, it is important to recognize that Public Officials are often subject to additional rules and restrictions. For this reason, Delivr often requires additional scrutiny of interactions with Public Officials. 2. Scope -------- This Policy applies to all employees, officers, directors and contractors of Delivr and its affiliates (collectively “Delivr Personnel”). In addition, Delivr also expects Third Party Representatives to abide by this Policy. 3. Definitions -------------- “Anti-Bribery and Anti-Corruption Laws ” refers to international and local laws that collectively prohibit bribery and corruption. Such laws include the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and the U.K. Bribery Act, as well as other local laws. These laws commonly require an organization to have an adequate system of internal financial controls, and to keep accurate and detailed books and records. Violation of these laws may give rise to criminal offenses punishable by fines and imprisonment, and individual liability may extend to those planning, carrying out or condoning prohibited acts. “Anything of Value” includes cash, money, goods and services, including consulting agreements, speaker fees, research agreements, travel, hospitality, meals, favors, entertainment, donations, gifts, or anything that confers a personal benefit. “Public Official” refers to: (a) any elected or appointed official or employee of a government or government department, government agency, or of a company owned or partially owned by a government; (b) any elected or appointed officers or employees of public international organizations (e.g., United Nations, World Health Organization); (c) any person acting in an official capacity for or on behalf of a government or an government department, government agency, government-owned entity (including Health Care Professionals (“HCPs”) employed by government-run hospitals), or of a public international organization; (d) politicians and candidates for a political office; (e) any member of a royal family or member of the military; or (f) any other person who is considered to be a public official according to applicable laws, regulations and industry codes. “Third Party Representative” refers to those who are authorized to act for or on behalf of Delivr, and may include distributors, subcontractors, regulatory agents, advisors, consultants, clinical research organizations, market research firms, meeting planners, agents, custom brokers and contract manufacturing organizations. 4. Responsibilities and Consequences of Non-Compliance ------------------------------------------------------ All Delivr Personnel and Third-Party Representatives are expected to be aware of, and comply with this Policy and immediately report, actual or suspected violations to Delivr’s Legal Department in accordance with Section 5 below. Managers are expected to oversee their direct reports’ understanding and compliance with this Policy and Anti-Bribery and Anti-Corruption Laws. Violations of this Policy by any Delivr Personnel may result in disciplinary action up to termination of employment as well as the potential for prosecution, fines or imprisonment in accordance with applicable laws. Any Third-Party Representatives who violate this Policy may face termination of contracts and business relations with Delivr. Requirements for Delivr’s Business Activities and Interactions A. Prohibited Payments Delivr Personnel and Third-Party Representatives are prohibited from giving, offering, promising or receiving Anything of Value, directly or indirectly, with the intent to obtain an improper business advantage for Delivr. In some cases, even payments not intended to be improper can appear to be so and expose the individual and company to potential liability. As a result, Delivr Personnel and Third-Party Representatives must transparently consult with their Legal and Business Conduct colleagues as required by this Policy. Below is a non-exhaustive list of activities and interactions where particular care needs to be taken to comply with this Policy. i. Consulting Arrangements Consulting arrangements with third parties present a heightened risk if a third party is engaged – or may reasonably perceived to be engaged – for the purpose of acquiring an improper business advantage. Accordingly, consulting arrangements, including arrangements with Public Officials (e.g., speaker engagements, advisory engagements), may only be entered into if there is a bona fide and legitimate business need for the services, compensation does not exceed fair market value, the arrangement is permitted by applicable laws and the arrangement complies with this Policy. ii. Gifts, Hospitality and Entertainment Gifts, hospitality and entertainment may only be provided if they are of a reasonable value, made for a legitimate business purpose, are not prohibited by applicable laws and comply with this Policy. Some countries may prohibit or strictly limit the value of gifts, hospitality or entertainment that may be provided to Public Officials, HCPs and/or patient organizations. No gifts, hospitality or entertainment may be provided with the intent to obtain an improper business advantage for Delivr. Reasonable hospitality is only permitted if incidental to a legitimate business meeting. iii. Providing Funding or Other Support to Third Parties (including Sponsorships, Grants and Donations) Providing funding or other support to third parties, such as through sponsorships, grants and donations, may create a heightened risk if such support is provided – or may reasonably be perceived to be provided – in exchange for an improper business advantage. Accordingly, funding or support to third parties may only be provided if permitted by applicable laws, are of a reasonable value, made in direct support of a legitimate business purpose, such as supporting medical education or improving patient welfare, and follows this Policy. Some country laws prohibit or strictly limit the nature or value of sponsorships that may be provided to Public Officials. Funding or support to third parties may not be provided with the intent to obtain an improper business advantage for Delivr. iv. Political Contributions and Lobbying Activities All political contributions made on Delivr’s behalf to Public Officials must be compliant with applicable laws, regulations and industry codes. Further, political contributions must be fully transparent. For purposes of this Policy, political contributions include both monetary and non- monetary (e.g., resources and facilities) contributions to support Public Officials, political parties or political initiatives. Lobbying activities must always be compliant with applicable laws and regulations, and may not be made with the intent to improperly influence the decision of a person or group of people to purchase, prescribe, use or recommend any Delivr product, or to improperly provide any other favorable treatment to Delivr. v. Facilitation Payments Delivr prohibits all Delivr Personnel from offering or authorizing the offer of “facilitation payments,” which are small, unofficial payments (sometimes referred to as “tips” or “grease payments”) to Public Officials to expedite the performance of routine government actions. All requests for facilitation payments (either direct or indirect) must be reported to Delivr’s Legal Department, see Section 5 below. By contrast, expediting payments made to a government agency itself (not to an individual) that have been officially authorized (such as an official fee to expedite passport renewal) are not facilitation payments and, accordingly, are permissible. vi. Receiving Improper Advantages from Third Parties (e.g. Vendors) In accordance with Delivr’s Conflict of Interest Policy and certain Anti-Bribery and Anti- Corruption Laws, Delivr Personnel and Third-Party Representatives may not accept any excessive gifts, gratuities or entertainment from companies, such as vendors, that have business dealings with Delivr. You may accept gifts, entertainment, meals or social invitations that are part of normal, ethical business practices. Please refer to the guidance contained in Delivr’s Conflict of Interest Policy available on GNET, or consult with your manager and/or Delivr’s Legal Department for further information. vii. Mergers and Acquisitions In connection with merger and acquisition transactions, Delivr considers and evaluates the potential risks posed by an acquisition target under Anti-Bribery and Anti-Corruption Laws and the potential impact such risks could pose to Delivr. Any questions about the level of due diligence required should be directed to Delivr’s Legal Department. B. Retention and Oversight of Third-Party Representatives Delivr may face liabilities relating to violations of Anti-Bribery and Anti-Corruption Laws by its Third-Party Representatives, even in situations in which Delivr is unaware that illegal payments have been made. Delivr’s reputation for conducting its business using only legal and ethical means could be undone by a single act of a Third-Party Representative. Delivr engages Third Party Representatives to provide services for or on behalf of the company in the normal course of business. Delivr Personnel who retain Third Party Representatives are responsible for appropriate oversight of the conduct of the Third-Party Representatives, including taking steps to ensure that the amount Delivr pays does not exceed the fair market value of products or services being purchased. Prior to engaging a Third-Party Representative, Delivr conducts reasonable due diligence regarding the proposed Third-Party Representative. The level of due diligence undertaken depends on the nature of services provided and other circumstances outlined in this Policy, and may include, without limitation, questionnaires to be completed by the Third-Party Representative, interviews and background checks. Delivr will periodically update its due diligence requirements, provide training, require certifications of Anti-Bribery and Anti-Corruption Laws compliance and perform audits of certain Third-Party Representatives. As described in Section 4 above, Delivr Personnel and Third-Party Representatives are required to monitor and take appropriate action if there are signs of potential bribery or corruption risk. C. Signs of Potential Corruption or Bribery Risk No Delivr Personnel or Third-Party Representative may ignore suspicious behavior by others. In conducting Delivr’s business affairs, Delivr Personnel and Third-Party Representatives must be vigilant towards certain factors that may signal the possibility of a bribery or corruption risk, including but not limited to: Threats that Delivr representatives will be denied access to hospitals or clinics unless benefits are provided; Promises of support for Delivr treatment strategies or products in exchange for benefits; Requests for payments in cash, to a third party, or to an account that is not domiciled in the country of the payee or where the transaction takes place; Requests for excessive compensation; Consulting agreements with only vaguely described services; Requests for gifts, hospitality or travel beyond reasonable standards; Third parties with close relations to or associations with Public Officials; Suggestion of favorable treatment in exchange for use of a particular local vendor or supplier; and Past violations of applicable laws. D. Recordkeeping All payments by Delivr and its Third-Party Representatives must be supported by proper documentation, such as receipts and invoices. As mandated by Anti-Bribery and Anti-Corruption Laws, including the books and records requirements of the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, records, including support documentation, must be kept in reasonable detail to accurately and fairly reflect all business affairs and transactions. Additionally, records of all transactions should reflect execution in accordance with internal policies and professional accounting standards. It is prohibited to mischaracterize or omit any transaction on Delivr’s books or those of its Third-Party Representatives. 5. Reporting Violations ----------------------- If any Delivr Personnel or Third Party Representative is taking actions or may be considering taking actions that are suspicious and that you think may be a violation of this Policy, you must immediately report these actions (actual or potential) or suspicions to Delivr’s Legal Department. Delivr has a Complaint Procedure and Non-Retaliation Policy to protect those raising concerns from being retaliated against for doing so. This Policy will be communicated in writing to all employees and through training on a periodic basis. Delivr will periodically audit and monitor its activities to ensure compliance with this Policy. 6. Where to Get Help -------------------- You are strongly encouraged to ask any questions that you may have regarding this Policy, or actions you believe might be in violation of such laws. These questions should be directed to Delivr’s Legal Department.

7.title: Are GS1 Digital Link and Smart, Connected Packaging the same thing?
7.content: Are GS1 Digital Link and Smart, Connected Packaging the same thing? Data Policy Home>Documentation>Are GS1 Digital Link and Smart, Connected Packaging the same thing? Delivr Are GS1 Digital Link and Smart, Connected Packaging the same thing? Are GS1 Digital Link and Smart, Connected Packaging the same thing? Not exactly—but they’re deeply connected. Both help brands provide detailed product information via QR codes. While GS1 Digital Link is a universal barcode standard , smart, connected packaging is a broader concept that uses QR codes (and other triggers) to engage consumers, deliver transparency, and support traceability throughout the product's lifecycle. --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- What is Smart, Connected Packaging? Smart, connected packaging includes labels, inserts, and packaging that use QR codes to provide consumers with more than just printed information. This can include: Ingredients, nutrition, and allergen details Sustainability and sourcing information Recipes, videos, and how-to guides Product authentication or regulatory disclosures Traceability and supply chain visibility Platforms like SmartLabel® are often used as destinations, but the term extends beyond that — enabling brands to build trust, meet compliance requirements, and offer post-purchase engagement. What is GS1 Digital Link? GS1 Digital Link is a global standard that converts product identifiers (such as GTINs) into URLs. A single GS1 Digital Link-enabled QR code can: Be scanned at retail POS Connect to SmartLabel®, product pages, or instructions Route based on language, location, or device Enable product recalls, verification, or serialization Support traceability, blockchain, and compliance With resolver logic , you can future-proof packaging by updating destinations over time—without changing the code. Key Differences ------------------- Feature Smart, Connected Packaging GS1 Digital Link What it is A way to connect physical packaging to digital content A global standard for web-enabled barcodes Purpose Consumer Engagement, Product InformationTransparency, Traceability POS Transactions, Product Information, Supply Chain Traceability, Consumer Engagement QR type Any QR (static or dynamic)Standards-based dynamic or static QR POS compatibility Not always Yes Uses resolver? Optional Optional Delivr support Full ecosystem support Fully GS1-aligned Why this matters. More brands are adopting GS1 Digital Link as the backbone of their connected packaging strategy. It simplifies things by using a single QR code for both consumer and supply chain use—eliminating the need for multiple codes on pack. How Delivr helps. Delivr makes connected packaging scalable and straightforward. As a GS1-aligned, DENSO-licensed platform, we help brands: Launch GS1 Digital Link–enabled QR codes Power SmartLabel® and other product experiences through one code Support serialization, batch traceability, and blockchain Maintain privacy, compliance, and seamless user experience One smart code. Endless possibilities. Powered by Delivr. Some leading consumer packaged goods (CPG) brands have launched an effort to offer data controlled by the brand through a secondary QR code. The SmartLabel content operates similarly. --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- However, the GS1 Digital Link is a single barcode that can be used for both point-of-sale (POS) checkout and by consumers to access product information. Moreover, the Digital Link's durability allows for using a "Resolver," which enables different applications to direct to other web pages using the same barcode. 12.title: Asset Intelligence Powered by Dynamic QR Infrastructure
12.description: Markdown export of Delivr Asset Intelligence page
12.content: Asset Intelligence Powered by Dynamic QR Infrastructure URL Source: http://delivr.com/page/831/asset-intelligence-powered-by-dynamic-qr-infrastructure Markdown Content: For years, basic barcodes on equipment and parts have been limited to inventory management and shipment tracking. By replacing — or supplementing — the barcode with a “smart” QR code, manufacturers can store more data, streamline workflows, and make information accessible to operations, suppliers, warehouses, service teams, and customers across every touchpoint in the product lifecycle. Combining QR codes with a modern digital encoding scheme and an experience portal unlocks nearly limitless possibilities. From Material Master and Batch/Lot to item-level serialization, this new digital link framework connects components, finished units, and shipments to rich information and services across the supply chain, product cloud, and other systems. The result — use cases that empower people to see, know, and do more at every stage of the product journey. -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Designed for Operations, Suppliers, Warehouse, Service Teams, and Customers --------------------------------------------------------------------------- Operations. See the entire product lifecycle in real time, track performance, and resolve bottlenecks to keep workflows running smoothly. Suppliers. Monitor parts and shipments from production to installation, improving supply chain efficiency and customer satisfaction. Warehouse. Track inventory accurately, reduce misplaced parts, and simplify daily operations for a more efficient workflow. Service. Scan equipment on-site for instant access to manuals, troubleshooting, and resources, boosting field efficiency and problem-solving. Customers. Link equipment to manuals, videos, warranties, and service requests, reducing downtime and making maintenance effortless. Real-World Use Cases -------------------- Instant Asset Tracking. Scan a part or piece of equipment to view its details, history, and location immediately. Maintenance Management. Ensure timely servicing by linking QR codes to maintenance logs. Field Support. Technicians access manuals, service guides, and on-site information to replace parts. Inventory Audits. Count and verify stock quickly, eliminating manual errors. Returns and Spare Parts Tracking. Streamline reverse logistics for faster replacements, repairs, and forensic analysis. How It Works ------------ ➀ Generate Labels. ---------------- Create labels with QR codes for each part or piece of equipment. ➁ Attach Critical Data. --------------------- Link QR codes to manuals, specifications, warranties, or service history. ➂ Scan Anywhere. -------------- Smartphones or handheld scanners update inventory and status in real time. ➃ Monitor and Audit. ------------------ Track movement, usage, and availability in real time. ➄ Analyze and Optimize. --------------------- Use scan data to enhance inventory management, reduce downtime, and prevent bottlenecks. ➅ Dynamic QR Codes. ----------------- Update linked information at any time without reprinting labels. This feature not only saves time but also cuts costs associated with delays and reprinting. Imagine parts that move between warehouses and service vehicles; any change in their details is instant. By preventing downtime due to outdated information, dynamic QR codes help you avoid high operational costs and ensure the seamless movement of parts across multiple facilities. Why QR Codes Work for Parts & Equipment --------------------------------------- Reduce Errors. Prevent incorrect parts from being installed or shipped. Save Time. Scan items instead of logging them manually. Boost Visibility. Instantly see the location of every part and piece of equipment. Improve Maintenance Compliance. Ensure timely servicing of machines and equipment. Lower Costs. Reduce downtime, overstocking, and lost items. Fact. According to a study by the Logistics and Supply Chain Management Society, companies using QR codes for parts management reduce misplacements by up to 40% and improve maintenance compliance by 30%. Integrates Seamlessly With Your Existing Systems ------------------------------------------------ Compatible with ERP, SAP, WMS, and field service platforms. Works on smartphones, tablets, and industrial scanners. Track parts across warehouses, trucks, and service sites. Start Optimizing Your Parts & Equipment Today --------------------------------------------- Generate, scan, and track your parts and equipment. Reduce errors, improve maintenance, and gain complete visibility into your operations, all with a simple QR code. To make your decision risk-free, we invite you to pilot a small batch of parts using our QR code system before committing to a full rollout. This trial period will allow you to experience the transformative impact on your operations firsthand, without any upfront commitment. Custom Programming for Zebra Printers ------------------------------------- Precision label printing for parts and equipment. ------------------------------------------------- We provide custom ZPL (Zebra Programming Language) and ZBI (Zebra Basic Interpreter) development to support the logistics of high-volume parts and equipment throughout your entire supply chain. From asset tags to durable QR code labels, we design ZPL templates and printer logic that integrate into your operational workflows. What We Deliver with ZPL and ZBI -------------------------------- Custom ZPL Label Templates -------------------------- Precisely formatted labels for parts and equipment, optimized for scan reliability for different environmental conditions, durability, and industrial use. Dynamic QR Code Generation in ZPL --------------------------------- Embed QR codes containing ERP/MRP variables such as supplier entity, material master, part numbers, model numbers, manufacturer dates, serial numbers, batch/lot, hazardous materials, maintenance references, or URLs that link to live system data. ZPL Workflow Automation ----------------------- Seamlessly integrate your label printing into your existing ERP/MRP workflow and build the business logic with trigger events such as when parts are received, moved, serviced, or issued. Conditional Printing Logic -------------------------- Use ZPL to control a self-adjusting or flexible label layout, data fields, and print behavior based on asset type, location, or operational state. Printer-Specific Optimization ----------------------------- ZPL optimized for print speed, print density (darkness level), label size, media type (thermal transfer / thermal direct), dpi resolution across a variety of Zebra printers (desktop, industrial, mobile). Stand Alone or Network Integrated --------------------------------- Custom code logic can be developed to operate as an isolated, stand-alone workstation or a fully networked integration. Industrial Labels or POS Receipt Printing ----------------------------------------- Depending on your needs, custom ZPL and ZBI development can be used to create either individual labels or continuous purchase receipts. Built for Parts & Equipment Environments ---------------------------------------- Durable asset and parts labels printed using ZPL and ZBI. High-contrast QR codes optimized for fast scanning in warehouses and field environments. Support for fixed desktop, industrial, and mobile Zebra printers using ZPL. Consistent labeling across multiple sites and printer models. Why ZPL Matters --------------- Accuracy. Eliminate manual label creation and mismatched data. Speed. Print labels instantly as part of operational workflows. Consistency. Standardize part and equipment identification across your organization. Flexibility. Update QR code destinations without changing printed labels when using dynamic links. Input Triggers. Generating labels from a variety of triggers, such as ERP-integrated requests, user input from a keyboard, or scanned data. From ZPL template design to automated printing workflows, we help you unlock the full power of Zebra printers. Every part and piece of equipment is clearly labeled, easily scanned, and always traceable.

13.title: Attractive and Effective QR Codes: Design Tips and Inspiration
13.content: Attractive and Effective QR Codes: Design Tips and Inspiration URL: https://delivr.com/faq/1616/attractive-and-effective-qr-codes-design-tips-and-inspiration Title: Attractive and Effective QR Codes: Design Tips and Inspiration URL Source: http://delivr.com/faq/1616/attractive-and-effective-qr-codes-design-tips-and-inspiration Markdown Content: QR codes have evolved far beyond pixelated black-and-white grids. They’re now powerful visual tools that can elevate your brand, spark curiosity, and seamlessly bridge the physical and digital worlds. Whether you’re designing packaging, signage, or marketing collateral, your QR codes should be as striking as they are functional. And with the right platform, creating visually engaging QR codes is accessible to everyone. -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Why Visual Appeal Matters ------------------------- If a QR code looks like a glitch, it’s likely to be ignored. Today’s audience expects every design element to be intentional, even a QR code needs to stand out. When your code is eye-catching and paired with a compelling call to action, it draws attention, builds trust, and drives interaction, be it from a poster, mailer, or product label. Designs That Capture Attention. ------------------------------- What makes certain QR codes so clickable? Here are the high-impact design strategies brands are using. Brand colors over default black-and-white . A pop of brand color makes your QR instantly recognizable. Logo integration . Embedding a logo adds trust and reinforces identity. Creative frames + CTAs . “Scan for a Deal” performs way better than a lone barcode. Stylized modules & corners . Rounded edges, unique shapes, and playful patterns make codes look like part of your design aesthetic. Example: Picture a soft coral QR code on a beauty product, with the brand monogram in the center and “Tap to Get Your Sample” elegantly framed. Balancing Aesthetics with Functionality. ---------------------------------------- A beautifully styled code is worthless if it won’t scan. Here’s how to hit the mark. Contrast is key . Use a light background with dark modules (or vice versa) to ensure reliable scans. Respect the quiet zone . Keep at least 4 module-widths of clear space around the code. T est extensively . Try it on both iOS and Android devices to confirm scannability. Avoid over-designing with excessive gradients, intricate details, or animations that can interfere with scanning Common Design Pitfalls to Avoid. -------------------------------- Don’t let good design go wrong. Low-res exports blurry scans. Feature overload scanning errors. Triggering desktop sites poor user experience. Poor contrast or clashing backgrounds low scan success. How Delivr Simplifies Stunning QR Code Design. ---------------------------------------------- Delivr doesn’t just help you generate QR codes, it gives you a complete design and engagement toolkit built for modern marketing. Live QR code previews as you design. Instantly see how your code will look and function across devices while you customize. Editable QR codes post-deployment . Made a mistake or changed your mind? Update your QR code’s destination, design, or metadata even after it’s live. Campaign grouping & management . Organize your QR codes into folders, campaigns, or clients for better team collaboration and tracking. Smart redirect feature s. Send users to different destinations based on language, device type, or time of day for a tailored experience. Deep scan insights . Go beyond basic metrics with detailed tracking on traffic sources, user platforms, and scanning behavior. No app required for end users . Delivr QR codes are universally scannable with any standard camera — no special app or tech needed. Optimized mobile experiences . Ensure every scan leads to a responsive, mobile-ready destination, no extra effort required. With Delivr, creating a QR code becomes part of a larger strategy not a standalone task. You get tools to design, deliver, and measure with confidence. Style Meets Strategy. --------------------- In 2025, QR codes aren’t just tech, they’re touchpoints. Every scan is a chance to educate, inspire, or convert. Achieving the right blend of form, function, and flexibility, especially with a platform like Delivr, lets these tiny icons become powerful assets in your marketing toolkit. Final Thought: Design Smart, Scan Smarter. ------------------------------------------ In a world where attention spans are short and digital touchpoints matter more than ever, your QR code shouldn’t just function it should shine. Great design captures attention. Smart strategy drives results. And with Delivr, you don’t have to choose between the two. Whether you’re a creative professional, brand manager, or small business owner, Delivr transforms QR code creation into a seamless part of your design workflow, giving you the power to craft codes that look amazing, scan flawlessly, and adapt to your needs even after they’re live. Because when your QR code does more than just connect, it converts. ------------------------------------------------------------------- Start designing with Delivr and turn every scan into a brand experience worth remembering.

Data Policy Home>Documentation>Best Practices for Designing Digital Out-Of-Home (DOOH) Creative. Delivr Best Practices for Designing Digital Out-Of-Home (DOOH) Creative. Best Practices for Designing Digital Out-Of-Home (DOOH) Creative. Great digital poster design combines smart, minimal copy with high-contrast and striking visuals. Delivering a strong creative with a clear call-to-action is the best way to use digital out-of-home (DOOH), particularly across roadside displays where there is a need to quickly create impact with shorter visibility times. If you are designing your own creative, below are some guidelines to assist you. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ Simplify everything. ------------------------ Focus on one message. Don't present a complex message or numerous images. The best outdoor media reduces a complex message to its essential elements. Product Identification. --------------------------- Make sure you can read the advertiser's name. Simple font use. -------------------- Use two or fewer fonts in your writing design. If you include more fonts, it may create confusion. Bold is better but Medium and Regular weight non-serif fonts work also. Avoid using Thin and Ultralight weight fonts. Make the text large. ------------------------ Fonts should be simple, clear, and easy to read. Digital screen text should be large enough that people can read at a glance from a reasonable distance. That said, ad copy at close viewing should be no smaller than 30px. Readable headlines and short copy. -------------------------------------- The length of the sentence should be short, Strive for no more than 10 words total, and 5-7 words in the headline. High contrast. ------------------ The color of the text should be in high contrast with the background for better visibility and readability. Short words. ---------------- Use short words for faster reading comprehension. Use bright, bold colors. ---------------------------- Using colour is crucial in enhancing visual attention, so using a dark background with elements of vibrant colour or contrasting color combinations will deliver the most eye catching and impactful ads. Stick with fully saturated web-safe hues. Digital displays often work best with analogous color schemes (those that are next to each other on the color wheel). Complimentary colors (such as yellow and purple) are not legible together because they have similar value. Use RGB colors. ------------------- Design as you would for a website, TV, or computer monitor. Pick your images wisely. ---------------------------- Take a small object and make it large (like a watch) rather than a large object small (like a building). Avoid using landscapes or complex scenes. We recommend 3 visual elements or less, total. As example, 1 image, 1 logo, and 1 headline. Fine detail is more suited to pedestrian traffic. Keep images simple for roadside targeted ads. Test your creative. ----------------------- View your creative from 3 meters and for 5 seconds. Then from for 5 seconds from 5-10 meters. This simulates viewing your creative from the road. Does your ad read well? Data Policy Home>Documentation>Best Practices for High-Performing Mobile Pages Delivr Best Practices for High-Performing Mobile Pages Best Practices for High-Performing Mobile Pages High-performing mobile pages prioritize speed, keeping load times fast and page sizes small to reduce bounce rates and improve engagement. Content should be concise and outcome-focused, with strong headlines that clearly communicate value at a glance. Simple, lightweight design helps users stay focused while supporting faster performance on mobile devices. Clear and prominent calls to action guide users to the next step without confusion. Thoughtful use of visuals and accessible design ensures the experience is easy to use, inclusive, and effective across channels. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Begin with Speed Optimization. ------------------------------ Before diving into optimization strategies, ask yourself: 'Does your page exceed 1 MB in size?' This simple check can quickly help you gauge the urgency of the changes needed to improve performance. Improving load times is necessary because they directly impact visitor retention and conversion rates. Prioritize streamlining data and reducing server response times to ensure pages load in 1-2 seconds. Once speed is optimized, focus on Concise, Outcome-Driven Copy. --------------------------------------------------------------- Users should immediately grasp the value proposition. Next, enhance with Strong Headlines and Subheads that instantly attract and maintain attention. Add a Call to Action. --------------------- Ensure visible, compelling calls to action so users always know the next step, increasing completion rates and making every key conversion opportunity clear and easy to act on. Adopt a Lightweight Design. --------------------------- This reduces loading times further and minimizes bounce rates. Build Accessibility-first Structures. ------------------------------------- This increases audience reach and trust, enabling all users—regardless of ability—to interact confidently, leading to higher engagement and brand inclusivity. Incorporate Purposeful Visuals. ------------------------------- Enrich content without hindering functionality. Omnichannel Reach. 16.title: Bridging the gap for inclusive experiences.
16.content: Bridging the gap for inclusive experiences. URL: https://delivr.com/page/806/bridging-the-gap-for-inclusive-experiences Title: Accessibility & QR Codes URL Source: http://delivr.com/page/806/bridging-the-gap-for-inclusive-experiences Markdown Content: Embracing accessibility as a core element of your business strategy is not just essential; it’s empowering. It’s about more than just meeting legal requirements; it’s about creating a world where everyone can fully participate regardless of their abilities. QR codes, a powerful tool for improving access to information and services, particularly for individuals with disabilities, put the power in your hands. By integrating QR codes with assistive technologies, you can confidently create inclusive experiences catering to various needs, knowing you’re making a significant difference. ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- QR Codes and Assistive Technologies ----------------------------------- QR codes offer a convenient way to access information. However, their full potential is realized when they are compatible with assistive technologies like screen readers, voice assistants, and haptic feedback systems. For users with visual impairments, the process is not just simple; it’s reassuring. They can scan QR codes using a smartphone and then relay the encoded information through a screen reader. This allows access to menus, product details, or instructional guides without the need for printed text. Additionally, haptic feedback and audio prompts can enhance the user experience, making interaction with QR code-enabled content smooth and comfortable. Businesses can seamlessly integrate QR codes with Siri or Google Assistant. This innovative integration ensures that individuals with varying abilities can independently access important information, avoiding unnecessary barriers. It’s a technology designed to work for everyone, making the user experience smooth and intuitive. Inclusive Applications of QR Codes ---------------------------------- Many industries have embraced QR codes to promote inclusivity by offering innovative solutions that cater to diverse accessibility needs. These are not just solutions but innovations that are changing how we think about accessibility. Some noteworthy applications include: Voice-Enabled Menus . Restaurants and cafes utilize QR codes that link to audio descriptions of their menus, allowing visually impaired customers to listen to menu options instead of reading them. Accessible Public Spaces . Museums, parks, and historical sites use QR codes to provide guided audio tours, making cultural and recreational experiences more inclusive. Pharmacy and Healthcare Information . Medication packaging and medical brochures can include QR codes that link to audio instructions or videos demonstrating proper usage. Innovative Packaging . QR codes on product packaging can direct consumers to accessibility-friendly content, such as sign language videos or text-to-speech descriptions. These examples illustrate how QR codes dismantle barriers, ensuring inclusive and equitable interactions for everyone. Best Practices for Designing Accessible QR Codes ------------------------------------------------ While QR codes enhance accessibility, businesses must adopt best practices to optimize their effectiveness. Ensure High Contrast and Proper Sizing . Use dark QR codes on light backgrounds (or the reverse), and make sure they are large enough for easy scanning. Provide Alternative Text (Alt Text) Descriptions . Include a brief, descriptive label explaining the purpose of the QR code for screen readers. Link to Accessibility-Friendly Content . Ensure the linked content is compatible with screen readers, includes closed video captions, and incorporates voice-over functionality when necessary. Test with Assistive Technologies . Before launching, verify that the QR codes work correctly with screen readers and other assistive tools. Avoid Decorative QR Codes . While branding is essential, overly stylized QR codes may be difficult to scan. Opt for simple, scannable designs with clear call-to-action labels. Delivr’s Commitment to Accessibility ------------------------------------ As a human-centered software and services company, Delivr prioritizes user-friendly, accessible solutions. By enabling brands to create dynamic QR codes that seamlessly integrate with assistive technologies, Delivr ensures that businesses can enhance their customer experience while promoting inclusivity. Whether for retail, healthcare, education, or public services, Delivr empowers organizations to make their content accessible to everyone. WCAG Accessibility Violations Monitoring Summary for Delivr and the Landing Pages it Hosts. A-level violations: 0 violations AA-level violations: 0 violations AAA-level violations: 0 violations At Delivr, everyone should be free to access content quickly, regardless of their abilities. That’s why we are dedicated to making our website simple, intuitive, and accessible for all users. We work hard to ensure everyone can use our services comfortably and independently. We strive for inclusion and a sense of security online to be the standard, not the exception. Conclusion ---------- QR codes are not just convenient; they also serve as a gateway to inclusivity. By designing QR codes with accessibility in mind, businesses can create seamless, user-friendly experiences for individuals of all abilities. Platforms like Delivr make it easier than ever to implement accessible QR codes, ensuring that no one is left behind. Last Updated 19 Feb 2026
