168.title: Give everything a unique identity.
168.content: Give everything a unique identity. URL: https://delivr.com/page/764/super-simple-serialization Title: Super Simple Serialization by Delivr. URL Source: http://delivr.com/page/764/super-simple-serialization Markdown Content: Ridiculously easy to use, Super Simple Serialization is plug & play, requiring no infrastructure, software, or APIs to maintain. -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- We can think of many remarkable uses for serialization; here are three. ➀ Physical products. ------------------ Make product registration easier. Super Simple Serialization eliminates the need to enter or search for a serial number manually. By scanning a QR code, a serial number can be filled in automatically on a registration page. ➁ Digital communications. ----------------------- Increase the effectiveness of your sales efforts by making your digital (or print) communications even more personal. Super Simple Serialization bridges the divide between mass marketing and personalized engagement. ➂ Smart objects. -------------- Instead of generating and managing thousands or millions of managed Short links or QR codes, use Super Simple Serialization to power a serialized link that passes the object's identity to your service or webpage. Serialize product packaging, direct mail, posters, POS materials, equipment, and parts with a unique identifier. Other uses for serialization. ----------------------------- Improve inventory visibility by serializing your inventory, giving each piece of merchandise a unique digital identity - its digital twin. Assign individual serial numbers to printed items to facilitate personalized URLs. With our DIY product serialization feature, you can easily track the sales of your products across different regions and identify any areas where they may be appearing despite not being distributed there. Our reporting system provides you with valuable insights into the performance of your products in various markets. Serialize the links to your campaigns so the social influencer can be attributed. Print a serialized QR code on a car rental receipt, making it easier for customers to reach their personalized rental landing page. Manage single-use coupons and promotions. Serialization has never been this straightforward. Mastery in 5 Steps. ------------------- You'll need a Delivr VIP account. ➀ Sign in to your Delivr Account Dashboard and shorten your destination link, e.g., https://yourdomain.com. This action creates a campaign with a managed Short URL. Copy the Short URL for the campaign, e.g., https://delivr.com/abc123 ➁ Create a spreadsheet where you append a series of unique IDs to your campaign short URL, e.g., https://delivr.com/abc123/UniqueID. This action self-provisions a series of serialized URLs for encoding into NFC tags or to be variably printed as QR codes. You could also programmatically generate the unique URLs/QR codes on demand at the point of sale and digitally print them on customer receipts. For example, this would look something like this, https://delivr.com/abc123/0001, https://delivr.com/abc123/0002, https://delivr.com/abc123/0003. Or, like this using a multi-level structured data/serialization approach such as https://delivr.com/path/05-0123-4500-0008, https://delivr.com/path/01/05012345000008/10/ABC123, https://qr.yourdomain.com/path/[storeID], https://id.yourdomain.com/path/01/05012345000008/10/ABC123, etc. See also, GS1 Digital Link. No limits. ---------- There are no limits to the number of serializations you can create - hundreds, thousands, or millions. And, because you are self-provisioning on demand, there are no APIs to interact with and slow you down. ➂ Provide this spreadsheet to your NFC tag or Print provider. Or, have your development team program into your POS or other systems. Using best practice guidelines during the design and setup process, you must designate an area on your materials to include the QR code. Please confirm with your printer that they can variably print or programmatically generate QR codes on the press from a given series of URLs. If not,contact us we know printers that can. Most NFC tag providers can encode tags from a spreadsheet. ➃ Once your materials are in use, each QR code scan or NFC tap is captured by Delivr for reporting. Simultaneously; visitors are redirected to your destination link with the unique ID appended, e.g.; https://yourdomain.com/?trace 0001 ➄ Your destination page/application can ignore the unique ID or be modified by your development team to accept the unique ID for further processing, regex processing, workflow, and action, e.g., deliver different experiences. Custom web applications can also be designed and developed by the Delivr Team. Talk to us about your goals. We're here to discuss your plans and help you get started. LET'S TALK Need safer, smarter packaging? TrueMrk® Brand Protection builds upon Super Simple Serialization℠ to make it simple to combine brand protection and consumer engagement into a single, intuitive experience. TrueMrk provides product serialization, authenticity, provenance, and consumer engagement while identifying counterfeiting and gray market diversion incidents. Learn more about TrueMrk Brand Protection. About Delivr ------------ Delivr is a marketing technology platform thousands of businesses use to drive audiences from print to digital experiences. Established in 2008, Delivr has developed software to track and visualize consumer engagement with printed media, labels, and packaging. Delivr has extended its marketing, consumer engagement, and analytics platform to authenticate products and identify counterfeiting and gray market diversion incidents.

169.title: Making a positive impact on the planet, one QR code scan at a time.
169.content: Making a positive impact on the planet, one QR code scan at a time. URL: https://delivr.com/page/804/sustainability-and-qr-codes-making-a-positive-impact-on-the-planet-one-qr-code-scan-at-a-time Title: Sustainability & QR Codes URL Source: http://delivr.com/page/804/sustainability-and-qr-codes-making-a-positive-impact-on-the-planet-one-qr-code-scan-at-a-time Markdown Content: For businesses, sustainability is not just a goal but a responsibility. In this journey, the practicality of QR codes has emerged as a powerful tool. By facilitating paperless solutions, QR codes are instrumental in helping companies reduce waste, conserve resources, and streamline operations, significantly contributing to sustainability. ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Why QR Codes Are Good for the Planet ------------------------------------ Let’s talk paper. Producing one ton requires 17 trees, thousands of gallons of water, and a significant amount of energy. After all that resource use, many printed materials end up in landfills, releasing methane as it decomposes. Now, picture the positive change we can bring by replacing these materials with a simple QR code. It’s a small step, but it’s a step towards a more sustainable future. Imagine the impact of replacing menus, brochures, tickets, and receipts with a simple QR code. By using digital alternatives, businesses can skip the printing process entirely. QR codes are quick, flexible, and a responsible choice that helps us reduce paper waste and make a positive change for our planet. Consider this staggering number: If just 1% of all marketing flyers in the U.S. were replaced with QR codes, over 2.5 million trees could be saved every year. This is not just a statistic — it’s a testament to the power of QR codes in significantly reducing paper use and making a real difference in sustainability. Industries Leading the Paperless Movement ----------------------------------------- Industries are not just adopting QR codes but leading the paperless movement. QR codes are appearing everywhere, helping industries rethink their reliance on paper and showing their commitment to sustainability. Consumer Packaged Goods (CPGs) ------------------------------ QR codes on packaging provide customers with detailed product information, such as sourcing, nutritional facts, and usage tips. This transparency builds trust and engagement, allowing consumers to make informed choices while increasing brand loyalty. Manufacturers ------------- QR codes improve inventory management and production tracking. By placing codes on products or packaging, manufacturers can quickly access information about production batches, quality control, and supply chain logistics, enhancing efficiency and reducing errors. Retail ------ Forget those long paper receipts. Many stores now offer digital receipts via QR codes, making it easier for customers to track purchases and returns while reducing paper waste. Events ------ From concert tickets to conference schedules, QR codes replace bulky, printed materials with sleek, digital solutions. Education --------- Schools and universities use QR codes to distribute study materials, track attendance, organize events, and foster eco-friendly habits among students. This use of QR codes should make us all optimistic about the future generation’s environmental consciousness. Healthcare ---------- Hospitals are digitizing patient forms and information-sharing processes. It’s good for the planet and makes life easier for everyone involved. Restaurants ----------- Digital menus are one of the most visible examples. A quick scan lets diners access an up-to-date menu on their phone without printing, a game-changer for restaurants that change their menus often. These industries are proving that sustainability and efficiency can go hand in hand. How Delivr Supports Sustainable Practices ----------------------------------------- At Delivr, we’re proud to play a small part in helping businesses go green. Please take one of our manufacturer partners, for example. By swapping printed brochures, sale sheets, and catalogs for QR code campaigns connected to digital PDF versions of these materials, they reduced their paper use by 85% — and saw higher customer engagement. Our secure, dynamic QR codes allow businesses to update content on the fly without reprinting anything. As a result, businesses use fewer resources, generate less waste, and are more adaptable. But this isn’t just about technology — it’s about giving brands the tools to make meaningful changes. We’re here to help companies turn their sustainability goals into actionable, everyday practices. Why Sustainability Matters to Your Customers -------------------------------------------- Sustainability isn’t just a corporate goal; consumers care deeply about it. A global Nielsen survey found that 81% of people expect businesses to take the lead in improving the environment. y choosing eco-friendly options like QR codes, brands show their customers that they care about more than just profit — they care about the planet, too. It’s not just the right thing to do; it’s smart business. QR Codes. The Digital Bridge to Innovation. ------------------------------------------- While their environmental benefits are clear, QR codes also open the door to innovation. They can link users to virtual product demos, digital catalogs, feedback forms, or augmented reality experiences. It’s a win-win: reducing waste while delivering fresh, engaging content. Think of them as a gateway to doing more with less. Whether replacing printed manuals with a simple scan or sharing live updates with a dynamic QR code, businesses can embrace innovation while reducing their footprint. Small Changes, Big Impact ------------------------- Switching to QR codes might seem small, but the collective impact can be huge. By reducing paper use, businesses can save resources, lower costs, and contribute to sustainability efforts. It’s about more than what one company can do; it’s about what we can all achieve together. Whether you’re a brand, manufacturer, retailer, or a global enterprise, there’s a simple truth: doing right by the planet is good for business. QR codes are just one tool in the journey toward sustainability, but they’re powerful. The question isn’t whether to adopt their use but how quickly you can switch. Let’s make sustainability the norm, one QR code scan at a time.

170.title: Team Collaboration Guide.
170.content: Team Collaboration Guide. URL: https://delivr.com/faq/1589/team-collaboration-guide Title: Team Collaboration Guide. URL Source: http://delivr.com/faq/1589/team-collaboration-guide Markdown Content: Delivr’s team collaboration tools help groups stay organized, aligned, and efficient when managing marketing, product, or event campaigns. With features like Labels, Groups, Tags, Stars, Workspaces, and UTM Parameters, teams can structure campaigns clearly, find assets quickly, and track performance with confidence. --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Managing & Organizing Campaigns in Delivr with Teams Quick Reference When multiple people work together on marketing, event, or product campaigns, organization is the key to staying aligned and efficient. In Delivr , tools like Labels, Groups, Tags, Stars, Workspaces, and UTM Parameters make it easy for teams to structure their work, find what they need instantly, and analyze results without confusion. Assign clear, descriptive titles and notes so campaigns are instantly recognizable and their purpose is understood by everyone on the team. Arrange related campaigns into folder-style collections for faster access, better organization, and easier management. Apply consistent keywords for powerful filtering and quick cross-project searches. Mark your most important campaigns or groups to keep top priorities visible and easy to find. Automatically gathers all campaigns and groups with the same star color into a centralized, customizable dashboard. UTM Parameters . Add tracking codes to monitor campaign performance, traffic sources, and audience behavior in analytics tools. By combining these features, teams can keep campaigns structured, easy to find, and measurable from creation to results. Labels Campaign Titles & Notes Think of labels as your campaign’s internal name tag —a quick way for everyone to know what a campaign is about without opening it. Purpose for Teams --------------------- Give each campaign a clear, descriptive title and notes so any teammate can instantly understand its purpose. This reduces “What’s this for?” questions and speeds up onboarding for new members. How to Use Collaboratively ------------------------------ Agree on a naming convention (e.g., Event - Location - Date or Product - Channel - Quarter). Use the notes field to document objectives, deadlines, or special instructions. Keep labels consistent across the team for cleaner filtering and reporting. Groups Folder-Style Organization Campaign Groups are like folders , keeping related campaigns together for easy navigation. Purpose for Teams --------------------- Store all related campaigns in one place so anyone can find them—whether they’re tied to a single event, product line, or department. How to Use Collaboratively ------------------------------ Create groups for major initiatives (e.g., “Spring 2025 Product Launch” or “Trade Show Assets”). Assign permissions so the right team members can access and manage the group. Use groups as shared “home bases” for multi-channel campaigns. Organizing Campaigns in Multiple Groups ------------------------------------------- Campaigns can be placed in one or more Groups , giving you the flexibility to organize them in multiple ways for different purposes. This is especially useful when a single campaign is relevant to more than one category or initiative. For example, a single campaign might belong to a Product Launch group—so it’s tracked alongside all assets for that launch—while also being assigned to other Groups based on media type (e.g., digital displays, in-store posters, or packaging). This multi-group structure allows teams to: Find campaigns faster by searching within different organizational contexts. Collaborate more efficiently when multiple departments need access to the same campaign for different purposes. Report and analyze by segment , such as comparing how the same campaign performs across various media types or placements. By leveraging multiple Groups, you can keep campaigns logically organized without duplicating work or creating extra copies of the same asset. Tags Flexible Classification Tags are keywords that make searching and filtering lightning-fast—perfect when projects overlap. Purpose for Teams --------------------- Let team members find campaigns by theme, audience, channel, or stage without digging through folders. How to Use Collaboratively ------------------------------ Decide on a shared tag vocabulary so everyone uses the same keywords (e.g., “Retail,” “Social,” “B2B”). Layer tags with groups for more granular searches. Use tags to quickly identify campaigns across different teams or purposes. Stars & Workspaces Prioritize at a Glance Stars Keep Priorities Visible ----------------------------------- Stars make it easy to keep your most important Campaigns and Groups front and center. They’re color-coded for quick meaning—e.g., red urgent, yellow review needed, green live, purple needs update—and can be applied independently to Campaigns and Groups. Independent Starring. Starring a Group does not star the Campaigns inside, and starring a Campaign does not affect its Group. Organized Execution. Use stars to prioritize and group campaigns without altering live settings or disrupting reporting. Starred Items & Workspaces. When you star a Campaign or Group, it’s automatically added to the corresponding Workspace based on its star color. Workspaces Your Centralized Dashboards -------------------------------------------- Workspaces are centralized dashboards in Delivr that automatically organize Campaigns and Campaign Groups by their star color. This makes it easier for teams to access, collaborate, and focus on the work that matters most. Perfect for organizing by team, goal, task, or any custom purpose , Workspaces can be named however you like and adapted to your workflow. Always Up to Date . Unstarring a Campaign or Group instantly removes it from any Workspace without affecting the original item. Workspace Notes . Add context for the team—such as goals, responsibilities, deadlines, or campaign status—directly inside the Workspace. Private & Flexible . Visible only to the Account Owner/Admin and Contributors. Workspaces do not affect how others see or use Campaigns. Campaign Parameters (UTMs) Track the Full Journey UTM parameters turn your campaigns into measurable data sources in tools like Google Analytics. Purpose for Teams --------------------- Attribute results accurately, understand where your traffic is coming from, and link QR scans to conversions. How to Use Collaboratively ------------------------------ Use a shared UTM template so your analytics remain consistent. Assign responsibility—decide who adds UTMs before launch. Review UTM performance together to refine future campaigns. Putting It All Together A well-structured workflow might look like this: ------------------------------------------------ 1. Create a Group (e.g., “Spring Product Launch”). 2. Build campaigns with clear Labels and notes. 3. Add Tags for filtering (e.g., “Retail,” “In-store Display”). 4. Place campaigns into one or more Groups as needed. 5. Star items to surface priorities in Workspaces. 6. Add UTM parameters to track performance and conversions. This combined approach keeps campaigns easy to find, easy to manage, and easy to measure across any team. Best Practices 1. Start with structure . Set naming, tagging, and color-coding rules before launching campaigns. 2. Keep it clean . Archive or unstar old campaigns to prevent clutter. 3. Review together . In team check-ins, confirm campaigns are organized, tagged, and tracked correctly. 4. Document the system . Save these rules in your shared workspace so new team members can onboard quickly.

173.title: The case against retargeting in a privacy-first world.
173.content: The case against retargeting in a privacy-first world. URL: https://delivr.com/page/810/the-case-against-retargeting-in-a-privacy-first-world Title: Privacy & Retargeting URL Source: http://delivr.com/page/810/the-case-against-retargeting-in-a-privacy-first-world Markdown Content: For over a decade, retargeting has tracked users online to recover lost conversions. Its limits are now clear. Changing expectations, stricter laws, and new browser technology have turned retargeting into a strategic risk—one that threatens trust, brand perception, and growth. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- When Retargeting Works — and Why That Matters --------------------------------------------- Retargeting is not inherently flawed. In certain contexts, it can still serve a purpose: Long-consideration purchases where users expect reminders Logged-in, first-party environments where the relationship is explicit Clear intent signals, such as abandoned carts or saved items Recognizing these scenarios is essential. The issue is not retargeting itself, but its default, widespread use without regard for consent quality, user experience, or brand impact. Retargeting Comes Across as Intrusive, Even When It Is Legal ------------------------------------------------------------ Even when compliant, retargeting often feels unsettling to users. “I looked at one pair of shoes once,” the familiar complaint goes, “and now they follow me everywhere.” This response is rational. Trust is emotional, not technical. Most users do not distinguish data types or compliance frameworks — only repeated, unwanted brand exposure. Studies have shown that browsing-based personalization is often seen as “creepy,” especially when ad intent is unclear. Consent banners may meet regulatory requirements, but rarely shift perception. In digital spaces, trust is fragile. Perception is reality. Repetition Can Quietly Erode Brand Equity ----------------------------------------- Retargeting systems optimize for frequency and conversion probability, not user sentiment. When a user does not convert, the response escalates: more impressions and the same message across sites and devices. What starts as relevance soon becomes pressure. Visibility may briefly boost recall, but repetition causes frustration. Even strong brands look impatient or intrusive — especially when perception matters more than gains. Retargeting can drive clicks, but it often does so at the expense of the relationship. Retargeting Often Overstates Its True Impact -------------------------------------------- Measurement presents another challenge. Retargeting usually targets users already likely to convert. When they do, the ads get credit, whether or not they influenced the outcome. This inflates metrics while hiding true incremental value. Industry analyses have shown that when retargeting campaigns are paused, conversion rates often decline far less than expected. While retargeting captures attribution, it does not always generate proportional lift. In controlled tests, retargeted and non-retargeted cohorts frequently convert at similar rates, casting doubt on the causal effect. As marketing teams face increasing pressure to demonstrate real business impact, attribution inflation is becoming harder to defend. Privacy and Platform Changes Are Making Retargeting Fragile ----------------------------------------------------------- Beyond perception and results, retargeting infrastructure is weakening. Third-party cookies are vanishing. Consent rates vary. Signal loss is rising. Sustaining retargeting now demands greater technical complexity, legal scrutiny, and data workarounds—often for a lower return on investment. This raises a fundamental question: should brands continue to invest heavily in tactics built for an ecosystem that no longer exists? User Fatigue Is Real—and Measurable ----------------------------------- Even setting privacy aside, retargeting encounters a basic human limitation: attention. Repeated exposure leads to fatigue. Users ignore the ads or start to avoid the brand. In severe cases, annoyance spills over to other channels, dulling effectiveness and brand affinity. When this occurs, retargeting not only loses efficiency—it damages perception. What Comes Next: A Better Model for Engagement ---------------------------------------------- Criticizing retargeting matters only when a better option exists. Marketers should earn attention and encourage explicit participation. Avoid persistent tracking; instead, invest in approaches that directly engage your audience. Contextual relevance instead of behavioral surveillance First-party, consented engagement within owned or trusted environments. Value-driven touchpoints through content, utility, service, and transparency Moment-based interactions that respect boundaries rather than follow users indefinitely Adopt transparent, collaborative approaches that deepen trust and drive measurable, sustainable results — not just more impressions. A Strategic Rethink ------------------- The case against retargeting is not about relevance. It’s about refusing to sacrifice trust, brand health, and resilience for short-term conversions. In a privacy-first world, lead with transparency, deliver clear value, and design experiences that invite active user engagement. Shift resources to strategies that reflect these priorities. The future belongs to brands that are remembered, not repeated. Brands thrive when they cultivate authentic loyalty through meaningful engagement and transparent practices, rather than relying on constant retargeting for visibility.

175.title: Infrastructure for the Connected World
175.content: Infrastructure for the Connected World URL: https://delivr.com/page/799/the-delivr-engagement-suite-with-dynamic-qr-code-generator Title: The Delivr Engagement Suite with Dynamic QR Code Generator URL Source: http://delivr.com/page/799/the-delivr-engagement-suite-with-dynamic-qr-code-generator Markdown Content: From physical touchpoints to digital and spatial experiences, Delivr’s all-in-one Engagement Suite lets brands build, manage, and measure every interaction — across media, products, campaigns, channels, and real-world experiences, leveraging QR codes, links, and pages. Privacy-first and enterprise-ready. As the physical and digital worlds converge, Delivr makes technology invisible, so interactions feel seamless and intentional. Every scan becomes a meaningful signal — building trust and guiding what comes next. From packaging in hand to campaigns in motion, Delivr helps brands create human-centered experiences designed with privacy at their core — seeing people as individuals, not data points. ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Image 1: The Delivr Engagement Suite Diagram Build. Manage. Measure & Optimize. Identify & Authenticate. Trust. ------------------------------------------------------------------ Delivr’s modular tools enable brands to: Why It Matters -------------- Every scan — on packaging, print, retail, or signage — becomes a moment of intent. Delivr allows brands to understand, act on, and optimize engagement while keeping privacy and trust central. Enterprise Assurance -------------------- 99.9% uptime, enterprise reliability GDPR and CCPA compliant Unified governance and analytics for global teams Human-Centered by Design ------------------------ Delivr’s privacy‑first approach means no cookies, no profiling, and no personally identifiable tracking — only aggregated, anonymized metrics that respect user trust while helping brands optimize connection and engagement. BUILD Core℠Dynamic QR Code Generator ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Delivr’s Core ℠ Dynamic QR Code Generator empowers enterprises to deploy dynamic, brand-aligned QR codes that can be updated at any time, eliminating costly reprints and maximizing campaign flexibility. It combines enterprise-grade security, 99.9% uptime, GDPR/CCPA compliance, and rich analytics to ensure reliability and global scalability. The solution also supports custom design and export formats to meet diverse print and digital use cases while providing real-time performance insights. Explore the Dynamic QR Generator. BUILD QR Studio℠ Custom Code Design ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- QR Studio ℠ is a purpose-built creative environment that enables creative teams to design visually striking, fully branded QR codes that go beyond what is offered by generic generators by combining aesthetic control with built-in performance analytics and enterprise-grade compliance. It supports advanced design options — including customizable colors, patterns, logos, Motion QR™, VisualQR™, and ShapeQR™ — while ensuring every code remains fully scannable, GS1-ready, and integrated with real-time analytics for actionable insights. Tailored for high-volume packaging, cross-channel campaigns, and collaborative workflows, QR Studio℠ helps brands turn every scan into a measurable engagement opportunity with governance and workflow controls to support distributed creative teams. Explore Custom Code Design. BUILD Nest℠ Mobile Experiences ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Nest ℠ Mobile Experiences delivers fast-loading, branded mobile pages and profiles that improve user engagement and conversion rates by minimizing friction after QR scans or link taps. Built for enterprise reliability, these mobile-optimized destinations enable use cases from product information and onboarding to corporate profiles and global localized content. This solution enhances customer experiences by aligning performance, design consistency, and real-world outcomes. Explore Mobile Experiences. BUILD Kit℠ Brand System ----------------------------------------------------------------- The Kit ℠ Brand System provides a centralized brand asset and design management system to ensure consistent application of colors, logos, templates, and content across all QR codes, landing pages, and experiences. It helps teams maintain brand integrity, streamline collaboration, and scale campaigns with a single source of truth for creative assets. Explore the Brand System. MANAGE Orchestra℠ Campaign & Team Management ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Orchestra ℠ Campaign & Team Management centralizes all campaign components — links, QR codes, landing pages, NFC tags, and collaboration — into a unified dashboard for better visibility, consistency, and control. It supports role-based permissions, shared workspaces, and real-time analytics to orchestrate complex multi-channel campaigns and align teams enterprise-wide. Built on a privacy-first foundation, it reinforces brand integrity and streamlines cross-departmental workflows. Explore Campaign & Team Management. MANAGE Acuity℠ Intelligent Link Management ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Acuity ℠ Intelligent Link Management is a performance-driven link intelligence platform that goes beyond basic URL shortening by enabling automatic routing based on device, location, or language to deliver the most relevant user experience. It supports branded short links and QR codes with customizable domains and visual styles to strengthen brand trust and engagement. Additionally, Acuity provides real-time insights and dynamic link updates on live campaigns without technical complexity. Explore Intelligent Link Management. MEASURE & OPTIMIZE Worldview℠ Advanced Analytics ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Worldview ℠ Advanced Analytics powers the Measure layer of The Experience Suite layer — turning every interaction into clear, actionable insight, designed privacy-first by design. Worldview ℠ delivers a comprehensive view of audience engagement by converting scan and click interactions into actionable insights like geographic hotspots, time-based trends, and campaign ROI. The analytics platform supports enterprise decision-making by highlighting patterns, user preferences, and channel performance in real time. With privacy-first, zero-knowledge reporting and integration with tools such as Google Analytics® , it enables brands to optimize strategies without compromising trust. Explore Advanced Analytics. IDENTIFY & AUTHENTICATE Imprint℠ Super Simple Serialization ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Imprint ℠ Super Simple Serialization transforms traditional serialization by allowing enterprises to quickly generate large volumes of unique, traceable links or QR codes without the need for APIs or infrastructure, scaling from hundreds to millions of identifiers. Each serialized item delivers real-world engagement data and serves as a unique digital identity. This feature enhances product traceability, authentication, and campaign attribution while maintaining privacy-first compliance. Explore Super Simple Serialization. IDENTIFY & AUTHENTICATE Ring℠ GS1-compliant Digital Link ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Ring ℠ GS1-compliant Digital Link enables enterprises to replace multiple codes on packaging with a single, ISO-compliant QR code that supports point-of-sale checkout, supply chain traceability, and consumer engagement. By embedding GS1 identifiers into structured URLs, it simplifies operations, reduces scanning errors, and enhances visibility across commerce and logistics. This unified approach also unlocks rich digital experiences and real-time insights while maintaining privacy and compliance. Explore GS1-compliant Digital Link. TRUST Hush℠ Privacy-First, Zero-Knowledge ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Hush ℠ Privacy-First Analytics ensures that QR code and connected experience data collection is fully anonymized and compliant with GDPR, CCPA, and global privacy standards, eliminating cookies, tracking IDs, and individual profiling. It provides enterprises with actionable insights — such as timestamps, device type, location, and campaign attribution — without compromising user trust or ethics. This zero-knowledge analytics approach supports brands that prioritize ethical data use while still driving strategic decisions. Explore Privacy-First, Zero-Knowledge Architecture. TRUST Vault℠ Trust Center --------------------------------------------------------------------- Delivr’s Vault ℠ Trust Center serves as the central source of truth for the company’s data protection, compliance, and security documentation, helping enterprises assess risk and trust assurances in one place. It provides transparent access to Delivr’s privacy policies, compliance standards, and security commitments — reinforcing confidence in QR codes, landing pages, and connected digital experiences. By simplifying privacy and compliance insights, Vault℠ enables security, legal, and procurement teams to evaluate and verify protections without deep technical lift. Explore the Trust Center.

176.title: Where every experience feels human.
176.content: Where every experience feels human. Our privacy-first tracking anonymizes IP addresses and avoids storing personal or sensitive data, profiling individuals, or fingerprinting devices. Data Policy OUR VALUES Where every experience feels human. Where every experience feels human. Home>Our Values>The Human-First Product Path The Human-First Product Path Delivr offers dynamic QR experiences that are quick, easy to use, and clear. With more than 15 years of experience and millions of campaigns served, we bring together simple workflows with strong privacy and security features. Our platform is anonymous, GDPR-compliant, and tamper-resistant, so anyone can create and manage campaigns with confidence while keeping audiences safe. By focusing on user-friendly design, privacy, and security, Delivr helps brands build reliable connections that last. ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Experience Is the Last Advantage -------------------------------- As technology advances and products become more alike, the real edge comes from how a product feels — its speed, clarity, accuracy, and human touch. A memorable product experience stays with you longer than any feature. It builds trust and encourages users to develop lasting habits with the platform. For Delivr’s customers, experience means more than just how things look. It’s about how easy it is to use, how clear it is, and how confident users feel at every step, from starting a QR project to viewing results. That sense of simplicity and empowerment sets us apart, the result of years of effort. With over 15 years in the industry, millions of QR campaigns delivered, and a history as a pioneer and leader in dynamic QR technology, we understand what it takes to make every interaction seamless, dependable, and genuinely human. Trust Begins With Respect for the People Who Scan ------------------------------------------------- QR interactions should not come with hidden surveillance. Delivr was founded on a simple belief: the world doesn’t need more tracking — it needs more respect. That’s why every scan through Delivr is anonymized, with no fingerprinting, profiling, or cross-site tracking. Our GDPR-compliant analytics give brands meaningful insights without exposing or exploiting their audiences. Privacy is not a feature toggle; it is built into the foundation of the platform. Security reinforces that trust. Dynamic QR codes must be protected from hijacking, spoofing, and malicious misuse. Delivr secures the entire lifecycle — from creation to routing to destination — ensuring that every scan is genuine and every interaction is safe. For end users, this means QR experiences that are safe, anonymous, and tamper-resistant by design. Empower the Non-Technical ------------------------- For our customers, this means clarity, simplicity, and powerful capability. People feel empowered when things are simple. That’s why Delivr makes advanced privacy and security automatic and built-in, with no settings to configure and no technical expertise needed. Clear controls. No jargon. No gatekeepers. Any user, regardless of skill level, can create, launch, and manage secure, dynamic QR experiences with confidence. When users can easily achieve expert results, the product becomes more than just a tool. It becomes an advantage. Own the Experience Layer ------------------------ Empowering our customers while ensuring their audience stays safe. Delivr offers intuitive workflows supported by a strong, invisible infrastructure: Privacy for audiences that customers never have to think about. Security that detects threats before customers even notice. Performance that feels instant, reliable, and personal. When brands use Delivr, they create QR experiences that are always seamless, safe, and trustworthy for their audience, while gaining clarity, simplicity, and reliability in the product. This is the experience layer for dynamic QR: predictable, respectful, and designed around real human expectations on both sides of the scan. Build What Lasts ---------------- Great products are simple on the outside, strong underneath, and built on solid principles. Delivr’s mission is to empower every customer and protect every audience, bringing clarity, trust, and respect to both sides of every interaction. We build not just features but strong foundations: privacy-first architecture, security-first engineering, and human-first design. This helps brands connect with people naturally and authentically, without ever compromising audience safety or privacy or adding extra work to their team. As long as experience and trust are essential, our advantage will endure. 177.title: How wonderful thingsstarted.
177.content: How wonderful thingsstarted. URL: https://delivr.com/page/813/the-story-of-delivr Title: The Story of Delivr URL Source: http://delivr.com/page/813/the-story-of-delivr Markdown Content: The Years Before Delivr ----------------------- 2001 This story begins the way many great ones do — with a kingdom to challenge, dragons to slay, and a quest ahead of its time. But like all modern myths, it started with people — frustrated by limits, curious about what came next, and convinced the world could work better. Image 1: Once upon a time... Before the age of smartphones, before touch and swipes. Before kingdoms bore the names iOS and Android. Before our lives were packaged, sold, and hoarded like treasure by dragons in disguise. A time when technology promised connection — but left us further apart. The world was quietly weaving itself into something new. The mobile internet was young and fragile.Creators had ideas. Brands had stories. People had voices — but few ways to be heard. It existed behind high walls—narrow corridors of email, chat, and simple games, passed through closed, proprietary gates. Like the internet of 1995, it was tightly guarded. Carriers clung to their walled gardens, wary of the open world beyond, keeping possibility close — and people at a distance. As the mobile web emerged, so did a quieter realization. The more connected the world became, the easier it was to reduce people to data, moments to metrics, and lives to inventory. What began as connection was at risk of becoming extraction — where attention was mined, behavior tracked, and identity quietly turned into fuel for someone else’s business. Before this logic hardened into infrastructure, a different choice felt possible. We imagined something more. At a team meeting in mid 2001, a simple question changed everything — Why can’t people everywhere connect without barriers? That question stayed with the team — not as a product brief, but as a conviction that connection should be liberating, not restrictive. The team didn’t see a future where connection was controlled by gatekeepers or harvested by monetizers. We saw a world waiting to be opened. We believed it would become the first step onto the internet for billions — and that people themselves would be the true magic within it. Not software. Not platforms. People. “ We didn’t set out to build a company. We set out to protect people — and to give them the freedom to connect, create, and share without being treated as a product. ” — David Harper, Founder As the world fractured and the mobile web was built behind closed gates, we chose a different path —to unite people. We set out to build a place where anyone could publish, share, and connect — no code, no gates, no permission required. A platform for creators before “creator” was a title. A place that gave everyone a voice and left no one behind — built for inclusion, creativity, and human expression. And from the very beginning, we made a vow. To protect people, not exploit them. To empower communities, not harvest them. To build with care — and never monetize people. Every quest begins with a belief. Ours was simple. And quietly revolutionary. A Movement Ignites ------------------ 2001 – 2002 In 2001, the team that would become Delivr began building a mobile-first platform for user-generated content and community. In early 2002, Winksite (One World. No Borders.®) launched.Image 2Born in the first days of the mobile revolution, we helped move the Internet from the desktop into everyday life. Each Winksite (a Mobile Site) included built-in features like pages, blog, chat, surveys, forums, profiles, community tools, QR codes, and more. Early adopters didn’t come for the technology. They came because the platform finally let them do something that had been difficult or impossible — tell a story, build a community, or connect with people in the real world — without needing a developer, a budget, or permission. Over the years that followed, more than 250,000 independent creators and grassroots organizations embraced it, using the mobile web as a direct path to their audiences. News and media institutions took notice, including The New York Times, which featured our work in several articles, including " A Library And Cinema In Your Pocket" and "Software Out There. The Internet is entering its Lego era". That early work shaped a belief that still guides Delivr today — when technology fades into the background, human connection comes forward. The Birth of Delivr ------------------- 2008 As smartphones and mobile networks improved, the web began moving into everyday life. Yet the physical world — products, packaging, places, and media — remained largely disconnected from digital experiences. Image 3: Delivr Logo 2008-2013Delivr was built to change that, sparked by a single belief that a scan could open an entirely new world. QR codes existed, but they were static and purely functional. Delivr reimagined QR codes as dynamic, updateable experiences — not because the technology demanded it, but because people did. We introduced a platform for effortless sharing through mobile-friendly URLs and QR codes — designed to be fast, accessible, and instantly updateable. While QR codes already existed, they were static and utilitarian. Delivr reimagined them as dynamic, living experiences. Every scan wasn’t an endpoint — it was a beginning. From the start, Delivr—a Dynamic QR Code Platform— combined accessibility, design, performance, and measurement to create moments that felt seamless and intuitive. Not because it was easy — but because compromise would have meant becoming the very thing we set out to challenge.The physical and digital didn’t just coexist; they enhanced each other. Early creators and organizations adopted our tools, setting the stage for millions of connections across brands and experiences. Image 4 Image 5 Image 6 The Power of Insight -------------------- 2009 – 2012 Before — and alongside — Delivr, we built PercentMobile. PercentMobile was an early mobile analytics service built to make understanding mobile audiences simple, human-centered, and accessible. Designed as a lab for mobile insight, it helped brands including Volkswagen, The New York Times, Coca-Cola, Warner Bros., Burger King, Best Buy, GAP, MTV, and Nike visualize engagement across hundreds of millions of mobile page views on thousands of sites — revealing signals traditional analytics often missed. Image 7You can read more about PercentMobile's approach, Mobile Analytics, Simplified' here. But the real outcome wasn’t data — it was insight. We learned how people moved between physical moments and digital experiences. Patterns emerged. Signals mattered. Context mattered. And in those patterns, a new possibility revealed itself: not just measuring behavior, but bridging the physical and digital worlds to create meaningful engagement. PercentMobile’s focus shifted from tracking mobile behavior to understanding real-world signals. That shift marked a decisive turning point — moving from observation at PercentMobile to action at Delivr, where we began shaping how the physical and digital worlds connect. As Delivr gained momentum, PercentMobile was intentionally wound down. The platform may have ended, but its insights endured — guiding the next chapter of Delivr. A New Kind of Experience ------------------------ 2012 — 2013 Armed with insight from PercentMobile, Delivr shifted from observation to activation. We built tools that transformed offline moments into fast, mobile-first experiences — including intelligent link routing by device, location, and language. Scans became adaptive, responsive, and context-aware. By the end of 2013, Delivr had evolved into a platform capable of supporting large-scale, real-world campaigns — laying the foundation for enterprise adoption. In hundreds of campaigns across retail and packaging, brands found that a scan was no longer an end — it was a bridge. QR codes became gateways to stories, unlocking deeper engagement and measurable connection without compromise. Design as Strategy ------------------ 2014 — 2015 As QR codes moved beyond static print into screens, motion, and live environments, Delivr made design a performance strategy — not an afterthought. Image 8: Delivr Logo 2013-2023With Visual QR ℠, we treated color, form, and composition as functional inputs that improved recognition, scannability, and brand expression. Beauty and performance became inseparable. With Broadcast QR ℠, we engineered QR codes for motion, light, glare, compression, and variable resolution — enabling reliable scanning from video, live broadcasts, and streaming content. QR codes became intuitive, dependable, and enjoyable — across any surface, any screen, any moment. Intelligence Without Intrusion ------------------------------ 2016 From the beginning, privacy shaped how insight was designed. Delivr built measurement around moments, not people — capturing engagement without identity, signal without surveillance. Data was collected with intention, limited by default, and used only to improve the experience itself. This proved a simple truth — analytics don’t need to overreach to be effective. Insight became something brands could rely on — and something people could trust. Smarter, Safer Experiences -------------------------- 2017 — 2018 As QR codes matured from simple engagement tools into infrastructure, Delivr expanded its focus from discovery to trust. Scanning became not just a way to connect — but a way to verify. We introduced serialization, GS1-compliant Digital Links, and TrueMrk® brand protection capabilities that allowed companies to authenticate products, detect counterfeits, and deliver verified experiences at the point of scan. Each interaction confirmed legitimacy while unlocking trusted information, and trust evolved from a promise into a measurable, deployable capability embedded directly into the experience. Those capabilities helped brands prepare for a more transparent, regulated future — one where products carry digital identity, provenance matters, and accountability is built in. Smarter experiences emerged. Safer experiences followed. And trust became not only a function of the platform, but the defining value of the company. Trust Became the Core --------------------- 2019 — 2020 As contactless interactions became essential and QR adoption surged globally, our beliefs sharpened into something more than a mission. They became a responsibility. Every scan became a moment of trust. Every interaction became a chance to prove legitimacy. Trust evolved from a promise into a measurable, deployable capability — embedded directly into the experience. And trust became not only a function of the platform, but the defining value of the company. Privacy Isn’t a Feature ----------------------- 2020 — 2021 We’ve always believed privacy isn’t something you add later. Trust and privacy are not optional — they are the foundation. In an age where personal data is too often treated as the product, Delivr insists that human experience should never be harvested — never traded, never mined, never exploited. Even when the easier path was extraction. Even when the market still rewards shortcuts. Because this is how you build trust. Because this is how you create experiences that feel human. Because this is how you honor the people behind the scan. And as the world became more regulated, more transparent, and more accountable, our commitment only grew stronger. Growing Globally ---------------- 2023 — 2025 Delivr’s story didn’t stay in one place. It grew. As our customers and their campaigns scaled across borders, Delivr evolved into a unified system built for enterprise teams. Creation, campaign management, analytics, governance, and digital identity came together in a single platform — the Delivr Experience Suite.Image 9: Delivr Logo 2023-presentBrands could now design, launch, manage, and optimize offline-to-online journeys without fragmentation — gaining control, consistency, and clarity at scale. We built an international team across the UK and India — a global collaboration that broadened our perspective, sharpened our craft, and deepened our purpose.Different voices.Different lived experiences. One shared belief — technology should disappear, and human connection should remain. The Future We’re Building ------------------------- Today Delivr powers millions of real-world interactions every month — connecting people, products, and stories with purpose, without trading privacy. An approach increasingly shared by the world around us. It’s a belief in what the world can become. Where the physical and digital coexist beautifully. Where experiences are seamless, meaningful, and human. And where trust and privacy aren’t optional — they’re the foundation. We’re not just building tools. We’re crafting the next layer of the world. Once again, the world is weaving itself into something new. The story continues. And the dragons, as always, remain. NEXT A Founder's Letter ------------------ Read more about the values that guide Delivr. They define how we approach the future, design experiences, and choose what to protect. A statement of intent for the One World we are building. Explore Our Values

178.title: Top 10 QR Code Design Best Practices for Maximum Scannability & Engagement.
178.content: Top 10 QR Code Design Best Practices for Maximum Scannability & Engagement. URL: https://delivr.com/faq/1550/top-10-qr-code-design-best-practices-for-maximum-scannability-engagement Title: Top 10 QR Code Design Best Practices for Maximum Scannability & Engagement. URL Source: http://delivr.com/faq/1550/top-10-qr-code-design-best-practices-for-maximum-scannability-engagement Markdown Content: If you're incorporating QR codes into your designs, there are several key best practices that you should keep in mind to ensure their effectiveness. These top 10 guidelines when designing with QR codes can help you create QR codes that are user-friendly, functional, and easy to scan. By following these best practices, you can optimize the use of QR codes in your designs and enhance their overall impact. ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ➀ Size ➁ Contrast Good contrast between the background color of the QR code and the dot pattern color of the QR code is very important when a standard Black & White code is not used. In cases of colorized codes, the QR code should have a dark color with a light background color. Also note that some QR code readers cannot read reversed colors e.g., light color with a dark background color, white with black background color. Good contrast also ensures your QR code is able to be seen by people with visual impairments. ➂ Quality and format Avoid pixelated or blurry QR codes and make sure to print them sharp and clear. Not only do they scan better, but they also look more professional. Depending on the size of your QR code, raster JPG and PNG formats work well for small-scale printing such as business cards and brochures. That said, graphic designers and print shops prefer vector files like EPS and SVG. The advantage of the EPS and SVG formats is that they can be scaled easily to any size — without compromising on quality. This means there won’t be any pixelation or stretching of modules. You can repurpose the same QR code on different materials like billboards, flyers, TV commercials, etc. in different sizes. Learn more about the difference between raster and vector formats here. ➃ Number of QR codes Printing multiple QR codes close to each other or even placing QR Codes next to barcodes can lead to accidentally scanning the wrong one. When possible, use only one QR code per application. This creates focus on the action you want consumers to take. It also prevents phone cameras from jumping focus from code to code. However, there will be times when you need to put more than one QR code on an application. If you must, place QR codes on opposite ends of your layout and/or increase the empty space around each QR code to ensure the consumer can focus their camera on just a single code. Usually, this should only be done when the user is at arm's length of the QR code to allow them the flexibility to move the camera to focus on a single code. This does not work well on large signage at a distance (a billboard, for example). ➄ Margins and quiet zone Margins ------- Regardless of application, the margins around a QR code should be equivalent to at least four data modules (the Delivr default). QR code modules are the dots that make up QR code. Keep in mind that the number of modules can differ among QR codes that have the same overall dimensions. Quiet zone ---------- The quiet zone is the space surrounding the QR code that separates the QR code from other designs. QR codes need a quiet zone so that scanners can detect what images are QR code pixels and which aren’t. Without the quiet zone, it’s likely that the QR code will not work. ➅ Call-to-action (CTA) A QR code on its own is not a call-to-action. A simple directive such as "Open Camera & Scan Code" near the code can incentivize consumers to reach for their phones. It is recommended that you clearly inform the consumer what content they will receive if they scan your code. ➆ Placement and timing Keep QR codes within reach -------------------------- Placing your QR code too high up, too low on the ground, or too far away means your customers will have a hard time reaching to scan the code. Place QR codes within appropriate reach for maximum accessibility. Would you crouch down or use a ladder just to scan a QR code? Placing QR codes at eye-level is recommended. Designers will want to avoid placing QR codes at the top third of their creative if the screens are installed with the midline above eye-level. Show the QR code for as long as possible ---------------------------------------- Consider the time it takes for a person to walk on a sidewalk or public hallway, see content on digital signage, notice the QR code, decide they want to scan it, and successfully scan it. Many out of home ads appear for 10 seconds or less, so even if the creative is dynamic, designers should present the QR code for the full length of the slot to maximize the opportunity for successful conversion. ➇ Printing surfaces Creativity can go a long way whether printing on paper, glass, aluminum or plastic. That said, be wary of choosing material that reflects too much light or distorts the image and competes with the scannability of your QR code. Uneven surfaces that are ribbed may disfigure the code and printing in between magazine folds may section off parts of the code so it’s harder to view and scan the full QR code image. Use your best judgement and make sure to test your QR code to confirm it’s scannable. ➈ Check your content Imagine your customers’ dismay when they take the time to scan your QR code just to find a broken link or outdated information. This is a mishap we’ve frequently seen that can be easily avoided. To keep from being stuck with a QR code that you can’t ever update, make sure to create a Dynamic QR Code. With this type of code, you can change the destination URL or content any time even after you’ve already printed and distributed your marketing material. ➉ Always test your QR code Verify your QR code will scan at the size it will be printed or displayed before using it. Apple leads other devices. With the release of iOS 14.4, Apple devices became able to read smaller QR codes as well as codes wrapped around objects when the native camera app is used for scanning. Apple doesn’t specify the minimum size that can be read, but it shows a continued trend toward improved native compatibility. Last Updated 23 Nov 2025 Documentation

Our privacy-first tracking anonymizes IP addresses and avoids storing personal or sensitive data, profiling individuals, or fingerprinting devices. Data Policy Home>Legal>Trademark Notice Trademark Notice Delivr's trademarks are valuable assets that we need to protect. Therefore, we ask that you use our trademarks properly in accordance with the following guidelines. -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- These guidelines apply to the use of all Delivr trademarks in advertisements, marketing materials, labels, press releases, product literature, websites, catalogs, brochures, training materials, user documentation, packaging, and all other instances in which the trademarks are used. For Questions Regarding these Guidelines ---------------------------------------- The following guidelines are examples of how Delivr trademarks should and should not be used. If you have any questions concerning the use of these guidelines, please contact our Marketing Department at 631.750.2785. Permissible Use --------------- You may generally use Delivr trademarks to refer to Delivr's products and services. For example, an authorized subscriber can issue a press release stating that they used Delivr®-based technology solutions. Brands and marketing companies can include labels on their campaigns that says "Powered by Delivr®" or "Enabled by Delivr®". Prohibited Use -------------- You may not use Delivr trademarks in a manner which could cause confusion as to Delivr sponsorship, affiliation, or endorsement. You may not use any Delivr trademark or product name in combination with the trademark or product name of a third party. Trademark Usage --------------- Use the appropriate trademark attribution (® or ™) for each trademark. When text is in all caps, another distinctive font such as italics may be used. Trademarks are used as adjectives. Do not use trademarks as nouns, verbs, or in the possessive. You must use a generic term with the trademark the first time the trademark appears in text and as often as possible after that. Improper: You can create TrueMrks® with Delivr®. Correct: TrueMrk® makes it simple to provide product authenticity, provenance and consumer engagement while identifying incidents of counterfeiting and gray market diversion. Improper: Delivr®s benefits include real-time reporting. Correct: Real-time reporting is one of the benefits of the Delivr® platform. Correct Trademark Use of Company Name ------------------------------------- Delivr Corporation, is the name of our company and should not be used with a trademark symbol. List of Trademarks ------------------ Delivr® Wonderful things start here.℠ The Platform for Wonderful Things.℠ Welcome to the Platform for Wonderful Things.℠ Engagement Suite℠ Infrastructure for the connected world.℠ Infrastructure for the Connected World ℠ QR infrastructure for the connected world.℠ QR Infrastructure for the Connected World ℠ One World.℠ One World. No Borders.℠ Always Open® TrueMrk® AMP® Super Simple Serialization℠ thin.gs℠ Core℠ Dynamic QR Code Generator QR Studio℠ Custom Code Design Acuity℠ Intelligent Link Routing Imprint℠ Super Simple Serialization Worldview℠ Advanced Analytics Orchestra℠ Campaign & Team Management Kit℠ Brand System Nest℠ Mobile Experiences Ring℠ GS1-compliant Digital Link Hush℠ Privacy-First, Zero-Knowledge Vault℠ Trust Center Forge℠ Content Management Doorways.℠ Smart Book℠ Motion QR℠ MotionQR℠ BroadcastQR℠ Broadcast QR℠ Logistic QR℠ LogisticQR℠ Privacy QR℠ PrivacyQR℠ Secure QR℠ SecureQR℠ Security QR℠ SecurityQR℠ Shape QR℠ ShapeQR℠ Vision QR℠ VisionQR℠ Spatial QR℠ SpatialQR℠ Assigning Ownership of Trademarks --------------------------------- The following text should be included in all materials referring to any Delivr trademark: Delivr® is a registered trademark of Delivr Corp. The following text should be included in all materials referring to TrueMrk: TrueMrk® is a registered trademark of Delivr Corp. Corporate Identity Guidelines ----------------------------- "Delivr" takes an initial capital letter in text and copy. It can appear in all capital letters in a headline when the other words are also all in capital letters. Improper: DELIVR Corporation Correct: Delivr Corporation Permissible: DELIVR® ENABLES ADVANCED CAMPAIGN MANAGEMENT AND TRACKING. 181.title: TrueMrk Brand Protection: Consumer Engagement
181.description: Markdown export of Delivr TrueMrk brand protection page
181.content: TrueMrk Brand Protection: Consumer Engagement URL Source: http://delivr.com/page/679/truemrk-brand-protection-consumer-engagement Markdown Content: TrueMrk® by Delivr makes it simple to provide product serialization, authenticity, provenance, and consumer engagement while identifying counterfeiting and gray market diversion incidents. Engage customers, collect real-time intelligence, and prove authenticity with a single QR code. TrueMrk is available with enterprise plans. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ TrueMrk combines brand protection and consumer engagement into a single, intuitive experience. ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- TrueMrk® by Delivr helps brands deliver assurance about product authenticity and provenance to consumers while using the power of deep data analytics and advanced algorithms to help combat fraud. TrueMrk increases consumer trust and embraces Image 1: TrueMrkconsumer needs for product information while protecting and enhancing your brand. For brands, implementing a brand protection and marketing program with TrueMrk is simple. ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Two billion consumers with a smartphone want tools to find your authentic products. 1. TrueMrk is an easy to integrate solution that arms consumers in the battle against product fraud while satisfying their demand for information. 2. TrueMrk enables frictionless product serialization with plug & play simplicity, giving every product a unique identity in the connected world. 3. TrueMrk is easy to implement, easy to use – and requires no infrastructure or software to maintain. TrueMrk uses the features that are already present in smartphones. There is no need for any special equipment, ink, paper, or applications, which greatly lowers the expenses and time required to deploy a program. Once applied, TrueMrk allows consumers and supply chain inspectors to verify the authenticity of a product with a simple scan using their smartphone. By harvesting data during the scan process and applying proprietary algorithms, TrueMrk identifies unusual or fraudulent activity incidents. For consumers, verifying the safety of a product with TrueMrk is simple. ------------------------------------------------------------------------ Once applied to a product or package, consumers can verify the authenticity of a product and view product information with a smartphone scan. Empowering consumers to authenticate products captures more data than other brand protection methods. TrueMrk turns ordinary packaging into safer, smarter packaging. Image 2: Metrics Leverage high-value connections for Marketing & CRM. ---------------------------------------------------- Consumers who use TrueMrk to identify authentic products are more motivated, loyal, and visible than a typical consumer. Authentication opens a channel for brands to converse with consumers at whatever moment suits their strategies — to manage product after-care, recalls, reward repeat business, introduce additional products, and deliver targeted promotions — the choice is yours. Brand Protection ---------------- Enables product verification Informs consumers of product origin Captures data and provides insights Establishes traceability at the unit level Identifies incidents of counterfeiting Recognizes patterns of grey market diversion Protects your brand Consumer Engagement ------------------- Promotes brand confidence Activates and engages consumers Provides product information and videos Delivers marketing programs Displays an offer or promotion Facilitates product reorder Encourages warranty registrations Offers a "Call Now" feature TrueMrk can be integrated into product labels and packaging, or applied as a separate security label. ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- TrueMrk® adds unit-level serialization, multi-level security, and tracking to product labels and packaging. At the heart of each TrueMrk label is an intelligent, secure QR Code which contains a public URL, unique fingerprints matched to a manufacturer's database, covert & overt serialization, and other product information/supply chain data. TrueMrk is a versatile anti-counterfeiting approach that can be combined with additional anti-counterfeiting printing technologies. It involves printing security labels on tamper-resistant and tamper-evident substrates to enhance product security. Example TrueMrk security label. ------------------------------- The separate security label below features a user experience incorporating a 6-digit verification code. The verification code can be visible or hidden beneath a scratch-off layer. The actual design would be per the client’s (the brand’s) requirements. Image 3: Example Securty Label ACTUAL LABEL SIZE: 45MM WIDTH x 15MM HEIGHT, 12MM x 12MM CODE The QR code and verification pin shown above is not activated. When activated and scanned, it would lead to a demo product authentication page, showcasing one of several user experiences offered by the TrueMrk service. When scanned repeatedly and with different browsers or devices, the product will eventually be flagged as a 'suspect' when scan attempts exceed a threshold. Thresholds can be defined per batch of labels by the user (brand). REQUEST DEMO Image 4: Experience Stack Several brand protection experiences are available. --------------------------------------------------- 1. Product Verification w/ 6-Digit Code followed by a Landing Page (w/ PIN) 2. Landing Page w/ Product Verification Overlay (w/ PIN) 3. Activity-based Product Guidance followed by a Landing Page (NO PIN) 4. Landing Page w/ Product Guidance Overlay (NO PIN) 5. Direct to Landing Page (NO PIN) Product authentication is followed by consumer engagement.Image 5: Consumer Engagement Stack --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- With just a few clicks and in a matter of minutes, brand owners can easily create mobile-friendly landing pages for their marketing campaigns without the need for coding or developers. This allows for quick development and delivery of marketing content and smart labels. The marketing content can include videos, product information, instructions, ingredients, promotions, offers, and lead capture. Additionally, brand owners can provide a link to their website or programs if they prefer. Clear and simple reports are designed for people, not specialists, allowing anyone to turn insights into action. ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- After every scanning event, data is generated which can be viewed instantly on Delivr's online portal. This data can be used to identify instances of counterfeiting and grey market diversion on a per unit or aggregate basis. Brands can access this real-time business intelligence to observe patterns and take necessary measures to safeguard their brand. About Delivr ------------ Delivr is a marketing technology platform thousands of businesses use to drive audiences from print to digital experiences. Established in 2008, Delivr has developed software to track and visualize consumer engagement with printed media, labels, and packaging. Delivr has extended its marketing, consumer engagement, and analytics platform to authenticate products and identify counterfeiting and gray market diversion incidents. Delivr is a licensed DENSO Partner.

Data Policy Home>Documentation>Types of account users & roles. Delivr Types of account users & roles. Types of account users & roles. Delivr simplifies team collaboration by providing customized access to campaigns, groups, and account features. Whether working with colleagues, contractors, or clients, understanding the roles, access levels, and organizational tools allows everyone to contribute effectively while safeguarding your data. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ Account Users & Roles Account Owner / Admin ------------------------- The primary owner of the account with full control over all settings, campaigns, and access permissions. Can grant or remove access for others. Can configure high-level settings (with Delivr Support assistance). Only one Admin per account. Contributors ---------------- Team members with broad creative and management permissions across the account (when given account-level scope). Can create, edit, and manage campaigns. Can handle links, QR codes, groups, landing pages, profiles, and reports. Can bulk import/export campaigns. Cannot delete campaigns—only the admin has that permission. Editors ----------- Focused access to specific campaigns or groups. Can edit and manage campaigns within their assigned scope. Can manage links, download QR codes, edit landing pages, and pause campaigns. Cannot see or edit anything outside their assigned group(s) or campaign(s). Analysts ------------ Report-focused role for reviewing data and insights. Can view reports. Can download QR codes. Access can be granted at the account, group, or campaign level. Access Levels at a Glance ----------------------------- When you invite others to collaborate on your Delivr account, you can set how much access they have and to which campaigns or groups. Account Scope ----------------- Full visibility and editing rights for all campaigns and groups in the account. Group Scope --------------- Access is limited to campaigns within the selected group(s). Campaign Scope ------------------ Access is limited to individual campaigns you choose to share. Steps to add a Team Member. Adding new collaborators to your Delivr account is simple and lets your team share campaign management responsibilities. ➀ Go to Account Settings ---------------------- From the top navigation bar, click Account. ➁ Select “Grant Access" --------------------- Choose the level of access: Account, Group, or Campaign. ➂ Enter Details ------------- Type the person’s Delivr username or email address. ➃ Send Invite ----------- An email invitation will be sent to the user. If they decline, they will not appear in your list of granted users. ➄ Confirm Access -------------- Once accepted, the team member will have the permissions you assigned. Collaboration Best Practices Contributor Dashboard Message. You can display a custom message on your Contributors' dashboards. This can include project updates, instructions, deadlines, or tips to keep collaboration running smoothly. Use Groups for Team Projects. Group campaigns related to the same project or department for easier team management. Label & Tag for Clarity . Adding clear labels and tags helps all collaborators quickly find and manage campaigns. Star Priority Campaigns. Use stars to keep high-priority items visible to your team. Add UTM Parameters. Ensure campaigns have tracking data for performance analysis across the team. Limit Scope. Thoughtfully: Grant only the minimum necessary access to keep your account secure.
