Our privacy-first tracking anonymizes IP addresses and avoids storing personal or sensitive data, profiling individuals, or fingerprinting devices. Data Policy Home>Documentation>Types of campaigns and mobile experiences. Delivr Types of campaigns and mobile experiences. Types of campaigns and mobile experiences. At Delivr, every campaign falls into one of three main types— URL , Page , or Profile . Each type serves a different purpose and offers unique advantages, allowing you to choose the best fit for your audience and goals. ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 1. URL Campaigns ---------------- A URL Campaign sends users directly to a web address when they scan your QR code. URL Campaigns are Ideal for: -------------------------------- Linking to an existing website, online store, video, form, or document. Driving traffic to pages you already manage outside Delivr. Key Features of URL-based Campaigns: ------------------------------------ Fast and direct—perfect for time-sensitive campaigns. Works seamlessly with any secure (HTTPS) link. Destination can be updated at any time without reprinting your QR code. 2. Page Campaigns ----------------- A Page Campaign opens a mobile-optimized landing page hosted within Delivr. Page Campaigns are ideal for: ----------------------------- Creating focused, single-purpose experiences (event info, product highlights, promotions). Delivering content that looks good on any device without coding or web hosting. Key Features of Pages: ---------------------- Simple, built-in page editor for adding text, images, buttons, and calls to action. No external hosting required—everything is managed inside Delivr. Fully trackable with scan analytics and engagement data. 3. Profile Campaigns -------------------- A Profile Campaign acts like a digital business card or link hub, showing multiple links and resources in one place. Profile Campaigns are ideal for: -------------------------------- Sharing all your key links (website, social media, videos, contact info) in one QR code. Personal branding, team portfolios, or influencer link-in-bio style pages. Key Features of Profiles: ------------------------- Organize and customize multiple links with headers and icons. Add personal or brand details for context and recognition. Update or reorder links anytime—perfect for evolving campaigns. Switching Campaign Types ------------------------ You can change an existing campaign from one type to another, i.e., URL → Page , Page → Profile , or vice versa without losing analytics or reprinting your QR code. This flexibility means your QR codes stay relevant and up to date as your needs change. 184.title: Types of QR codes & barcodes.
184.content: Types of QR codes & barcodes. URL: https://delivr.com/faq/1604/types-of-qr-codes-barcodes Title: Types of QR codes & barcodes. URL Source: http://delivr.com/faq/1604/types-of-qr-codes-barcodes Markdown Content: Not all QR codes are the same—each type serves a different purpose and provides unique advantages. On Delivr, you can select the QR code type that best aligns with your campaign goals, tracking needs, and long-term plans. Understanding these differences will help you maximize our platform and your marketing efforts. ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- The Two Main Types of QR Codes in Delivr. 1. Dynamic QR Codes (Trackable & Editable) ---------------------------------------------- Best for marketing campaigns, product packaging, labels, DOOH, or any situation where you may need to update the destination or measure engagement. Key Features Change the destination URL at any time without reprinting the QR code. Track scans in real-time (location, time, device type, etc.). Add campaign names, notes, and tags to make organization easier. Benefits .Flexible, measurable, and future-proof. Design. Standard & Custom Design 2. Static QR Codes (Fixed & Non-Editable) -------------------------------------------- Best for permanent uses where the destination will never change. Key Features Directly encodes the destination into the QR code. No tracking or analytics available. Cannot be updated after creation. Benefits .Simple, cost-free, and doesn’t require an ongoing subscription. Design. Standard & Custom Design Choosing the Right Type for Your Needs ------------------------------------------ If You Need To… Choose Track scans and engagement Dynamic Update the destination after printing Dynamic Use once with no changes or tracking Static Link to content that will never change Static Run time-sensitive marketing campaigns Dynamic How to Select a QR Code Type in Delivr ------------------------------------------ When creating a new campaign , you’ll be prompted to choose between Dynamic and Static . You can also edit an existing Dynamic campaign’s destination anytime from your dashboard—but Static codes cannot be changed once generated. Pro Tip ----------- If in doubt, start with Dynamic QR Codes . Even if you don’t need analytics today, having the flexibility to update your destination later can save you time, money, and reprints. Barcode Generator The Barcode Generator produces static barcodes—once created, they cannot be edited, managed, or tracked. You can choose from 22 barcode types, including: QR Code Family ------------------ QR Code A widely used 2D barcode that stores large amounts of data, scannable from any angle. Micro QR Code A smaller, simplified version of the QR Code designed for tight spaces and lower data capacity. Other 2D Matrix Codes ------------------------- Not part of the QR standard but similar in appearance/use. Aztec Code A 2D barcode is often used in tickets and transportation, readable even when printed small or with poor contrast. Data Matrix A compact 2D barcode ideal for marking small products, widely used in manufacturing and healthcare. Grid Matrix A 2D barcode type with a grid-based pattern optimized for compact printing and quick reading. A 2D barcode made of dots, designed for high-speed printing on packaging lines. A stacked linear barcode that can hold large amounts of text or data, often used in identification cards. PDF417 Truncated A shortened version of PDF417 with less redundancy to save space. 1D Linear Barcodes ---------------------- Traditional barcodes with lines and spaces. Channel Code A continuous 1D barcode symbology that supports multiple character sets and variable lengths. A simple numeric barcode often used in libraries, healthcare, and logistics. A stacked linear barcode that combines Code 128 with a multi-row format for more data capacity. Code 11 A numeric-only barcode used mainly in telecommunications. Code 128 A high-density alphanumeric barcode is widely used in shipping and product labeling. Code 39 A versatile alphanumeric barcode is often used in inventory and industrial applications. Code 39+ An extended version of Code 39 that supports the full ASCII character set. A short 8-digit European Article Number for small retail packages. A 13-digit European Article Number used globally for retail products. A 14-digit code used for grouping items in logistics and shipping. A standardized identifier for books, encoded as an EAN barcode. A 12-digit Universal Product Code is used mainly in North American retail. UPC-A + Check Digit A UPC-A barcode with an explicit check digit printed for verification. A compressed version of UPC-A is used on small product packaging. Last Updated 27 Jan 2026 Documentation

Our privacy-first tracking anonymizes IP addresses and avoids storing personal or sensitive data, profiling individuals, or fingerprinting devices. Data Policy Home>Documentation>Understanding & Implementing Super Simple Serialization. Delivr Understanding & Implementing Super Simple Serialization. Understanding & Implementing Super Simple Serialization. Super Simple Serialization gives each item, campaign, or link a unique, trackable ID—no APIs, coding, or extra infrastructure needed. By adding a unique ID to a Delivr short URL, you can instantly generate and track hundreds, thousands, or even millions of serialized links or QR codes. -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Steps to add Super Simple Serialization to a Campaign. ➀ Create Your Base Campaign ----------------------------- Sign in to Delivr, shorten your destination URL, and copy the resulting Experience URL (managed Short URL) link (e.g., https://delivr.com/abc123). ➁ Generate Serialized URLs ---------------------------- Append unique IDs to your short URL in a spreadsheet, e.g., https://delivr.com/abc123/0001, https://delivr.com/abc123/0002. Use simple IDs or more complex structured formats, e.g., https://delivr.com/abc123/[storeID] ➂ Provide the Serialized List to Your Printer or Encoder ---------------------------------------------------------- Your print provider can variably print QR codes from the spreadsheet. NFC tag suppliers can encode the same list directly into tags. ➃ Deploy Your Materials ------------------------- Each scan or tap is logged by Delivr. The unique ID is passed to your destination URL, e.g., https://yourdomain.com/?trace 0001 ➄ Process the IDs in Your Application (Optional) -------------------------------------------------- Your website can simply ignore the ID, or your development team can use it to deliver different experiences, workflows, and log data Key Benefits of Super Simple Serialization. ----------------------------------------------- Fast setup. No coding or integrations required. Scalable .Works for small or mass deployments. Granular tracking. Identify engagement down to a specific item or location. Popular Use Cases --------------------- Here are just a few ways customers use Super Simple Serialization: 1. Physical Products . Automatically pre-fill product registration forms when customers scan a QR code. No more typing in serial numbers. 2. Digital & Print Marketing . Deliver personalized landing pages for each recipient, bridging mass marketing with one-to-one engagement. 3. Smart Objects . Assign unique identities to packaging, posters, POS materials, parts, or equipment—without creating thousands of separate campaigns. 4. Inventory Tracking . Give every item a “digital twin” for visibility across regions, detect unauthorized distribution, and manage localized offers. 5. Influencer & Partner Attribution . Serialize links so you can track exactly which partner or influencer drove engagement. 6. Coupons & Promotions . Manage single-use codes that expire after redemption. No Limits ------------- Because you generate serialized links yourself, you can create as many as you need—hundreds, thousands, or millions—on demand. Pro Tips Plan space for QR codes in your design. Use printers capable of variable data printing. Maintain consistent ID structures for easy tracking. 186.title: Understanding QR codes, technology, and trust with Delivr.
186.content: Understanding QR codes, technology, and trust with Delivr. URL: https://delivr.com/faq/1601/understanding-qr-codes-technology-and-trust-with-delivr Title: Understanding QR codes, technology, and trust with Delivr. URL Source: http://delivr.com/faq/1601/understanding-qr-codes-technology-and-trust-with-delivr Markdown Content: What is a QR Code? ---------------------- A QR code (Quick Response code) is a two-dimensional barcode that stores data—such as URLs, payment details, or contact information—and can be quickly scanned with a smartphone camera. Created by DENSO WAVE in 1994, QR codes can hold much more data than traditional barcodes and can be read from any angle, even if partially damaged, thanks to error correction, alignment patterns, and a structured grid of modules. Important components include position and alignment markers, timing patterns, format and version info, data modules, error correction, and a quiet zone for accurate scanning. QR codes are common for payments, marketing, packaging, event tickets, and secure file access. Delivr, licensed by DENSO, generates ISO-compliant QR codes using both proprietary and patented technology, ensuring high reliability, security, and privacy. Delivr’s platform supports advanced features, including encrypted connections, threat detection, dynamic redirects, and privacy-first deployments. With over 15 years of experience and a 99.9% uptime record, Delivr powers millions of QR code connections worldwide, offering trusted, enterprise-grade solutions for marketing, commerce, and secure interactions. Image 1: Structure of a QR Code Decoding the Structure of a QR Code. ---------------------------------------- A QR code consists of small black-and-white squares called modules , each representing binary data (0s and 1s). Their arrangement enables scanners to decode the information accurately, no matter the code’s orientation. Each part of this grid has a specific function: Position Markers . Large squares in three corners that help scanners locate and correctly read the code from any angle. Alignment Markers . Smaller squares that compensate for distortion, ensuring accurate scanning even on curved or uneven surfaces. Timing Patterns . Alternating black-and-white lines between position markers that define the grid’s size and help the scanner correctly map each module. Format Information . Encoded near the position markers, this stores details such as the error correction level (L, M, Q, H) and the mask pattern applied to the data modules. It helps the scanner interpret the QR code’s layout before decoding the actual data. Version Information . Version Information indicates the QR code’s version number, which determines its size and data capacity. It ensures the scanner knows exactly how to map and read the modules. Data Modules . The area containing the actual encoded information, which can range from plain text and URLs to complex data like payment instructions. Error Correction (ECC) . Built-in redundancy that allows the code to remain readable even if parts are damaged, obscured, or missing. Quiet Zone. The Quiet Zone is the blank margin surrounding the QR code, typically at least four modules wide on all sides. It acts as a buffer, separating the code from any surrounding text, graphics, or background patterns, so scanners can clearly identify where the QR code starts and ends. Without a proper quiet zone, scanning reliability drops significantly. How do QR codes work? ------------------------- When you scan a QR code, your phone’s camera captures the image, and the decoding software interprets the pattern of black-and-white modules. In seconds, it can open a website, process a payment, add a contact, or perform other predefined actions. 1. Encoding the Data A QR code starts with information you want to share (text, a URL, payment details, etc.). This data is converted into binary (0s and 1s). The binary is arranged into modules —tiny black or white squares in a specific pattern according to the QR code standard. Error correction (ECC) is added so that the code remains readable even if partially damaged. A mask pattern is applied to balance the distribution of black and white modules, improving scanning reliability. 2. Printing or Displaying the Code The finished grid is rendered as a visual pattern and placed with a quiet zone around it. QR codes can be printed, displayed on screens, or embedded in documents. 3. Scanning the Code A camera or QR scanner captures the image. The scanner first detects the position markers to locate the code and determine its orientation. Alignment markers and timing patterns help map out the module grid precisely. 4. Decoding the Data The scanner reads the format information (error correction level + mask pattern) and, if present, the version information . The mask is removed to restore the original binary pattern. The binary sequence is interpreted according to the encoding mode (numeric, alphanumeric, byte, Kanji, etc.) to reconstruct the original data. 5. Using the Information If the data is a URL, the device can open it in a browser. If it’s payment data, contact details, or other structured information, the device’s software can process it accordingly. Why are QR codes so reliable? --------------------------------- QR codes are designed to be fast, flexible, and damage-resistant : 360° readability : Scans from any direction Error correction : Up to 30% of the code can be damaged and still work (thanks to Reed-Solomon error correction ) Works on curved surfaces : Thanks to alignment patterns Supports multiple languages Compact but powerful : Can store 200x more data than a traditional barcode Who owns the rights to QR codes? ------------------------------------ DENSO WAVE holds the patent for the QR code , but its specifications are open and free to use worldwide. Delivr is proud to be one of a handful of companies globally licensed by DENSO to generate ISO-compliant QR codes , ensuring accuracy, durability, and security at every scan. What are QR codes used for? ------------------------------- QR codes bridge the gap between physical and digital. Common use cases include: Payments (Scan & Pay) Marketing & Advertising Product Packaging (How-to videos, ingredients, AR) Event Tickets & Check-ins Touchless Menus Business Cards & Networking Loyalty Programs Secure File Access Logistics & Inventory The platform you use to generate your QR codes is as important as the reason you generate them. --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- There are three variables you can’t control in the QR world: 1. The device being used 2. The operating system 3. The scanner app But you can control the code itself. ---------------------------------------- Delivr helps you do just that—with precision, performance, and privacy. Delivr has been a leader in QR code technology since 2008, with over 15 years of proven reliability and a 99.9% uptime track record. Delivr generates ISO-compliant QR codes and are officially licensed by DENSO—the inventor of the QR code. Delivr’s platform offers patented and proprietary solutions, built for secure, privacy-first deployments. Delivr is trusted by the world’s leading brands that prioritize analytics, compliance, and consumer trust. We've supported thousands of businesses and over a million creators, powering more than 100 million direct connections across 250+ countries, territories, and regions, in over 200 languages and dialects. Delivr also delivers the highest-performing landing pages in the industry—designed for speed, conversion, and seamless user experience. How Delivr secures your QR codes. ------------------------------------- QR codes are inherently secure, but can be misused by attackers if you're not cautious. That's why source and technology matter . Encrypted Connections (HTTPS, AES-256) keep data safe during scans. Threat Detection blocks malicious links and suspicious scanning patterns. Access Controls , such as 2FA and SSO, prevent unauthorized use. Dynamic Redirects let you update or disable QR codes at any time. Privacy by Design ensures no personal data is collected or exposed. Compliance with laws like GDPR and CCPA protects user rights. Brand Protection Tools help detect fakes and ensure product authenticity. This ensures your QR codes are both trusted and tamper-resistant , making them safe for enterprise, government, and consumer use.

Data Policy Home>Documentation>Understanding the Brand Kit. Delivr Understanding the Brand Kit. Understanding the Brand Kit. The Brand Kit organizes and manages your brand’s visual identity across all campaigns, QR codes, landing pages, and profiles. You can upload logos, choose brand colors, and apply consistent styling elements to keep a unified look across all assets. To access it, click the Brand link at the top of the page (to the right of Archive). You will see five tabs; each covers a different aspect of your brand's appearance within Delivr. ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 1. Design This tab controls the design defaults for your campaign experiences. Upload your logo, define brand colors, and manage key design elements. Here you can define: Interstitial Page Design Apple Touch Icon Device GPS Prompt Screen QR Code Casing: Uppercase or Lowercase QR Code Default Color Default Page Link Color Don’t forget to click Save All Design Settings when you're done. 2. Pages Apply consistent branding across all your Pages . Logo (universal logo for top of all pages) Upload logo (Max 500kB, 1080px wide recommended) Footer Customization Footer text Background & Text Color Social Media Icons Include links to: LinkedIn, Facebook, X (Twitter), Instagram WhatsApp, Line, Discord YouTube, TikTok, Twitch Calendar and more Icon Color Settings Use brand colors or define custom colors for social icons. Click SAVE to confirm changes. 3. Profiles Apply consistent branding across all your Profile pages . Organization Name & Logo Logo Upload (Max 500kB, 1080px recommended) Optionally, add a URL link to the logo Main Copy & Footer Custom messaging Footer customization with color options Social Media & Icon Colors Same categories and customization as in the Pages tab. Click SAVE to confirm changes. 4. QR Logos Upload and manage logos used within QR code templates . These logos are available for use within the QR Code Designer. 5. QR Templates Access and manage template designs for QR codes : Create consistent QR code visuals for different campaigns Templates can include pre-set colors, frames, logos, and more. Templates saved here can be reused across various campaigns for speed and brand consistency. 188.title: Understanding the campaign report.
188.content: Understanding the campaign report. URL: https://delivr.com/faq/1596/understanding-the-campaign-report Title: Understanding the campaign report. URL Source: http://delivr.com/faq/1596/understanding-the-campaign-report Markdown Content: Want to see how your campaign is performing? Delivr’s Campaign Report provides detailed analytics to help you understand engagement, audience behavior, acquisition sources, and more — all in one place. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Steps to access a Campaign Report. ➀ Locate the Campaign ----------------------- From your Dashboard, find the campaign you want to review. ➁ View Report --------------- Click the VIEW REPORT button for that campaign to access its analytics. Note: Report time zone is set to your Account Time Zone . You can change this by clicking Change in the top-right corner of the report view. Report Summary The Summary section provides a high-level snapshot of how your campaign has performed over the selected time period. Key Metrics Include: ------------------------ Connections. Number of scans or clicks your campaign received. Cost per Connection. Your average cost per connection at Delivr. Top Countries & Cities. Geographic hotspots where your audience engaged most. Private Connections. Represents traffic from protected, anonymized, or privacy-focused sources such as VPNs or private browsers. Top Languages. Displays the primary languages set on users’ devices or browsers during engagement. Response Rate. Indicates user engagement as a percentage of total impressions—where estimated audience size is set. Recent Connections. List of recent connections. Campaign Parameters. Shows the Campaign Parameters/UTMs added to your campaigns in the Delivr Campaign URL Builder. This section is ideal for quickly assessing overall reach, effectiveness, and geographic distribution. Detailed Metrics & Charts ----------------------------- Dive deeper into campaign performance with a full suite of visual analytics. This section includes charts and key metrics that provide insights into user behavior, traffic sources, device types, engagement patterns, and more, helping you understand not just how many connected, but how and when they did. Detailed Metrics & Charts include: Audience Heatmap Locations Trends Time Dayparts Engagement Methods Acquisition Operating System Language Benchmarking Serialization People First Audience Heatmap Visualizes where your audience engaged your campaigns by plotting connections on a geographic map over a selected time period. Highlights -------------- The heatmap is followed by a list of interactions with timestamp , device type (e.g., desktop or mobile), action (scan or click), and method (e.g., IP detection). Includes geolocation accuracy and location data (city, region, country). Shows Trace ID for serialization and deeper tracking or troubleshooting. You can export all connections from the selected time frame for deeper analysis or archiving. Location The Location tab shows where your campaign traffic is coming from — eographically. Highlights -------------- Lists countries, states/provinces and cities where connections occurred (e.g., United Kingdom , London ). Helps identify regional interest or concentration of engagement. Useful for tailoring localized messaging or planning region-specific campaigns. Trends The Trends tab gives you insight into which days saw the most engagement with your campaign. Use these insights to fine-tune your future campaigns around high-traffic days . Highlights -------------- Shows the busiest single day of activity (e.g., Friday, Jul 25, 2025 ). Identifies the most active day of the week during the selected time frame (e.g., Friday ). Great for spotting weekly patterns and peak audience behavior. When viewing an All-Time report, an additional Trend section appears — highlighting long-term momentum across the full campaign lifecycle, with up to three years of historical data. Growth Trends ----------------- When viewing an All-Time report, an additional Trend section appears — highlighting long-term momentum across the full campaign lifecycle, with up to three years of historical data.This reporting module displays campaign performance over time with visuals showing connections by month and by year. Easily identify growth, seasonal patterns, or declines to guide strategic decisions and optimize future campaigns. Time The Time tab helps you understand when your audience is most active throughout the day. This is especially useful for optimizing campaign timing or planning future outreach. Highlights -------------- Displays key activity windows, such as: Most Active Time Period (e.g., Morning, Afternoon, Evening) Most Active Commuting Period (e.g., Morning Commute, Afternoon Commute) Helps identify high-engagement windows for scheduling social posts, ad placements, or email campaigns. Dayparts These charts surface the best times to reach your audience by showing when they’re most active and engaged across different channels. Explore segmented charts that provide: Connections by DOOH & OOH Daypart ------------------------------------- Visualizes engagement during standard out-of-home advertising time blocks (e.g., Early Morning, Daytime), helping you align campaign timing with peak exposure. Connections by Television & Connected TV Daypart ---------------------------------------------------- Highlights connection activity during traditional TV and streaming time slots (e.g., Daytime, Prime Time), offering insights into second-screen behavior and cross-channel effectiveness. Engagement Method The Engagement Method tab breaks down how users interacted with your campaign — whether through scanning a QR code or clicking a short URL. Metrics Displayed --------------------- Engagement Method . Lists all available entry points (e.g., QR Code, Short URL). Connections. The number of times each method was used. %. Percentage of total connections per method. Response Rate . Not applicable for this section unless an audience size is defined. Note: You can define a target audience size to activate response rate calculations. Without it, response rates will show as N/A . Acquisition The Acquisition tab shows how visitors found your campaign by breaking down the channels driving traffic. Channels & Connections -------------------------- Direct. Visitors who arrived by directly entering your URL, clicking an untracked link, or from sources without referrer data (known as dark traffic). Examples include email, SMS, certain crawlers, or copied-and-pasted links. Other. Traffic coming from other known channels. Additional Channel Details ------------------------------ Connections by Social Channel. Lists traffic from social media platforms (shows activity if available). Connections by Chat Channel. Shows visits coming through chat apps (shows activity if available). Note: In some timeframes, there may be no activity for social or chat channels. Operating System The Operating System tab shows which devices and operating systems your campaign visitors are using. These insights help you understand your audience’s technology preferences so you can better optimize your campaigns and content. Connections by Operating System: ------------------------------------ Breakdown of visits by OS, such as: Desktop OS (Windows, macOS, Linux) Apple iOS (iPhone, iPad) Other OS includes apps or processes that mask the operating system. Connections by Platform --------------------------- Includes most desktop computers and tablets. Includes smartphones and other mobile devices. Language The Language tab shows the primary language settings of your audience’s devices, helping you tailor messaging and content to their preferred languages. Highlights -------------- Indicates the language your visitors’ devices are set to — for example, English, Spanish, French, etc. Helps you understand if your campaign is reaching the intended language-speaking audience. Connections by Language --------------------------- Displays the number and percentage of connections per language. Benchmarking The Benchmarking tab provides valuable insights into how your campaign performs compared to other marketing channels and helps you understand the cost-effectiveness of your efforts. Response Rate by Marketing Channel -------------------------------------- This metric becomes available once you set your campaign’s audience size. It shows how your campaign’s response rate compares with other marketing channels. Average Cost per Connection (All Time) ------------------------------------------ Displays your average cost to acquire a connection through Delivr. Example: ≈ $0.125 per connection A lower cost per connection means you’re getting more engagement for less spend. Cost Comparison ------------------- Your campaign’s cost per connection is compared with major platforms like Facebook Ads, Google Ads, Instagram, LinkedIn, and X Ads. For example, your campaign at $0.13 per connection is more cost-effective than many traditional digital ad platforms. Serialization Available in VIP accounts and above, this section provides a detailed report of connections by Trace ID. Powered by Super Simple Serialization , it enables item-level tracking for serialized QR codes — ideal for product authentication, supply chain visibility, or personalized experiences. People First This top-level overview brings your marketing back to its core purpose — genuine connection. The People First tab is a reminder that behind every scan or click is a real person, not a statistic. This section highlights total unique connections your campaign received during the selected time period. It shifts the focus from raw metrics to real engagement and human interaction.

Data Policy Home>Documentation>Understanding your account dashboard. Delivr Understanding your account dashboard. Understanding your account dashboard. Your Delivr Dashboard is the central hub for everything you do . At its foundation are your campaigns—the core building blocks that connect every link, QR code, and analytic. From here, you can create and manage campaigns, track performance, organize your work, and access account settings. Each section has a clear purpose—here’s how it all comes together. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ Navigation Area Top Row ------------------------- This row gives you quick access to account-wide tools and support: Hello, Account Username. Displays the account you’re logged into. Account. Manage your account and company information. Security. Settings and recommendations to help you keep your account secure. Update your password and security settings. Subscription. Manage your subscription and payment details Learning Center . Access guides, FAQs, and help articles. Support. Submit a support request. Contact. General contact for Delivr. Phone Number . Leave a message for sales or support. Navigation Area Second Row ---------------------------- Here you’ll find tools for managing your campaigns and brand: Dashboard. Returns you to your Campaigns. Archive. Stores campaigns you’ve archived but may want to reference later. Brand. Centralize your brand’s visual identity. Upload logos, set brand colors, and manage QR code templates to ensure consistent alignment with brand guidelines across all campaigns, assets, and digital experiences. Settings. Manage media and campaign locations, connect analytics, configure your custom domain, access APIs, and control other settings. Options. Choose the type of campaign to create. Additional creation options may appear depending on your permissions and plan. Modules. Manage specialized content such as Files, Forms, Offers, Properties, Galleries, and Scratch Offs. Sign Out . Log out of your account securely. Campaign Creation ----------------- From the Dashboard, just enter a link to generate a new campaign with a managed Experience URL and dynamic QR code. "START HERE" is where that creation process begins. Select Dashboard ---------------- If you have access to other accounts, a dropdown will appear allowing you to switch between them. The Tabs -------- Tabs organize your Dashboard so you can switch between different views and tools: Workspaces. A customizable area to group and organize starred campaigns and groups. Campaigns. View and manage all active campaigns. Groups. View your campaigns organised by campaigns group. Tags. View your campaigns by assign keyword or phrases. Digital Links. All your digital link campaigns, brought together in one place for easy access, collaboration, and management. Search. Quickly find a campaign by name, notes, tags, ID, or destination URL. ccount Status Bar ----------------- Displays your subscription level and the status of your allotted number of campaigns. Dropdown Navigation ------------------- These controls help you filter and manage campaigns: Select Group / Create Group . Appears when you have cteated at least one group. Shortcut to view Campaign Groups and to create a new Groups. Sort by . View campaigns by age/date, activity/views, or Campaign ID. Bulk Create ----------- Create multiple dynamic QR codes at once from a spreadsheet. My Campaigns ------------ Your list of campaigns, including for each campaign: Campaign managed Short URL, Campaign Name, Notes, ID, Creation Date, and Campaign ID. Type of campaign, Destination URL if a URL Type, Access to Campaign Parameters, and Preview Report access Quick actions for downloading and customizing QR code, editing the campaign, pausing a campaign, and archiving a campaign. Campaign Export --------------- Export a list of your campaigns with their total view count. Search Active Campaigns ----------------------- Find campaigns by ID, Destination URL (or partial URL string), Campaign Name, Campaign Notes, Campaign Tags, Page Title, or Campaign Parameters. URL Source: http://delivr.com/faq/1582/understanding-your-security-settings Markdown Content: Understanding your security settings. Data Policy Home>Documentation>Understanding your security settings. Delivr Understanding your security settings. Understanding your security settings. Your security is our priority. Delivr offers a centralized Security section that makes it easy to monitor and manage access to your account. You can access this section by clicking the Security link at the top of the page (to the right of Account ). -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Within the Security section, you’ll find five dedicated tabs — each providing visibility and control over a specific area of your account’s protection: 1. Password Change Your Password ------------------------ Passwords must be changed at least once every 365 days . Organizations with stricter policies (e.g., 90-day expiry) can contact Support to configure a custom schedule. Steps To Change your Password --------------------------------- 1. Enter your Current Password . 2. Enter and verify your New Password . 3. Click the green Save button to save changes. 2. Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) Add an Extra Layer of Protection ------------------------------------ 2FA helps prevent unauthorized access by requiring a second step to sign in. Choose one of the following methods to enable: SMS Code Voice Call Verification Authenticator App (e.g., Google Authenticator) Once enabled, you’ll sign in with both your password and a temporary 6-digit code. 3. Logs Access Log -------------- This section shows a record of login activity: Date, Time, Status, Action, Type, and Location If you don’t recognize a device or action: Click Don’t recognize this device? to report it. It's recommended to change your password immediately if you suspect any unauthorized activity. Security Notifications -------------------------- You’ll receive an email notification if any of the following occur: A sign-in is detected from a new device or IP address Sensitive account settings are changed Unusual activity deviates 4. Sessions Device Sessions: Where You’re Signed In ------------------------------------------- See all active sessions where your account is logged in. Each entry shows Device, OS, Browser , and Location . If a device seems unfamiliar or lost, click Sign Out for that session. As a precaution, you should also change your password. 5. Access Grant & Manage Access to Your Campaigns ------------------------------------------- You can grant full or limited access to others in your organization. Access can be set at the account , group , or individual campaign level. Steps to Grant Access ------------------------- 1. Enter a Delivr username or email . 2. Choose the access level. 3. Access is granted immediately. User Roles Account Owner/Admin ----------------------- Full control over all settings and campaigns. Each account has a single primary owner, referred to as the Account Owner or Admin . The Admin has full access to all aspects of the account, including settings, campaigns, and user management. This role is unique — only one Admin is assigned per account. The Admin can grant and revoke access to additional users, who are designated as Contributors , Editors , or Analysts , depending on their role and level of access. Delivr manages account setup during onboarding and continues to support any role or access changes over time. Some high-level account settings are reserved exclusively for the Admin or the Delivr Support Team. These foundational settings are typically configured once and rarely require updates. Contributors ---------------- Create and edit campaigns, manage QR codes, links, pages, and profiles. Contributors have the ability to create, edit, and manage campaigns within your account. This includes: Shortening and managing links Generating and managing QR codes Bulk importing and exporting campaigns Downloading QR codes Creating and managing groups Creating and editing landing pages and profiles Pausing, archiving, and restoring campaigns Viewing reports Note: Contributors cannot delete campaigns. This permission is reserved for the Account Owner (Admin). Editors ----------- Edit and manage campaigns at group/campaign level. The Editor role allows users to edit and manage campaigns within the assigned group(s) or individual campaign(s). Editors can manage links, edit and download QR codes, modify landing pages, pause campaigns, and view performance reports. Analysts ------------ Analysts can view reports and download the associated QR code. Reports can be shared with Analysts at the account, group, or individual QR code/campaign level, depending on the scope assigned. Contributor Dashboard Message --------------------------------- Set a custom message that Contributors will see on their dashboard. 191.title: Deliver connected, measurable experiences to TV audiences.
191.content: Deliver connected, measurable experiences to TV audiences. URL: https://delivr.com/page/745/unleash-the-power-of-shoppable-tv-with-broadcast-qr Title: Broadcast QR effortlessly transports viewers from TV screens to mobile screens. URL Source: http://delivr.com/page/745/unleash-the-power-of-shoppable-tv-with-broadcast-qr Markdown Content: Broadcast QR effortlessly transports viewers from TV screens to mobile screens. Delivr skip to main content +1.631.750.2785 Contact Support Learning Center Learning Center Documentation Uses of QR Codes Videos Our Values Wonderful Things Start Here Curiosity, Where Connection Begins Connecting Worlds, Safely and Securely The Human-First Product Path Delivr’s Approach to Data Privacy Let’s Build a Greener Future Sustainability & QR Codes Accessibility & QR Codes Security & QR Codes Privacy & Retargeting About Delivr About Delivr The Story of Delivr Image 1: Delivr Plans & Pricing Customers Solutions Solutions Overview Super Simple Serialization GS1 Digital Link Logistics Brand Protection Always Open Smart Label & Connected Packaging Touchless Marketing Digital Out-of-Home (DOOH) Shoppable TV Content Management QR Code Reader Engagement Suite The Delivr Engagement Suite The Delivr Difference Dynamic QR Code Generator Custom QR Code Design Mobile Experiences Brand System Campaign & Team Management Intelligent Link Management Advanced Analytics Super Simple Serialization GS1-Compliant Digital Link Privacy-First, Zero-Knowledge Trust Center Register Sign In Image 2: Delivr Our QR codes and short URLs are cookie-free. Our privacy-first tracking anonymizes IP addresses and avoids storing personal or sensitive data, profiling individuals, or fingerprinting devices. Data Policy Shoppable TV Deliver connected, measurable experiences to TV audiences. Dynamic QR codes built for TV, CTV, and digital video. Deliver connected, measurable experiences to TV audiences. Dynamic QR codes built for TV, CTV, and digital video. ------------------------------------------------------ Home>Solutions>Shoppable TV Broadcast QR effortlessly transports viewers from TV screens to mobile screens. Broadcast QR℠ lets you layer a QR Code on video, broadcast, and streaming media to create fresh, beautiful, clear call-to-action. With Broadcast QR, programming can contain onscreen QR codes. Users can simply point their mobile phone cameras at the codes and be redirected to digital experiences. This allows them to learn more, see more, and even purchase products. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ Deliver any experience you can imagine. Relevant and interactive content at the right time. Brands and networks can place Broadcast QR in any programming to launch a second-screen experience. Adding items to a shopping cart Deep linking to an extension of the broadcast/advertising content Launching an augmented reality experience How it works. Activating Broadcast QR during screen programming is simple. 1. Open your camera Alert viewers to hold their phone cameras up to the code on screen during a shoppable moment. 2. Scan the code The code appearing on screen will prompt your audience to scan the code. 3. Shop Customers will be redirected straight to the marketer's website to make their purchase. 4 key benefits of Broadcast QR. 1. Fresh, attractive, and visually integrated . 2. Simplified workflow. No editing of footage or encoding code into the source. Broadcast QR is provided as a scalable, transparent vector graphic (SVG, RGB color model) with the highest error correction level. Code added as an on-screen graphic, overlaying a portion of the screen area. 3. Device agnostic does not require a specialized app for scanning or tracking. It works on any smartphone. 4. Everything you need to manage and track campaigns within a single product suite. Campaign management. Custom domains and URL slugs. Broadcast QR code generator Link shortener and dynamic link management. Mobile-friendly landing pages. Reports with real-time analytics. Broadcast QR places brands, broadcasters and advertisers in direct, measurable contact with their chosen audience. Tracks direct digital audience engagement. Number of scans, when, where and with what device OS. Views by television daypart. Views by language. 100% Private & Secure. The Viewing Audience Everyone watches their favorite programs with their smartphone in hand. Everyone always has their phone with them. People who take the time to visit a station's or advertising program's web page are smartphone savvy. With the release of iOS 11, over 1 billion smartphones have built-in QR scanning via the native camera app. Android devices and social/chat apps also offer built-in QR scanning. 193.title: QR Codes for Billboards, OOH & DOOH.
193.content: QR Codes for Billboards, OOH & DOOH. URL: https://delivr.com/page/725/uses-of-qr-codes-for-billboards-ooh-dooh Title: QR Codes for Billboards, OOH & DOOH. URL Source: http://delivr.com/page/725/uses-of-qr-codes-for-billboards-ooh-dooh Markdown Content: QR Codes for Billboards, OOH & DOOH. Our privacy-first tracking anonymizes IP addresses and avoids storing personal or sensitive data, profiling individuals, or fingerprinting devices. Data Policy Home>Applications by Industry>QR Codes for Billboards, OOH & DOOH QR Codes for Billboards, OOH & DOOH. Using QR codes on billboards is a widespread application. In addition, QR codes can be included in any outdoor advertising. Outdoor advertising, also known as out-of-home advertising (OOH), is advertising that reaches consumers when they are outside their homes. ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Types of outdoor advertising. ----------------------------- The most commonly known type of outdoor advertising is the billboard. Common forms of outdoor advertising today fall into three categories: 1. Billboard Advertising Most people are familiar with billboard advertising, regardless of where they live or travel. Billboards are placed next to high-traffic highways as well as along less-traveled roads in non-urban areas. Drivers see them almost anywhere because they are an efficient and cost-effective means of communicating information that is geographically important – the location of the closest chain fast food restaurant on the route, the nearest motel, or campgrounds at the next exit. 2. Street Furniture A bit of a misnomer, “street furniture” applies not just to bus or park benches but also to bus shelters, newsracks, and telephone kiosks, among others. 3. Transit Ads on the sides of busses are the most common form of transit outdoor advertising, but outdoor advertising is common in subway stations and within subway cars, in taxis, along airport walkways, and wrapped around vehicles – one of the newer trends. Other forms of outdoor advertising. ----------------------------------- Advertising seems to follow consumers wherever they go – in supermarket shopping carts, in sports arenas, and in health clubs. They can be high-tech – think digital – or low tech – think personal-computer generated mini-posters offering services pinned to a coffee shop bulletin board or lamp post. Digital Outdoor Advertising --------------------------- Also known as digital signage, digital outdoor advertising is a specific type that uses electronic technology to change what’s displayed on a screen. Digital billboards feature messages from a handful of advertisers that rotate every six to eight seconds. Other digital signs might be free-standing on an urban sidewalk or shopping plaza. For Small Businesses -------------------- The most important outdoor ad for any small brick and mortar business is its built-in outdoor advertising: exterior signage. Business owners want crisp, clear signage that communicates well from a distance. While it should convey the company’s brand image, it should also help the business get discovered and found. Guidelines for QR codes that will be scanned from a distance. ------------------------------------------------------------- QR codes printed on billboards and other outdoor advertising must be scannable despite a long distance between them and smartphone users. The ratio of the scanning distance to the size of the QR code should be close to 10:1. The simplest approach is to use a QR Code size that is at least one tenth of the scanning distance, excluding the margin. For example, a code on a Billboard that will be scanned from a distance of 10m (32 ft) should be at least 1m (3.2 ft) in size, excluding the margin. Use a minimum margin of 4 blocks. Delivr default. Good contrast between the background and the QR Code color is very important when a standard Black & White code is not used. In cases of colorized codes, the QR Code should have a dark color with a light background color. Some QR Code Readers can not read reversed colors e.g., light color with a dark background color, white with black background color. Data Policy Home>Applications by Industry>QR Codes for Public Spaces QR Codes for Public Spaces. QR codes are increasingly being used in public spaces to share information, preserve art, and create interactive experiences. From parks and monuments to murals and installations, a simple scan can connect people to stories, history, and digital content that would otherwise be impossible to display physically. ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Public art, in particular, has found creative uses for QR codes. Using QR Codes to Restore Murals to Their Original State (via Wooster Collective). ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- A powerful example comes from a mural featured by Wooster Collective in Vancouver, British Columbia. At some point, the mural was vandalized with graffiti. Instead of simply repainting over the damage, a QR code was painted over the offending tag. When scanned, the QR code directed viewers to an image of the mural before it was vandalized — effectively restoring the artwork digitally. This creative approach allowed the public to see the original piece as the artist intended, while also making a statement about preservation and the evolving nature of street art. It turned an act of vandalism into an opportunity for digital storytelling. Image 6: Wooster Collective Mural with Graffiti Image 7: Wooster Collective Mural with QR code Image 8: Wooster Collective Mural with picture before graffiti Source: Wooster Collective, http://www.woostercollective.com/post/using-qr-codes-to-restore-murals-to-their-original-state How QR Codes Add Value in Public Spaces. ---------------------------------------- This example shows how QR codes can go beyond marketing and serve cultural and educational purposes. In public spaces, QR codes can be used to: Share the history of murals, monuments, or landmarks Display “before and after” restoration images Provide artist biographies and project details Link to audio guides or video explanations Offer multilingual information for tourists Because the linked content can be updated anytime, cities and artists can keep information current without altering the physical space again. Blending Physical and Digital Experiences. ------------------------------------------ QR codes help bridge the gap between the physical and digital worlds. In public spaces, they allow limited physical surfaces to connect to unlimited digital content. Whether preserving artwork, educating visitors, or enhancing cultural engagement, QR codes provide a simple yet impactful solution. By thoughtfully integrating QR codes into murals and public installations, communities can protect artistic intent, encourage interaction, and make public spaces more informative and engaging. 195.title: One code, many industries.
195.content: One code, many industries. URL: https://delivr.com/page/784/uses-of-qr-codes Title: Practical uses of QR codes across industries. URL Source: http://delivr.com/page/784/uses-of-qr-codes Markdown Content: A Doorway Between Worlds ------------------------ QR codes have become a quiet bridge between the physical and digital. Across industries, formats, and environments, they help people move from curiosity to clarity in a single scan — no apps, no friction, no guesswork. This collection explores how QR codes show up in the real world, guiding people wherever they encounter a brand, product, space, or story. Small Squares, Big Relief ------------------------- From hospitals and government offices to restaurants, retail, and public spaces, QR codes make everyday interactions easier. They reduce touchpoints, streamline communication, and surface the right information at the right moment. In places where trust matters most, they offer a simple, respectful way to keep people informed — without asking for more than a scan. When Packaging Starts Talking ----------------------------- On boxes, bottles, cans, labels, and printed materials, QR codes turn static surfaces into living ones. Brands use them to share ingredients, instructions, sourcing details, sustainability stories, and regulatory information — without crowding the design. One scan reveals what doesn’t fit on the label, and what consumers increasingly want to know. Stories That Refuse to Stay Put ------------------------------- In marketing, education, and media, QR codes let physical content keep going. From books and brochures to posters, billboards, storefronts, and displays, they connect offline moments to richer digital experiences. Educators, publishers, creators, and developers use them to invite interaction, deepen understanding, and extend the story beyond the page. Stories Behind Every Scan ------------------------- Across every use case, the magic is intention. When thoughtfully designed, QR codes don’t interrupt — they beckon. They offer a next step without pressure, adapt as needs change, and respect the person on the other side of the screen. Behind each experience is the need for reliability, privacy, and flexibility — so every scan works, evolves, and endures long after it’s printed. Explore the links below to see how a simple scan can open up new paths across industries, spaces, and experiences. QR Codes for Reducing Surface Contact and Disease Spread in Healthcare ---------------------------------------------------------------------- In moments where safety matters most, QR codes offer a cleaner way to share information. From check-ins to instructions, a simple scan reduces touchpoints while keeping people informed. Explore QR Codes for Reducing Surface Contact and Disease Spread in Healthcare. QR Codes for Consumer Packaged Goods ------------------------------------ Packaging has more to say than space allows. QR codes help consumer brands share ingredients, stories, and updates — without cluttering the shelf. Explore QR Codes for Consumer Packaged Goods. QR Codes for Healthcare, Doctors & Dentists ------------------------------------------- From patient forms to post-visit care, QR codes simplify communication in clinical settings. One scan delivers clarity while keeping interactions efficient and respectful. Explore QR Codes for Healthcare, Doctors & Dentists. QR Codes for Restaurants ------------------------ Menus, ordering, and updates — right when guests need them. QR codes help restaurants serve information quickly, cleanly, and without breaking the flow of the experience. Explore QR Codes for Restaurants. QR Codes for Food & Product Packaging ------------------------------------- What’s inside matters. QR codes let food and product packaging share sourcing, nutrition, and usage details — beyond what fits on the label. Explore QR Codes for Food & Product Packaging. QR Codes for Labels & Stickers ------------------------------ Small surfaces, big potential. QR codes turn labels and stickers into gateways for instructions, authentication, and evolving information. Explore QR Codes for Labels & Stickers. QR Codes for Books & Educational Materials ------------------------------------------ Learning doesn’t have to stop at the page. QR codes extend books and materials with videos, resources, and interactive context — right when curiosity strikes. Explore QR Codes for Books & Educational Materials. QR Codes for Point-of-Sale Displays ----------------------------------- When attention is fleeting, clarity counts, QR codes at the point of sale help shoppers explore details, compare options, and make confident decisions. Explore QR Codes for Point-of-Sale Displays. QR Codes for Bottles & Cans --------------------------- A single scan can reveal what’s behind the label. QR codes on bottles and cans unlock ingredients, stories, and brand experiences — without redesigns. Explore QR Codes for Bottles & Cans. QR Codes for Storefronts ------------------------ Even when doors are closed, discovery doesn’t have to stop. QR codes on storefronts invite passersby to explore offers, hours, and experiences at any time. Explore QR Codes for Storefronts. QR Codes for Immersive Experiences ---------------------------------- From physical spaces to digital worlds, QR codes help experiences unfold naturally. One scan becomes the entry point to something deeper. Explore QR Codes for Immersive Experiences. QR Codes for Products --------------------- Every product has a story, instructions, or updates to share. QR codes keep that information accessible long after the product leaves the shelf. Explore QR Codes for Products. QR Codes for Flyers ------------------- Flyers spark interest — QR codes carry it forward. A quick scan turns a glance into action, connection, or follow-up. Explore QR Codes for Flyers. QR Codes for Billboards, OOH & DOOH ----------------------------------- Big messages deserve easy next steps. QR codes connect large-format displays to immediate, mobile-friendly experiences. Explore QR Codes for Billboards, OOH & DOOH. QR Codes for Banners -------------------- From events to promotions, banners catch the eye. QR codes give people a simple way to keep going once they’ve noticed you. QR Codes for Banners. QR Codes for Educational Institutions ------------------------------------- Campuses are full of moments to guide and inform. QR codes help students, staff, and visitors find what they need — right when they need it. Explore QR Codes for Educational Institutions. QR Codes for Government ----------------------- Clear communication builds trust. QR codes help public agencies share accurate, up-to-date information in an accessible, efficient way. Explore QR Codes for Government. QR Codes for Sweepstakes & Giveaways ------------------------------------ Participation should be effortless. QR codes make entering, sharing, and tracking sweepstakes simple — for brands and audiences alike. - Explore QR Codes for Sweepstakes & Giveaways. QR Codes for Software & Game Developers --------------------------------------- From onboarding to bonus content, QR codes link physical moments to digital worlds. A scan becomes part of the experience itself. Explore QR Codes for Software & Game Developers. QR Codes for Brochures ---------------------- Brochures introduce the story — QR codes let it continue. One scan opens a deeper context without adding pages. Explore QR Codes for Brochures. QR Codes for Store Windows -------------------------- Windows invite curiosity. QR codes turn that curiosity into exploration — day or night. Explore QR Codes for Store Windows. QR Codes for Posters -------------------- Posters grab attention at a glance. QR codes offer a natural next step for anyone who wants more. Explore QR Codes for Posters. QR Codes for Websites --------------------- Bring digital experiences into the physical world. QR codes connect screens, spaces, and moments with a seamless scan. Explore QR Codes for Websites. QR Codes for Brands ------------------- Brands live across touchpoints. QR codes help keep experiences consistent, connected, and adaptable wherever people encounter them. Explore QR Codes for Brands. QR Codes for Entertainment Companies ------------------------------------ From promotion to participation, QR codes keep audiences engaged. One scan connects fans to content, events, and stories. Explore QR Codes for Entertainment Companies. QR Codes for Media Companies & Publishers ----------------------------------------- Stories don’t have to stay in one format. QR codes help media flow seamlessly between print, digital, and real-world contexts. Explore QR Codes for Media Companies & Publishers. QR Codes for Pharmaceutical Companies ------------------------------------- Accuracy and access matter. QR codes support compliant, up-to-date communication — without overloading packaging. Explore QR Codes for Pharmaceutical Companies. QR Codes for Retail, Beauty & Fashion ------------------------------------- From fit and finish to ingredients and care, QR codes give shoppers more confidence at every step of the journey. Explore QR Codes for Retail, Beauty & Fashion. QR Codes for Public Spaces -------------------------- Museums, parks, transit, and more — QR codes help people navigate shared spaces with ease and context. Explore QR Codes for Public Spaces. QR Codes for Display Windows ---------------------------- A display can do more than look good. QR codes invite interaction, discovery, and follow-through beyond the glass. Explore QR Codes for Display Windows. QR Codes for Automotive Dealerships ----------------------------------- From vehicle details to test drives, QR codes help shoppers explore options at their own pace — right on the lot. Explore QR Codes for Automotive Dealerships. QR Codes for Construction Companies, Architects & Equipment and Material Manufacturers -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Complex projects need clear communication. QR codes keep specs, updates, and documentation accessible on site. Explore QR Codes for Construction Companies, Architects & Equipment and Material Manufacturers. QR Codes for Hotels, Tourism, and the Hospitality Industry ---------------------------------------------------------- From arrival to adventure, QR codes help guests find information, inspiration, and support — without friction. Explore Explore QR Codes for Hotels, Tourism, and the Hospitality industry.

198.content: Vulnerability Disclosure Policy URL: https://delivr.com/page/776/vulnerability-disclosure-policy Title: Vulnerability Disclosure Policy URL Source: http://delivr.com/page/776/vulnerability-disclosure-policy Markdown Content: Vulnerability Disclosure Policy Delivr skip to main content +1.631.750.2785 Contact Support Learning Center Learning Center Documentation Uses of QR Codes Videos Our Values Wonderful Things Start Here Curiosity, Where Connection Begins Connecting Worlds, Safely and Securely The Human-First Product Path Delivr’s Approach to Data Privacy Let’s Build a Greener Future Sustainability & QR Codes Accessibility & QR Codes Security & QR Codes Privacy & Retargeting About Delivr About Delivr The Story of Delivr Image 1: Delivr Plans & Pricing Customers Solutions Solutions Overview Super Simple Serialization GS1 Digital Link Logistics Brand Protection Always Open Smart Label & Connected Packaging Touchless Marketing Digital Out-of-Home (DOOH) Shoppable TV Content Management QR Code Reader Engagement Suite The Delivr Engagement Suite The Delivr Difference Dynamic QR Code Generator Custom QR Code Design Mobile Experiences Brand System Campaign & Team Management Intelligent Link Management Advanced Analytics Super Simple Serialization GS1-Compliant Digital Link Privacy-First, Zero-Knowledge Trust Center Register Sign In Image 2: Delivr Our QR codes and short URLs are cookie-free. Our privacy-first tracking anonymizes IP addresses and avoids storing personal or sensitive data, profiling individuals, or fingerprinting devices. Data Policy Home>Legal>Vulnerability Disclosure Policy Vulnerability Disclosure Policy At Delivr, the most effective way to ensure software security is to incorporate it as an integral part of the application and platform development and delivery process. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ Delivr recognizes the importance of privacy, security, and community outreach. Our top priority is ensuring the safety and security of our technology users and protecting the information of Delivr, customers, consumers, and employees. To accomplish this, we have implemented a coordinated and constructive approach to address and report security issues. When properly notified of legitimate issues, we will do our best to acknowledge your vulnerability report, assign resources to investigate the case, and fix potential problems as quickly as possible. Whether you are a user of Delivr products, a software developer, or a security enthusiast, you are an essential part of this process. Reporting Security Issues ------------------------- If you believe you have discovered a vulnerability in a Delivr asset/system or have a security incident to report, please fill out a support form. In all cases, you must: Respect our privacy. Contact us immediately if you access anyone else’s data, personal or otherwise, such as usernames, passwords and other credentials. You must not save, store or transmit this information. Act in good faith. You should report the vulnerability to us without attached conditions. Please work with us. Promptly report any findings to us, stopping after you find the first vulnerability and requesting permission to continue testing. Allow us a reasonable time to resolve the vulnerability before publicly disclosing it. And you must not: Exfiltrate data. Instead, use a proof of concept to demonstrate a vulnerability. Exploit a vulnerability to disable further security controls. Perform social engineering. Use automated scanners. Next Steps ---------- Upon receipt of the vulnerability/security report, Delivr will undertake a series of steps to address the issue: 1. Delivr requests the reporter keep any communication regarding the vulnerability confidential. 2. Delivr investigates and verifies the vulnerability. 3. Delivr addresses the vulnerability and releases an update or patch to the software. If, for some reason, this cannot be done quickly or at all, Delivr will provide information on recommended mitigations. 4. Delivr will endeavor to keep the reporter apprised of every step in this process. We greatly appreciate the efforts of security researchers and discoverers who share information on security issues, allowing us to improve our products and services and better protect our customers. Thank you for working with us through the above process. Data Policy Home>Documentation>What are modules, and how do you use them? Delivr What are modules, and how do you use them? What are modules, and how do you use them? Modules in Delivr simplify content management, making it quick and efficient. They allow you to build and reuse specialized components—such as lead-capture forms, file downloads, or interactive experiences—across your landing pages and profiles. With modules, you can add powerful features without starting from scratch each time. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ Types of modules you can create. Each module serves a specific purpose and can be added to Page or Profile: Files --------- Upload PDFs that can be delivered via a short URL. Max file size: 10MB Accepted format: PDF File names should avoid special characters (allowed: , -, , ., ®, ©, @). Files are public—anyone with the URL can download. Forms --------- Create and embed custom forms on Pages and Profiles to collect visitor responses. Forms appear after page content. Be sure to whitelist noreply@delivr.com for response delivery. Offers ---------- Highlight promotions or incentives on Pages or Form Success Screens. Properties -------------- Add specific Properties to Profiles to showcase business or organizational info. Galleries ------------- Display visual content on Pages using image galleries. Scratch Offs ---------------- Add interactive scratch-to-reveal images. Tip: Images used must be the same size for correct display. How to Access Modules ------------------------- Access the Modules section from the top right of your Dashboard. Once created, modules can be easily added to Pages and Profiles —no coding required. Delivr What are response rate, mobile, desktop, location by IP and location by GPS in a report? What are response rate, mobile, desktop, location by IP and location by GPS in a report? Here are the definitions for the percentages shown in Delivr Reports: --------------------------------------------------------------------- Response Rate - Total Views as percentage of Campaign Circulation. Available only if Campaign Circulation is entered. Mobile - Percentage of Total Views using a mobile device browser. Desktop - Percentage of Total Views using a desktop browser. Location by IP - Percentage of Total Views where a location is determined using the IP address of an end-user's device. Location by GPS - Percentage of Total Views where a location is determined using the GPS location information provided with the end-user's consent directly from the end-user's device. 203.title: What are the best business uses of QR codes?
203.content: What are the best business uses of QR codes? URL: https://delivr.com/faq/1600/what-are-the-best-business-uses-of-qr-codes Title: What are the best business uses of QR codes? URL Source: http://delivr.com/faq/1600/what-are-the-best-business-uses-of-qr-codes Markdown Content: What are the best business uses of QR codes? Our privacy-first tracking anonymizes IP addresses and avoids storing personal or sensitive data, profiling individuals, or fingerprinting devices. Data Policy Home>Documentation>What are the best business uses of QR codes? Delivr What are the best business uses of QR codes? What are the best business uses of QR codes? QR codes are highly versatile. They link everyday interactions with online experiences, changing how businesses engage with customers. Creating and using a QR code is simple. Here's how companies use QR codes to improve their business. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 1. Frictionless Payments. Scan and Go ----------------------------------------- Customers can scan a QR code to pay, making checkout fast, contactless, and secure. This method, ideal for busy retailers, restaurants, and events, helps keep lines moving and provides a smooth customer experience, showing a commitment to customer convenience. 2. Interactive Marketing. Bring Your Campaigns to Life ---------------------------------------------------------- QR codes make marketing engaging by allowing customers to unlock videos, promotions, or exclusive content with a scan, while giving you the ability to track what works. 3. Smart Packaging. Tell Your Story Beyond the Label -------------------------------------------------------- Customers can scan to access product details, tutorials, and behind-the-scenes content. It's an effective way to build trust and transparency while offering helpful information that deepens the customer's connection to your brand. 4. Touchless Menus. A Modern Dining Experience -------------------------------------------------- In restaurants, QR codes have become essential. Diners scan the code at their table and browse the menu on their phone; there are no waiting or physical menus. It's convenient and hygienic, and restaurants can update their offerings instantly without reprinting menus. It's a smarter, faster way to serve customers. 5. Streamlined Events. Scan for Entry and Info -------------------------------------------------- QR codes make event management easier. They enable quick check-ins and easy access to schedules or maps, simplifying the process for both organizers and attendees. By removing paper tickets and reducing wait times, QR codes help events run more smoothly. They also provide quick access to extra content with just a scan, fostering a sense of control and efficiency. 6. Networking Made Easy. Share Your Info Instantly ------------------------------------------------------ Traditional business cards often need to be corrected or remembered, but with a QR code, your contact details are quickly saved to someone's phone. Whether you share your phone number, email, or LinkedIn profile, it's an easy and memorable way to connect. No more wasted paper—just instant digital connections, making networking simple. Data Policy Home>Documentation>What are total views, QR scans, NFC taps and short URL clicks? Delivr What are total views, QR scans, NFC taps and short URL clicks? What are total views, QR scans, NFC taps and short URL clicks? Delivr tracks a number of different events that reflect ways how end-users interact with QR codes, NFC tags or Short URLs. Delivr can track users scanning QR codes, tapping on NFC Tags, clicking on Short URLs, and more. --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Here are the definitions for the numbers shown in Delivr Reports: Total Views - Total visits to the Campaign Short URL or Campaign Landing Page or Profile through ALL tracking methods/URLs including scans, taps, clicks, and more. QR Scans - Total visits to the Campaign Short URL or Campaign Landing Page or Profile through scans of a Delivr QR code. NFC Taps - Total visits to the Campaign Short URL or Campaign Landing Page or Profile through taps of an NFC tag containing a Delivr NFC Tracking URL. Short URL Clicks - Total visits to the Campaign Short URL or Campaign Landing Page or Profile through clicks of the Delivr Short URL.
