Data Policy Home>Documentation>What are Workspaces, and how do you use them? Delivr What are Workspaces, and how do you use them? What are Workspaces, and how do you use them? Workspaces in Delivr offer a central location that automatically organizes Campaigns and Campaign Groups by tagged color—making access simpler, collaboration more seamless, and execution more focused. Perfect for organizing by team, goal, task, or any custom purpose, Workspaces can be named however you prefer and used in a way that makes sense for your workflow team. Each Workspace functions as a personalized dashboard, highlighting your most essential items. When you unstar a Campaign or Group, it is automatically removed from any Workspace where it appears. This provides full flexibility without changing your original Campaigns or Groups. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ How to Use Workspaces. Access Workspaces --------------------- Click the Workspaces tab at the top of your dashboard (next to the Campaigns tab). Open a Workspace -------------------- Click VIEW WORKSPACE to explore its contents and take actions such as: Edit Campaign Download QR Code View Report Edit a Workspace -------------------- Click EDIT WORKSPACE to: Rename the Workspace Add internal notes for context or collaboration Key Features ---------------- Starred Items When you star a Campaign or Group, it’s automatically added to the corresponding Workspace based on its tag color. Workspace Notes Leave helpful notes about goals, team roles, campaign status, or anything else your team should know. Private & Flexible Workspaces are visible only to the Account Owner/Admin and Contributors. They don’t impact how others view or interact with campaigns. Organized Execution Prioritize and group campaigns to stay aligned—without disrupting live settings or reporting. Starring Campaigns and Groups Are Independent Actions --------------------------------------------------------- Tagging a Campaign or Group with a star is an independent action—updating one does not affect the other. For example: 1. If you tag five Campaigns with a green star and place them in a Group tagged with a purple star , the Campaigns will retain their green tags. 2. A Group can contain Campaigns with a mix of star colors. Assigning a star color to the Group does not change or override the star colors of the Campaigns within it. In short: starring is flexible and non-hierarchical—each Campaign and Group keeps its assigned star, regardless of grouping. Our privacy-first tracking anonymizes IP addresses and avoids storing personal or sensitive data, profiling individuals, or fingerprinting devices. Data Policy Home>Documentation>What Is a Campaign? Delivr What Is a Campaign? What Is a Campaign? In Delivr, campaigns are the foundation of your Dashboard . Every link, QR code, and analytics connect to a campaign. Each campaign displays its title, notes, ID, creation date, and the destination for its QR code or managed short URL—shaping the experience for anyone who engages with it. Campaigns are fully dynamic, so you can update their destination or content at any time without reprinting your QR code. ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Where to Find Your Campaigns ---------------------------- All your campaigns are available from your Dashboard. Here you can: Edit details like destination, content, and QR code design. View reports to track scans, locations, devices, and more. Copy your Short URL for use in social posts or emails. Download QR codes Pause or archive campaigns to stop scans without deleting data. campaigns to see exactly what your audience will experience. Change the campaign type (e.g., URL → Page or Page → Profile) without losing analytics. Editing a Campaign ------------------ When you edit a campaign, you can: Update its Destination URL , page content, or profile links. Customize the QR code design (color, shape, logo). Add or update campaign notes and tags for better search and organization. Adjust tracking parameters for advanced reporting. Types of Campaigns ------------------ A campaign can be one of three types— URL , Page , or Profile —each offering different features and use cases. See the Types of Campaigns FAQ for a detailed breakdown of each option. How can we help you? -------------------- If you encounter any issues during registration or email verification, please contact our support team, and we’ll be happy to assist. A QR code is a 2D barcode that can store alphanumeric information such as a URL that links a webpage, chunks of text, phone numbers, email addresses, contact information, calendar events, and location coordinates. Advanced QR Code Generators allow the additional ability to design, manage/edit and track QR codes. -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- A QR Code Generator stores data such as a short URL or web addresses into a QR code. This can be easily done with the QR Generator by Delivr. With Delivr you can create a QR code by simply typing in your URL and downloading the QR code graphic in print-ready Vector (EPS, SVG) formats, or Bitmap/Raster (PNG, JPG) formats. Advanced QR Code Generators such as Delivr go one step further and make it possible to create dynamic QR codes, edit the destination of your QR code, customize the design of your QR code, and track scanning activity. 212.title: What is a QR code?
212.content: What is a QR code? URL: https://delivr.com/faq/1323/what-is-a-qr-code Title: What is a QR code? URL Source: http://delivr.com/faq/1323/what-is-a-qr-code Markdown Content: QR code stands for "Quick Response" code. A QR code is a 2D barcode that can store alphanumeric information such as a URL. This information can be decoded and read using a QR code scanner or a QR code reader app on a smartphone. The QR code was invented by DENSO Wave, a Toyota subsidiary in 1994. --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- The acronym QR is short for “ Q uick R esponse”. QR Codes are also often called 2D barcodes. However, this notation is not correct, because it means “a two-dimensional code consisting of bars” although a QR code consists of pixels (so-called “modules”) instead of bars. A QR code is a two-dimensional code (2D code, matrix code), this means it contains data not only in one dimension e.g., horizontally from left to right like the barcodes on the products in your grocery store but also in a second dimension (vertical and horizontal). As a QR Code can carry information in both the vertical and horizontal direction, it can store up to several hundred times the amount of data carried by a bar code. Over 7,000 numeric characters can be encoded in one QR Code. The Quick Response (QR) code was developed at DENSO Corporation’s Industrial Systems Product Division (currently separated in 2001 to become DENSO WAVE Incorporated) in 1994. The code was originally developed to manage automotive component inventories in DENSO plants. Traditional bar codes were conventionally used, but as the need increased to include more information in a limited space, DENSO set up a team in 1992 to develop a new code, and in two years the QR code was developed. Like other codes, the QR code symbol can be captured with imaging devices like a camera and then digitally processed. Software prepares the captured image data until it can be algorithmically processed according to the QR code standard so the QR code content can be read. The QR code has position detection patterns located at the three corners of the code, which enable reader equipment to quickly and accurately read the code from any direction. Since the QR code was originally developed for factory use, one of the development focuses was to create a code that could withstand stains and damage. Through this development, the code’s data can be restored even if a maximum of 30 percent of the code is damaged. Because of the excellent error correction QR codes provide and the high memory capacity (in comparison with other bar codes), the QR code also gained traction outside the automotive industry. Modern mobile phones are powerful enough to run QR code reading software and also usually provide a camera. This combination brought up a variety of possibilities to use the QR code to easily transfer data without needing to type a URL into a mobile web browser. Therefore, a widespread use of QR codes became possible with the recent smartphone boom and the increasing spread of mobile internet. The patent rights for the code are owned by DENSO WAVE, but the specifications and use of the code were made open and free to the public to widely expand its use in the market. The code was also approved as an ISO international standard in 2000. In Japan, where the QR code holds commanding share for two dimensional codes, it has been widely used in production and distribution management. And, since QR code reader functions were installed to cellular phones in early 2000, their usage has vastly expanded to the consumer market in areas including, advertising and marketing, business cards, ticket passes, coupons and more. History of the QR Code. More about QR codes can be found on Wikipedia. Where you generate your QR codes is as important as your reason for generating them. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ Delivr is one of a handful of companies worldwide licensed by DENSO. DENSO, part of the Toyota Group, is a world leader in automatic data capture technology and the inventor of the QR Code®. DENSO’s QR code technology is the undisputed standard in driving analog to digital engagement. Another DENSO technology, SQRC® is the worlds only ISO/IEC18004 compliant 2D barcode that can securely trigger a public and private action. Through this relationship, Delivr offers a compelling new generation of services that transform the use of the conventional QR code and takes QR codes to a higher level of intelligence, security, and customer experience. All the resulting solutions are either patented by DENSO or proprietary to Delivr and are superior to competing products. No other self-service QR Code Generator can make these same claims, it is our unique distinction. There are three conditions in the world of QR codes you simply can not control. You can't control which device is reading your QR code, the operating system on that device, or which QR code reader app is being used. As a DENSO partner, Delivr upholds rarefied standards when generating ISO compliant QR codes with our QR Code Generator. Take control over the only condition in the world of QR codes you can control - the QR code itself by using QR codes from Delivr. QR codes are matrix type codes developed to accomplish three primary goals: --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 1. fast, muti-directional scanning 2. achieve high capacity storage within a small area 3. portable information at your fingertips How do QR codes work? --------------------- QR codes can be read by the camera in most mobile phones. Once they are scanned, software deciphers the encrypted information and initiates a response. This response can involve either directing the phone to a website, providing the user with iintegrated data stored within the code or documenting an event. In any case, it eliminates the need to search for content and provides direct access to information. What are QR codes used for? --------------------------- Advertising Competitions Business cards Social networking (Facebook , Twitter, etc.) Branding Ticketing/registration Campaign tracking File access Statistics Logistics/parts tracking Marketing Payment Reminders/updates the possibilities are endless. In a nutshell. -------------- QR codes can hold over 200 times the data of a regular bar code and have over 30 fold increase in recording density 360 degree multidirectional, high speed reading - no need to align the reader to the code, simply point and scan Dirt and damage resistant - data redundancy ensures that the code can be read if part of the code is missing or damaged Warp resistance - code can be placed on a curved or warped surface Encode different language format - supports other languages character sets such as Japanese, Chinese, etc. High capacity space saving printing using less space, less ink and less intrusion for marketing space Linking function - one QR code can be divided into 12 QR codes What's next? ------------ DENSO has recently released the Security QR code (SQRC®). Any QR code enabled device can read QR codes therefore SQRC were developed to meet the growing demands for the secure transportation of data with the product. Confidential information is code key encrypted and combined in the QR code. This means that the QR code can still be read by general readers, but the encrypted data is protected and only accessible using a special reading device with the same SQRC code key. Not just for phones. -------------------- As the developers of QR code, DENSO data capture solutions format is best equipped to get the most out of your QR codes. They have faster, more accurate scanning rates and are known throughout the industry for their quality and durability. Ask DENSO which solution is best for your application.

Delivr What is a Resolver? What is a Resolver? The "resolver" component in Digital Link allows a barcode to direct to various data sources depending on the application that reads the symbol. If there's no "resolver," the link encoded in the barcode will only point to a single web address. -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Using a "resolver," brands can ensure that different scans of the same barcode lead to the most suitable endpoint. For example, a product may have only one QR code on its packaging, but scanning it with a "warehouse application" will direct you to a different URL than scanning it with a "consumer application." Our privacy-first tracking anonymizes IP addresses and avoids storing personal or sensitive data, profiling individuals, or fingerprinting devices. Data Policy Home>Documentation>What is a URL shortener? Delivr What is a URL shortener? What is a URL shortener? A URL shortener is an online tool that converts your long links into shorter ones. Shortened links are clean, easy to share and memorize. ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- A URL Shortener is an online application that converts a regular URL (a web address) into a condensed format. The user only has to copy the full URL of a website and paste it into a URL shortening tool to come up with an abbreviated version. A URL Shortener allows users to shorten very long links into shorter ones that are better suited for e-mailing, instant messaging, QR code or NFC Tag encoding and for services that restrict character counts. Other uses of URL shortening are to "beautify" a link and track clicks. Often regular unshortened links may be aesthetically unpleasing. Many web developers pass descriptive attributes in the URL to represent data hierarchies, command structures, transaction paths or session information. Marketing departments and their ad agencies add campaign parameters. This can result in URLs that are hundreds of characters long and that contain complex character patterns. Such URLs are difficult to memorize, type-out or distribute. As a result, long URLs must be copied-and-pasted for reliability. Thus, short URLs may be more convenient for websites or hard copy publications (e.g. a printed magazine or a book), the latter often requiring that very long strings be broken into multiple lines (as is the case with some e-mail software or internet forums) or truncated. On Twitter and some instant-messaging services, there is a limit to the number of characters a message can carry. Using a URL shortener can allow linking to web pages which would otherwise violate this constraint. Delivr generates Short URLs that are friendly and human-readable. URLs encoded in two-dimensional barcodes such as QR code are shortened by Delivr's URL shortener in order to reduce the printed area of the code and allow printing at lower density (less complexity) in order to improve scanning reliability. Delivr provides detailed information on the clicks a link receives, which can be simpler than setting up an equally powerful server-side analytics engine. Delivr can host your own branded short domain. Your domain replaces the “delivr.com” in your links. Delivr What is Delivr? What is Delivr? Established in 2008, Delivr is a Martech platform used by thousands of businesses to intelligently route, track and visualize consumer engagement with print and broadcast media. Delivr makes it simple to create and manage secure QR codes®, Short URLs and NFC tags. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ Everything you need to create, manage and track campaigns within a single platform. ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Campaign Management. URL Shortener with Link Management. QR Code Generator with QR Code Management. Mobile-friendly Landing Pages. Insights with Real-time Analytics. Brand Protection. Campaign Management Dashboard ----------------------------- We've developed a dashboard that cuts to what matters most in a fresh and remarkably clear manner. In one click you can create campaigns using triggers such as Short URLs and QR Codes®. URL Shortener with Link Management ---------------------------------- Manage links easily and effectively. With branded short domains, dynamic URLs, and intelligent link routing, you’ll have unprecedented control to optimize, manage, and track your links. QR Code Generator with QR Code Management. ------------------------------------------ QR codes are a bridge between offline and online brand experiences, content, and offers. The QR Code Generator by Delivr is the simplest way to create, design, manage, and track QR codes. With our Dynamic QR codes, you have full flexibility, because only a short URL that points to the content is encoded. As such, you can edit/update the stored links without having to generate and print the QR codes again. This will save reprinting costs, effort and enable you to respond to any changes in the campaign as quickly as possible. Export QR codes in both raster (PNG, JPG) and vector (EPS, SVG) formats. Select the best option for printing QR codes in any size, color and on any medium, with no compromises on quality. ISO 18004-compliant. Design your QR code. Export QR code in vector format for high-quality print. Edit destination anytime. QR code URLs encoded with HTTPS. Real-time reporting. 100% ad free. Mobile-friendly Landing Pages ----------------------------- In just minutes and a few clicks, you can create a variety of hosted mobile-friendly landing pages. No technical knowledge required. Visual, Motion & Broadcast QR ----------------------------- Enables you to effortlessly layer a QR Code on media to create fresh, beautiful, clear call-to-actions. Insights w/ Real-time Analytics ------------------------------- Straightforward reports display everything agencies, brands and broadcasters need to know about audience engagement in real time. Turn insights into action to increase purchase intent at the point of sale. TrueMrk® -------- Combines brand protection and marketing programs into a single, intuitive experience. Provides product authenticity, provenance and consumer engagement while identifying incidents of counterfeiting and gray market diversion. Delivr is a licensed DENSO Partner. DENSO, part of the Toyota Group, is a world leader in automatic data capture technology and the inventor of the QR Code®. 220.title: What is SQRC?
220.content: What is SQRC? URL: https://delivr.com/faq/1468/what-is-sqrc Title: What is SQRC? URL Source: http://delivr.com/faq/1468/what-is-sqrc Markdown Content: What is SQRC? Our privacy-first tracking anonymizes IP addresses and avoids storing personal or sensitive data, profiling individuals, or fingerprinting devices. Data Policy Home>Documentation>What is SQRC? Delivr What is SQRC? What is SQRC? SQRC (Secret-function-equipped QR code) is the worlds only ISO/IEC18004 compliant 2D barcode that can securely trigger a public and private action. SQRC codes while looking no different than a standard QR code, can contain public data and encrypted private data, providing a foundation for secure authentication and authorization to access confidential information. SQRC keeps the error correction capability of codes intact and undisturbed, ensuring greater readability when a code is damaged or obstructed. DENSO, part of the Toyota Group, is the inventor of the QR Code® and SQRC®. SQRC® is a registered trademark of DENSO WAVE INC. ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- SQRC conceals and protects private information. SQRC Conceals Private Information Can be scanned and ‘read’ by an ordinary QR code reader/scanner - but only public information is readable The fact that private information exists is not obvious; cannot be detected by a QR code reader/scanner SQRC Protects Private Information SQRC® Encoding: Can only be read by an authorized SQRC® capable scanner, making it possible to encode and manage confidential information. Secret Key Encryption: Can only be read by scanners that have the secret key used for encryption SQRC are readable when other codes might not be. Error correction allows reading of a code even if it is dirty or damaged. With SQRC®, full error correction stays intact. Some of the other ‘secure’ code methods involve using the error correction area to conceal ‘secret’ information; this means the error correction area is intentionally corrupted, which is an equivalent to a damaged code. If such code is then further obstructed in some way, whether by dirt, damage, or some other obstruction, the code might not be readable. SQRC®, on the other hand, retains the full error correction capability; thus, an SQRC® of the same size as a QR code® using such alternative method will provide higher readability when obstructed. Further, in such other solutions, the private data may not have the same error correction rate as the PUBLIC data, or not have the error correction capability at all. SQRC visual encoding is virtually impossible to crack. The QR code® specification is a public standard; this means there is a publicly known algorithm that produces a set of error correction information for each given set of data. When such error correction information is intentionally damaged by introducing a ‘noise’, it is always possible to determine the difference, that is to exactly find out what the introduced ‘noise’ is. Given time, today’s computer processing power, code breaking algorithms and sufficient number of samples, such noise can be analyzed, determining if it actually carries ‘secret’ information. Such analysis can eventually lead to breaking of the encryption used and thus reading, replication or counterfeiting of such ‘secret’ information. SQRC® technology, on the other hand, leaves the error correction intact; it is thus not possible to determine the difference based on the publicly known standard. It is actually not possible to determine with certainty that the QR code® is actually an SQRC®. SQRC are more secure, using secure key encryption. SQRC® secret information is further protected by a Secret Key encryption, where only the SQRC-capable scanners or App SDKs that also possess various levels of decoding authorization to access the Secret Key used for a particular SQRC® encryption can actually read and decrypt the private information contained in that particular SQRC®. Unauthorized applications can not read or detect the fact that private information is included, preventing data analysis and application of encryption breaking techniques to break the code and read the information. 221.title: What is the difference between a static and dynamic QR code?
221.content: What is the difference between a static and dynamic QR code? URL: https://delivr.com/faq/1459/what-is-the-difference-between-a-static-and-dynamic-qr-code Title: What is the difference between a static and dynamic QR code? URL Source: http://delivr.com/faq/1459/what-is-the-difference-between-a-static-and-dynamic-qr-code Markdown Content: Static QR Code. A QR code that encodes a fixed destination directly into the pattern — once created it cannot be changed and does not offer analytics. Dynamic QR Code. A QR code that encodes a redirect link which you can update anytime, allows tracking, and supports advanced features without reprinting the code. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Static vs. Dynamic QR Codes — What’s the Difference? ---------------------------------------------------- Static QR Codes --------------- A static QR code contains a fixed destination directly encoded into the QR pattern. Once created, the destination cannot be changed — ever. Static codes also do not offer tracking or analytics, and if you need to update the link, you must generate and print a new code. Dynamic QR Codes ---------------- A dynamic QR code contains a short redirect URL that points to a destination hosted on a QR platform. Because the destination is managed server-side, you can update where the code points at any time — even after printing. Dynamic QR codes also support analytics, tracking, and advanced behaviors. Key Differences at a Glance --------------------------- 1. Editability -------------- Static QR Code Editability &Operation ------------------------------------- Cannot be edited after creation Requires reprinting if the link changes Image 1 Dynamic QR Code Editability & Operation --------------------------------------- Destination can be updated anytime No need to reprint for minor changes Can support advanced behaviors (location, language, and device OS routing Can support expiration, A/B testing, password protection, campaign scheduling Image 2 2. Analytics & Tracking ----------------------- Static QR Code -------------- No scan tracking No built-in analytics Dynamic QR Code --------------- Provides scan analytics (time, location, language, device type, etc.) Helps measure campaign performance 3. Code Complexity ------------------ Static QR Code Complexity ------------------------- Static QR code encoded directly with the destination URL. Encodes full URL or data Can become visually dense if the URL is long Example , https://delivr.com/page/764/super-simple-serialization?utm source Presentation%20Deck&utm medium QR%20Code&utm campaign Super%20Simple%20Serialization&utm term Unlimited Image 3 Dynamic QR Code Simplicity -------------------------- Dynamic QR code encoded with a short URL.delivr.com is used by default.This can be replaced with a custom sub-domain of yours. Encodes a short redirect link Generates cleaner, simpler QR code patterns because only a short URL is encoded. Example ,https://delivr.com/2fuyx Image 4 4. Security ----------- Types of QR Codes and Their Level of Security --------------------------------------------- Image 5 Static QR Code -------------- Zero security Features. Fixed URL. Once it's in use, the Destination url cannot be Changed. Dynamic QR Code --------------- Dynamic QR code . The destination URL can be changed at any time. Grey market diversion. Can be indicated by approximate location. Serialized QR code . The destination URL can be changed at any time. Each QR is unique. Multiple scans of the same QR code may indicate a counterfeit. Secure QR code. Can be authenticated with a smartphone. Used for counterfeit detection. 5. Cost & Service Dependence ---------------------------- Static QR Code -------------- Free and permanent No ongoing service required The code will work as long as the destination link is valid. Dynamic QR Code --------------- Requires a QR platform or subscription If the service expires or is discontinued, the code may fail. When Should You Use Each Type? ------------------------------ Static QR Code Use Cases ------------------------ A fixed URL that will never change Short-term promotions with no tracking needs Simple use cases like contact details or Wi-Fi codes Dynamic QR Code Use Cases ------------------------- Marketing campaigns with measurable results Menus, pricing, or content that changes often A/B testing different destinations Brands needing centralized control across many printed codes How Dynamic QR Codes Work ------------------------- Dynamic codes encode a redirect link (e.g., a short URL) instead of your final URL. When scanned, the redirect service sends the user to the latest destination — and because the destination is controlled server-side, you can change it later. Can You Convert a Static QR Code to Dynamic? -------------------------------------------- No — static QR codes cannot be converted into dynamic ones. To switch to a dynamic QR code, you must generate a new code. Bottom Line ----------- Static QR codes are permanent, simple, and free — but not flexible. Dynamic QR codes are editable, trackable, and scalable — but require a platform.

Delivr What is the difference between vector and raster formats when downloading and using QR codes? What is the difference between vector and raster formats when downloading and using QR codes? The main difference between vector and raster graphics is that vector graphics are composed of paths, while raster graphics are composed of pixels. Vector graphics can be resized without loss of quality, which is ideal for printing. Vectors come in 4 file formats — .AI, .EPS (print use), .SVG (digital use) and .PDF. Raster graphics lose quality when resized. Examples of Raster formats include .GIF,.JPG,.PNG and .TIFF. For best results printing standard QR codes we recommend .EPS format. That said, if the QR code design is customized or includes tranparency, .SVG format should be used. --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Vector images are mathematical calculations from one point to another that form lines and shapes. When you enlarge a vector graphic, the math formulas stay the same, rendering the same visual graphic no matter the size. Vector graphics can be scaled to any size without losing quality. Raster images are made of pixels. A pixel is a single point or the smallest single element in a display device. If you zoom in to a raster image you may start to see a lot of little tiny squares. A raster image has a specific number of pixels. When you enlarge the image file without changing the number of pixels, the image will look blurry. When you enlarge the file by adding more pixels, the pixels are added randomly throughout the image, rarely producing good results. 223.title: What is the GS1 Digital Link?
223.content: What is the GS1 Digital Link? URL: https://delivr.com/faq/1553/what-is-the-gs1-digital-link Title: What is the GS1 Digital Link? URL Source: http://delivr.com/faq/1553/what-is-the-gs1-digital-link Markdown Content: What is the GS1 Digital Link? Our privacy-first tracking anonymizes IP addresses and avoids storing personal or sensitive data, profiling individuals, or fingerprinting devices. Data Policy Home>Documentation>What is the GS1 Digital Link? Delivr What is the GS1 Digital Link? What is the GS1 Digital Link? The GS1 Digital Link is a standardized structure that enables a 2D barcode to serve as a point-of-sale function and a web-based URL for consumer experiences. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- It has been announced that in 2027, a new standard will replace the Universal Product Codes (UPC) that have been in use for nearly 50 years. This groundbreaking standard will use QR codes encoded with a GS1 Digital Link, providing a range of benefits for businesses and consumers alike. The upgrade to QR codes will enable every product to become smartphone interactive, allowing consumers to connect with products seamlessly. This will enable transactions with point-of-sale systems, providing a more efficient and convenient shopping experience. GS1 Digital Link will also provide supply chain traceability, ensuring that each product can be tracked from its inception to its final destination. GS1 has long been recognized for creating a global business language based on unique and unambiguous identification. This identification is encoded in data carriers like QR codes and RFID tags, and it is essential for business processes that rely on those identifiers. These fundamentals are used worldwide to support the various business needs of supply chain partners, enabling greater efficiencies in processing goods and services. The World Wide Web is an environment that allows people and companies to access and share information on anything, including products and services. GS1 Digital Link serves as the bridge that connects GS1-based identification schemes with the syntax used on the World Wide Web. This provides a standard way of expressing GS1 keys and attribute data in a format that can be used on the Web. The GS1 Digital Link Standard defines how to structure web URIs to include additional information like batch/lot, expiration date, serial number, and other GS1 keys like GLN (Global Location Number) and SSCC (Serial Shipping Container Code). This powerful standard enables a single, standardized QR code to perform multiple online and offline functions, reducing the need for numerous codes on packaging. With the implementation of GS1 Digital Link, businesses and consumers will benefit from greater efficiency, traceability, and convenience. Data Policy Home>Documentation>What level of QR code designing is possible? Delivr What level of QR code designing is possible? What level of QR code designing is possible? Delivr's design tools allow you to effortlessly merge the functions of a dynamic QR Code with digital assets to create a beautiful and clear call to action. Combine the visual design that people love with the QR Code call-to-action that people know, creating an experience that draws people's attention and invites them to engage with your brand, from the world around them to digital and spatial experiences. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Standard Design QR Code with Customized Settings ------------------------------------------------ Modify the default settings of your standard design dynamic QR code. QR Code Designer ---------------- Delivr's QR Code Designer enables you to customize the design of your dynamic QR code. Set colors, choose a pattern and marker design, add your logo, add a background image, and choose a frame with your text. VisualQR & BroadcastQR ---------------------- VisualQR & BroadcastQR™ creates a specially formatted QR code that utilizes our MagicDot™ Scanning Technology. You can layer this QR code in SVG format on top of any print or digital asset - logos, graphics, photos, animations, video, broadcast, and streaming media. ShapeQR ------- ShapeQR™ creates a QR code in custom shapes. The square code within the shape is each shape's functional, scannable part. 228.title: What's with the white border around the QR code?
228.content: What's with the white border around the QR code? URL: https://delivr.com/faq/1455/whats-with-the-white-border-around-the-qr-code Title: What's with the white border around the QR code? URL Source: http://delivr.com/faq/1455/whats-with-the-white-border-around-the-qr-code Markdown Content: What's with the white border around the QR code? Delivr skip to main content +1.631.750.2785 Contact Support Learning Center Learning Center Documentation Uses of QR Codes Videos Our Values Wonderful Things Start Here Curiosity, Where Connection Begins Connecting Worlds, Safely and Securely The Human-First Product Path Delivr’s Approach to Data Privacy Let’s Build a Greener Future Sustainability & QR Codes Accessibility & QR Codes Security & QR Codes Privacy & Retargeting About Delivr About Delivr The Story of Delivr Image 1: Delivr Plans & Pricing Customers Solutions Solutions Overview Super Simple Serialization GS1 Digital Link Logistics Brand Protection Always Open Smart Label & Connected Packaging Touchless Marketing Digital Out-of-Home (DOOH) Shoppable TV Content Management QR Code Reader Engagement Suite The Delivr Engagement Suite The Delivr Difference Dynamic QR Code Generator Custom QR Code Design Mobile Experiences Brand System Campaign & Team Management Intelligent Link Management Advanced Analytics Super Simple Serialization GS1-Compliant Digital Link Privacy-First, Zero-Knowledge Trust Center Register Sign In Image 2: Delivr Our QR codes and short URLs are cookie-free. Our privacy-first tracking anonymizes IP addresses and avoids storing personal or sensitive data, profiling individuals, or fingerprinting devices. Data Policy Home>Documentation>What's with the white border around the QR code? Delivr What's with the white border around the QR code? What's with the white border around the QR code? A "quiet zone" is placed around the QR code image when it's generated to separates the QR code from anything else in its surroundings. The quiet zone is used to buffer the code from its surroundings to prevent any adjacent images or text from being interpreted as part of the code image itself and interfering with the scanning of the code. ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- The size of the buffer zone is mathematically determined when the code is created to ensures an error free code. This buffer zone must be retained. It's simply a white space border and when you generate the code you will see that it's part of the actual code image. The quiet zone is recommended to be a minimum of 4 times the width of a single data square in the code image, however depending on the surroundings of the code (ie; against an unpatterned dark background) you may be able to reduce that to 2 times pixel width. If you reduce the size of the quiet zone, the finished QR code should be tested thoroughly in its intended final setting to make sure that it still scans correctly. Data Policy Home>Documentation>When will GS1 Digital Link replace UPC at point-of-sale? Delivr When will GS1 Digital Link replace UPC at point-of-sale? When will GS1 Digital Link replace UPC at point-of-sale? The industry has established a deadline for adopting 2D barcodes at the point-of-sale, known as Sunrise 2027. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- This initiative aims to implement GS1 Digital Link throughout the entire global supply chain by 2027, with GS1 leading the project and collaborating with various businesses and organizations. GS1 US is working with both supply- and demand-side organizations and solution providers to facilitate the transition towards the "next generation" of barcodes. Data Policy Home>Documentation>Who invented the QR code? Delivr Who invented the QR code? Who invented the QR code? The QR code was invented by DENSO Wave, Incorporated. QR code® is a trademark of DENSO Wave, Incorporated. ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- DENSO CORPORATION DENSO Corporation, headquartered in Kariya, Aichi prefecture, Japan, is a leading global automotive supplier of advanced technology, systems and components in the areas of thermal, powertrain control, electronics, information and safety. Its customers include all the world's major carmakers. Worldwide, the company has more than 200 subsidiaries and affiliates in 38 countries and regions (including Japan) and employs more than 140,000 people. Consolidated global sales for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2015, totaled US$35.9 billion. Last fiscal year, DENSO spent 9.2 percent of its global consolidated sales on research and development. DENSO common stock is traded on the Tokyo and Nagoya stock exchanges. In North America, DENSO employs more than 22,000 people at 30 consolidated companies and affiliates. Of these, 25 are manufacturing facilities located in the United States, Canada and Mexico. In the United States alone, DENSO employs more than 14,000 people in California, Michigan, North Carolina, South Carolina, Tennessee, Kentucky, Georgia, Iowa, Ohio, Pennsylvania, Texas and Arkansas. DENSO’s North American consolidated sales totaled US$8.0 billion for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2015. DENSO ADC DENSO ADC is among the world's largest manufacturers of mobile data capture systems. The company, which pioneered CCD technology and invented the revolutionary QR code®, offers a wide range of advanced-technology, including handheld 1D and 2D terminals and scanners, featuring lightweight, yet durable, ergonomic designs. 233.title: Why GS1 Digital Link?
233.content: Why GS1 Digital Link? URL: https://delivr.com/faq/1552/why-gs1-digital-link Title: Why GS1 Digital Link? URL Source: http://delivr.com/faq/1552/why-gs1-digital-link Markdown Content: Why GS1 Digital Link? Delivr skip to main content +1.631.750.2785 Contact Support Learning Center Learning Center Documentation Uses of QR Codes Videos Our Values Wonderful Things Start Here Curiosity, Where Connection Begins Connecting Worlds, Safely and Securely The Human-First Product Path Delivr’s Approach to Data Privacy Let’s Build a Greener Future Sustainability & QR Codes Accessibility & QR Codes Security & QR Codes Privacy & Retargeting About Delivr About Delivr The Story of Delivr Image 1: Delivr Plans & Pricing Customers Solutions Solutions Overview Super Simple Serialization GS1 Digital Link Logistics Brand Protection Always Open Smart Label & Connected Packaging Touchless Marketing Digital Out-of-Home (DOOH) Shoppable TV Content Management QR Code Reader Engagement Suite The Delivr Engagement Suite The Delivr Difference Dynamic QR Code Generator Custom QR Code Design Mobile Experiences Brand System Campaign & Team Management Intelligent Link Management Advanced Analytics Super Simple Serialization GS1-Compliant Digital Link Privacy-First, Zero-Knowledge Trust Center Register Sign In Image 2: Delivr Our QR codes and short URLs are cookie-free. Our privacy-first tracking anonymizes IP addresses and avoids storing personal or sensitive data, profiling individuals, or fingerprinting devices. Data Policy Home>Documentation>Why GS1 Digital Link? Delivr Why GS1 Digital Link? Why GS1 Digital Link? UPC has offered price lookup functionality for decades. However, this classic 1D barcode no longer meets today's growing demands for greater product information transparency, traceability, and authentication. To address these concerns, product manufacturers have started using multiple data carriers, such as UPC, Datamatrix, and QR codes, on a single item, each serving a specific purpose. Nevertheless, having too many codes on a product can be confusing and take up valuable space. ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- The industry is now transitioning to two-dimensional (2D) barcodes, such as QR codes, that can hold more information to overcome these challenges. The Global Standards Organization (GS1) has also developed a new specification called "GS1 Digital Link," which allows ISO-compliant QR codes to contain a wealth of information about a product, including its global trade identifying number (GTIN), batch/lot details, logistics, and marketing activation information. This improvement notably enhances the data stored in conventional 1D UPC barcodes that only possess a product's GTIN. The industry is taking an essential step towards greater transparency and accountability by enabling consumers to access more detailed information about the products they purchase. 235.title: Wonderful things start here.
235.content: Wonderful things start here. URL: https://delivr.com/page/812/wonderful-things-start-here Title: A Founder's Letter URL Source: http://delivr.com/page/812/wonderful-things-start-here Markdown Content: A Founder's Letter Delivr skip to main content +1.631.750.2785 Contact Support Learning Center Learning Center Documentation Uses of QR Codes Videos Our Values Wonderful Things Start Here Curiosity, Where Connection Begins Connecting Worlds, Safely and Securely The Human-First Product Path Delivr’s Approach to Data Privacy Let’s Build a Greener Future Sustainability & QR Codes Accessibility & QR Codes Security & QR Codes Privacy & Retargeting About Delivr About Delivr The Story of Delivr Image 2: Delivr Plans & Pricing Customers Solutions Solutions Overview Super Simple Serialization GS1 Digital Link Logistics Brand Protection Always Open Smart Label & Connected Packaging Touchless Marketing Digital Out-of-Home (DOOH) Shoppable TV Content Management QR Code Reader Engagement Suite The Delivr Engagement Suite The Delivr Difference Dynamic QR Code Generator Custom QR Code Design Mobile Experiences Brand System Campaign & Team Management Intelligent Link Management Advanced Analytics Super Simple Serialization GS1-Compliant Digital Link Privacy-First, Zero-Knowledge Trust Center Register Sign In Image 3: Delivr Our QR codes and short URLs are cookie-free. Our privacy-first tracking anonymizes IP addresses and avoids storing personal or sensitive data, profiling individuals, or fingerprinting devices. Data Policy SCAN TO CONTINUE ON YOUR PHONE. OUR VALUES Wonderful things start here. Wonderful things start here. Home>Our Values>Wonderful Things Start Here A Founder's Letter This letter reflects the values that guide Delivr. It speaks to how we approach the future, how we design experiences, and what we choose to protect.It is our statement of intent for the One World we are building. --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Where Wonder Begins ------------------- A shift is underway as we witness the convergence of the real and the digital worlds. The world is quietly weaving itself into something new. The physical and the digital, once separate, are now intertwined. So seamlessly that the seams are disappearing. A scan becomes a moment. A product becomes an experience. Technology fades into the background and what’s left is something far more human. That’s where wonder lives. And that's why I want to start with a simple idea, one that will guide Delivr as we move forward. Wonderful things start here. This idea guides how we act and how we choose. It asks us to put human connection first, to design experiences that flow without friction, and to treat privacy with the respect it deserves. It keeps us focused on work that matters crafting interactions that feel intentional, innovative, and true. And in doing so, it gives every person here a shared compass: to build a One World experience that is connected, considered, and human. We No Longer Live in Two Worlds ------------------------------- For years, we talked about “offline” and “online” as if they were destinations. As if people moved between them deliberately. But that’s not how life works. People move fluidly. Instinctively. Without thinking in channels, screens, or touchpoints. They notice something in the real world. They scan. They explore. They share. Sometimes all in the same breath. There is no boundary anymore, just experience. We call this One World. And designing for One World changes everything. People Are Not Funnels ---------------------- Somewhere along the way, the world started mistaking measurement for meaning. We optimized clicks while losing moments. We chased conversion while forgetting connection. At Delivr, we’ve always believed something different. Many measure success in data points. We measure it in people. People matter more than metrics. Privacy and integration are not features we add. They are the foundation we build on. We do not create interactions. We create moments that become stories. Moments that resonate on a human level. Our technology is what we build. Our philosophy is what we believe. People are not data points. They are not targets. They are not funnels. They are stories. And when you treat people with respect, when you give them clarity, privacy, and choice, they don’t disengage. They lean in. Not because they’re tracked… but because they’re invited. That’s the difference between extraction and experience. Extraction is about what you can take. Experience is about what you can create. Building Behind the Curtain --------------------------- Our job isn’t to put technology front and center. Our job is to build it behind the curtain. Quietly. Reliably. Intentionally. So brands can connect with people without surveillance. So moments can unfold without friction. So curiosity can lead without coercion. Privacy first isn’t a feature. It’s a philosophy. Because when technology knows when to step back, something powerful happens: People feel empowered. They feel present. They feel wonder again. This Is the Moment ------------------ The old playbooks are fading. The next era hasn’t been fully written yet. That’s not something to fear. That’s an invitation. This is the moment to rethink what experiences can be. Not louder. Not more invasive. But more human. More intentional. More respectful. More meaningful. Together, we can build One World experiences that don’t interrupt but invite. That doesn’t demand attention but earns it. That doesn’t reduce people to metrics but elevates them to moments. Why We’re Here -------------- At Delivr, we exist to make this future real. We created a powerful digital ecosystem that combines innovative technology with tailored solutions. Our core offerings include seamless digital platforms, advanced analytics, and human-centered interaction experiences designed to deepen connection, invite participation, and bring brands closer to the people they serve. We build the infrastructure that lets wonderful things happen. between people and brands, between physical and digital, between curiosity and connection. Because in a world that’s becoming more connected every day, the opportunity isn’t just to keep up. It’s to lead. With care. With purpose. With imagination. Image 4 Scan to connect with David. And that’s why we believe now more than ever— Wonderful things start here. Now is the time to prepare for what's next. Reach out, and let’s make wonderful things happen. David Harper Founder & CEO, Delivr New York NEXT The Story of Delivr ------------------- How wonderful things started — and keep unfolding. This story begins the way many great ones do — with a kingdom to challenge, dragons to slay, and a quest ahead of its time. Wonderful things start with curiosity — and we’ve always followed it." 236.title: Every scan sparks stories and actionable insights.
236.content: Every scan sparks stories and actionable insights. URL: https://delivr.com/page/794/worldview-advanced-analytics Title: Worldview. Real-time insights into your audience. URL Source: http://delivr.com/page/794/worldview-advanced-analytics Markdown Content: Report Summary -------------- Get the full picture instantly. See your campaign performance at a glance and make smarter, faster decisions. Delivr Worldview℠ Advanced Analytics enables you to focus on what matters most —your audience and your campaigns. Track total scans and clicks to measure engagement across all touchpoints. Cost per Connection . Understand ROI and optimize your spend for maximum impact. Top Countries & Cities . Identify geographic hotspots and tailor campaigns to high-performing regions. Private Connections . Understand the level of anonymized or VPN traffic without compromising user privacy. Top Languages . Discover your audience’s preferred languages to create more relevant experiences. Response Rate . Measure the percentage of impressions that convert to engagement, giving you clear insight into effectiveness. Recent Connections . Stay on top of the latest activity and spot trends as they happen. Campaign Parameters/UTMs . Effortlessly track source campaigns and understand which channels drive results. Quickly assess reach, engagement, and distribution—all in one platform. With Delivr Worldview℠ Advanced Analytics, every scan tells a story, every metric drives insight, and every campaign becomes smarter. Explore a Sample Report. ------------------------ Take a peek at a sample analytics report and uncover actionable insights that drive better campaigns. START EXPLORING Audience Heatmap ---------------- Visualize exactly where and how your audience engages. Geographic Insights . Plots connections on an intuitive heatmap to see engagement hotspots at the city, region, or country level. Detailed Engagement Data . Track timestamps, device types, user actions, and interaction methods to understand how your audience is connecting. Accuracy You Can Trust . Verify location precision and ensure insights reflect real-world engagement patterns. Seamless Analysis . Export all connections for deeper analysis with your preferred third-party tools, enabling data-driven decisions across campaigns Location -------- Understand where your traffic comes from, from countries and states down to cities. This report enables you to identify regional interests, pinpoint areas of engagement concentration, and plan targeted campaigns with confidence. Trends ------ Spot patterns and plan. Gain a deeper understanding of how your campaigns perform over time. Identify your most active days and weeks to see when your audience is most engaged. Track long-term campaign momentum over months or even up to three years, providing you with the insights to plan strategically for future initiatives. Analyze connections by month and year to uncover seasonal trends and optimize your campaigns for peak performance. With these insights, you can make data-driven decisions that drive sustained growth and engagement. Time ---- Know exactly when your audience is most active. Delivr Worldview℠ Advanced Analytics shows you not just how many connections, but when they happen . See your busiest time of day at a glance, whether afternoon peaks, morning commutes, or evening sessions. With detailed charts by hour, daily patterns, and commuting periods, you’ll know the exact windows when your audience is most responsive. Calendar -------- Get a day-by-day view of your campaign performance. The Calendar highlights exactly how many connections happened on each date, making it easy to spot busy days, slow periods, and patterns across weeks or months. Dayparts -------- Not all hours are equal. With Delivr Worldview℠ Advanced Analytics, you’ll see exactly which dayparts drive the most engagement — from DOOH & OOH windows (morning commute, daytime, evening) to Television & Connected TV slots (daytime, prime time, late night). In this campaign, connections peaked during the Daytime period. Engagement Method ----------------- Engagement Method reveals how your audience chooses to connect—whether through QR codes, short URLs, or NFC taps. See not just the numbers, but the story behind each interaction: the share of total engagement, the response rates, and the moments that matter most. With this insight, you can celebrate real connections, understand what resonates, and craft experiences that inspire action. Acquisition ----------- See where your traffic is coming from, including direct links, dark traffic, social media, or chat apps. Understanding the sources driving your campaign helps you measure ROI and refine channel strategies. Operating System ---------------- Know the tech behind every scan. With insights into Operating Systems and Platforms, you’ll see whether audiences lean towards desktop, mobile, or a mix — helping you optimize campaigns for their preferred experience. Language -------- Speak your audience’s language — literally. With Connections by Language, you’ll see which device or browser settings dominate, making it easy to localize messaging and reach people in the language they prefer. Benchmarking ------------ Is your marketing spend working hard enough? With Delivr Worldview℠ Advanced Analytics, you’ll know. Benchmarking reveals your Response Rate by Marketing Channel and Average Cost per Connection, showing just how much more efficient QR-driven insights can be compared to traditional digital channels. Delivr consistently outperforms major ad platforms, providing a more cost-effective way to track real-world engagement.QR codes don’t just track — they unlock previously hidden insights into audience behavior, from location and time of day to device and language, helping you capture engagement that other channels often miss. Serialization ------------- Super Simple Serialization℠, a unique feature of Delivr, enables you to track each product individually, providing complete visibility across the supply chain while ensuring authenticity. Beyond security, it opens the door to personalized experiences for your customers, allowing you to connect with them in meaningful ways and gather actionable insights at the product level. From protecting your brand to optimizing operations, serialization transforms every item into a touchpoint for trust, engagement, and growth. People First ------------ Every connection is a real person. The People First chart highlights the human side of engagement, helping you see beyond raw numbers. Each scan or view represents an individual with a story, a moment of interest, and a decision to interact with your brand. It’s a reminder that behind every click is a person choosing to connect—not an anonymous statistic. By framing metrics in this way, the People First chart shifts the focus from conversions alone to the authentic human connections that drive meaningful relationships. Analytics. Trust. Enterprise-Ready. ----------------------------------- Why Delivr Worldview℠ Advanced Analytics? ----------------------------------------- Most QR code platforms stop at counting scans. Delivr goes further: every interaction becomes more than a number—it’s actionable intelligence. You get depth, breadth, trust, privacy, and flexibility to optimize campaigns, protect your brand, and drive growth. Protect. Track. Understand. --------------------------- With Super Simple Serialization and automatic bot removal, every QR code becomes a verified, trustworthy connection. TrueMrk® Brand Protection, combined with real-time fraud detection, ensures authenticity and gives your customers confidence in every interaction. See the Full Picture. --------------------- From unit-level traceability to omnichannel insights, understand who is engaging, where, and when—across packaging, print, and digital campaigns. Audience heatmaps, GPS precision, and daypart intelligence provide depth to your data, while campaign benchmarking and groupings offer breadth. Integrate Seamlessly. --------------------- Delivr integrates with Google Analytics® , Adobe Analytics® , HubSpot® , Marketo® , Salesforce® Marketing Cloud, Mixpanel® , Amplitude® , and Segment®— enabling you to track the customer journey across all channels. Real-time data, simple exports, and integrations using campaign parameters empower your marketing teams to act quickly and strategically. Privacy-First. Zero-Knowledge. ------------------------------ Delivr is built with privacy at its core and is firmly committed to prioritizing privacy above all else. Using a zero-knowledge approach, we never store personally identifiable information, ensuring full compliance with GDPR and other privacy regulations. This approach protects your customers while still providing you with actionable insights, allowing you to analyze engagement patterns without compromising trust.
